Ayah

سُبْحَـٰنَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَسْرَىٰ بِعَبْدِهِۦ لَيْلًا مِّنَ ٱلْمَسْجِدِ ٱلْحَرَامِ إِلَى ٱلْمَسْجِدِ ٱلْأَقْصَا ٱلَّذِى بَـٰرَكْنَا حَوْلَهُۥ لِنُرِيَهُۥ مِنْ ءَايَـٰتِنَآ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْبَصِيرُ (۱)
Immaculate is He who carried His servant on a journey by night from the Sacred Mosque to the Farthest Mosque whose environs We have blessed, that We might show him some of Our signs. Indeed, He is the All-hearing, the All-seeing. (1)

Key Points

The Mi'raj (Ascension) is the most sacred journey throughout history. Its traveler was the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, its airport was Al-Masjid Al-Haram, its passage was Al-Masjid Al-Aqsa, its Host was Allah, its goal was to witness the Divine Signs, and its gifts were the news of the heavens and the celestial realm (Malakut) and the elevation of human understanding beyond this material world.(10) Based on narrations, one year before the Hijra (migration), after the Maghrib prayer in Al-Masjid Al-Haram, the Prophet traveled to the heavens via Al-Masjid Al-Aqsa by means of the "Buraq"(11) and upon His return, He performed the Fajr prayer in Al-Masjid Al-Haram.(12) The Prophet's Mi'raj was physical and occurred while He was awake, not in a dream or merely spiritual! The principle of the Mi'raj is among the necessities of the religion and is agreed upon by all Islamic sects.(13) Mutawatir (consecutively transmitted) narrations(14) and some supplications and visitation prayers (Ziyaratnamahs) also refer to this matter, and in some Hadiths, the denier of it is introduced as a disbeliever. Allah brought Adam عليه السلام from heaven to earth, but He took the Messenger صلى الله علیه وآله from earth to heaven.(15) That night, the Prophet saw the higher realms, the celestial kingdom (Malakut) of the heavens, and the wonders of creation(16) and met with the Prophets.(17) Qudsi Hadiths (Divine Sayings) were revealed to Him during this journey, and the leadership and Wilayah (guardianship) of Ali عليه السلام were discussed. During this journey, the Messenger saw Paradise and Hell; the condition of the people of Paradise and their blessings, and the people of Hell and their torments, were among the Prophet's other observations. When the Prophet recounted the story of the Mi'raj, some people of weak capacity abandoned the religion.(18)

Objection and Answer: Some raise questions or doubts regarding the Mi'raj and the Prophet's صلى الله علیه وآله celestial journey, such as: there is no air in outer space, there is scorching heat and deadly cold, there is the problem of weightlessness, cosmic rays are dangerous, one night is too short for traversing the heavens, and if someone wants to travel in space, they must have a speed similar to or greater than the speed of light, and to escape gravity, a speed equivalent to 40,000 kilometers per hour is required, and similar issues.

The brief answer to these doubts is: the principle of the Mi'raj is mentioned in the Quran and Mutawatir narrations(19), and what is important is belief in the principle itself, while belief in its details is not necessary.(20) Furthermore, a miracle must not be rationally impossible, and since the Mi'raj is one of the miracles of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, it falls under the general rules governing all miracles. The rest of the problems and doubts are also solvable considering Divine Power. Moreover, today, when humans send airplanes, artificial satellites, and spacecraft to other planets, accepting the Mi'raj is easy. Just as the Quran mentions the instantaneous transfer of the throne of Bilqis from one country to another in the blink of an eye, this matter resolves the problem of traversing distance.

10) It is mentioned in Tafsir Furqan: The night of the birth, the night of the Mi'raj, the night of the Prophetic mission (Bi'that), the night of the Hijra, the night of entry into Medina, and the night of the Prophet's passing were all Monday nights. 11) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 12) Bihar, Vol. 18, p. 389. 13) This verse and verses 8 to 18 of Surah An-Najm indicate this matter. 14) Mutawatir refers to narrations transmitted by so many individuals that there is no room for doubt. 15) Tafsir Kashf al-Asrar. 16) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 17) Kafi, Vol. 8, p. 121. 18) Tafsir Tabari. 19) Bihar, Vol. 18, pp. 282 - 410. 20) Of course, some narrations are fabricated, such as seeing Allah in a physical form, and some are acceptable with justification and interpretation (Ta'wil), such as seeing the people of Paradise and Hell, and some are acceptable without interpretation.

Messages

  1. Mi'raj is not futile; rather, it holds significant secrets. «سبحان الّذى اسرى ...»

  2. The Mi'raj was a private camp and a scientific visit for the Prophet, otherwise, Allah is placeless. «سبحان الّذى اسرى ...»

  3. Servitude ('Ubūdiyyah) is the prelude to flight (ascension), and ascent is impossible without exiting base characteristics. «أسرى بعَبده»

  4. Even if a human ascends (to Mi'raj), he is still a "servant" ('Abd). Therefore, we should not exaggerate (ghuluww) concerning the friends of Allah. «أسرى بعَبده»

  5. Servitude is among the Prophet's honors and the basis for His Divine receptions. «بعَبده»

  6. A Prophet whose future nation will include astronauts must Himself have undertaken the Mi'raj and a celestial journey. «أسرى بعبده»

  7. Night is the best time for proximity to Allah.(21) «أسرى... لیلاً»

  8. The night of Mi'raj is a very important night. «لیلاً» is indefinite (nakirah).

  9. If the capacity and worthiness exist, the flight takes place in a single night. «أسرى بعبده لیلاً»

  10. The Mosque is the best launchpad for the believer's spiritual flight. «من المسجد»

  11. The Mosque must be the axis of our actions. «من المسجدالحرام الى المسجدالاقصى »

  12. Sacred movements must proceed through sacred paths. «أسرى... من المسجد»

  13. Greenery and blessings must revolve around the Mosque. «المسجد... بارَكنا حَوله»

  14. Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem) and its surroundings have historically been the place where Divine blessings descend, the headquarters of Prophets, and the landing site of angels. «باركنا حَوله»

  15. Human scientific capacity is greater than earthly knowledge, and the wonders of the heavens are also greater than those of the earth. «أسرى ... لنُریَه من آیاتنا»

  16. The goal of the Mi'raj was the acquisition of knowledge (Ma'rifah) and spiritual growth. «لنُریَه من آیاتنا»

  17. The Divine Signs are so endless that even the Messenger of Allah cannot attain all of them. «من آیاتنا»(22)

  18. Allah warns the opponents that He sees them and hears their words. «السّمیع البصیر»

21) «سُرى» means nocturnal travel and movement. 22) In another place, it is stated: «لقد رأى من آیات ربّه الكبرى » (An-Najm, 18).

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Isra

Surah "Al-Isra" has 111 verses and is generally considered Makki (Meccan). However, some consider five verses (26, 32, 33, 57, and 78) to be Madani (Medinan). This Surah is named "Subhan" due to its first word, "Isra" because of the event of the Mi'raj, and "Bani Isra'il" (Children of Israel) because it recounts their story. In this Surah, like other Makki Surahs, topics such as Monotheism (Tawhid), the Resurrection (Ma'ad), beneficial advice, the struggle against all forms of polytheism (Shirk), oppression, and deviation, individual and social traditions and laws in history, the history of Adam and Iblis, and the Prophet of Islam and His status in Mecca are discussed. One of the major and noteworthy issues in this Surah is the Mi'raj of the Messenger صلى الله علیه وآله. The Surah begins with the "Tasbih" (glorification) of Allah and ends with His "Hamd" (praise) and commendation. Narrations recommend reciting this Surah on Friday nights, and among its benefits is considered the meeting with Imam Al-Mahdi عليه السلام.(9)

9) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «من قرء سورة بنى اسرائیل فى كل لیلة جمعة لم یمت حتّى یدرك القائم و یكون من اصحابه». Tafsir Namuneh.

Ayah

وَءَاتَيْنَا مُوسَى ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَجَعَلْنَـٰهُ هُدًى لِّبَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ أَلَّا تَتَّخِذُوا۟ مِن دُونِى وَكِيلًا (۲)
We gave Moses the Book, and made it a guide for the Children of Israel—[saying,] ‘Do not take any trustee besides Me’— (2)

Key Points

In the Quran, reliance (Tawakkul) upon Allah and avoidance of reliance upon others is repeatedly commanded, and alongside the command to rely, the reason for it is also stated. For example, in Surah Al-A'raf, He says: Since Allah possesses infinite knowledge, rely upon Him (23). And in Surah Yusuf, He says: Since sovereignty belongs to Him, therefore rely upon Him (24). Or in Surah An-Nisa', He says: Since He is the absolute Owner, therefore take Him as your Trustee (25). The mission of Prophet Moses was universal, although in this verse, Moses عليه السلام is considered a guide for the Children of Israel, yet elsewhere He considers him chosen and a guide for all people: «نوراً و هدىً للنّاس» (26), «اصطفیتُك على النّاس» (27).

23) Al-A'raf, 89. 24) Yusuf, 68. 25) An-Nisa', 131. 26) Al-An'am, 91. 27) Al-A'raf, 144.

Messages

1- In propagation, sometimes the preacher being a compatriot or fellow citizen of the audience has an effect. «هُدىً لبنى اسرائیل»

2- The essence of the prophets' invitation is Monotheism (Tawhid). «آتینا موسى الكتاب ... الاّ تتّخذوا»

3- Man needs a support, and the prophets introduce Allah as his true support. «الاّ تتّخذوا من دونى وكیلاً»

Ayah

ذُرِّيَّةَ مَنْ حَمَلْنَا مَعَ نُوحٍ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ عَبْدًا شَكُورًا (۳)
descendants of those whom We carried [in the ark] with Noah. Indeed, he was a grateful servant. (3)

Key Points

Noah (عليه السلام) has been called the "Second Father" of humanity, because during Noah's Flood, all people drowned except those who were with Noah in the Ark. Therefore, the meaning of "ذُرّیّة مَن حَمَلنا" (the descendants of those We carried) is similar to the phrase "يا بنى آدم" (O Children of Adam). Noah (عليه السلام) lived longer than all other Prophets and was constantly afflicted by the harassment and persecution of the disbelievers and the obstinate, but He was a very grateful servant. (28) Allah has also held Noah (عليه السلام) in special regard and bestowed a specific salutation upon him. (29)

(28) A portion of his prayers and expressions of gratitude is mentioned in the Tafsirs Majma‘ al-Bayan and Atyab al-Bayan. (29) "سَلَامٌ عَلَىٰ نُوحٍ فِي الْعَالَمِينَ" (Surah As-Saffat, 79).

Messages

1- Being in the school of the Prophets and accompanying them is the secret to human salvation and survival. "ذُرّیّة مَن حَمَلنا مع نوحٍ"

2- The history of those who passed away is a factor for the education and warning of future generations. "ذُرّیّة مَن حملنا"

3- Drawing the attention of children to the honor and faith of their ancestors is an emotional way to educate them and encourage their acceptance of the call and responsibility. "ذُرّیّة مَن حمَلنا"

4- Servitude and worship of Allah are the basis for safety from calamities and destruction. "حَملنا مع نوح انّه كان عبداً شكوراً"

5- Honoring noble and exemplary men is among the factors of education. "كان عبداً شكوراً"

6- One of the blessings of being grateful (شكور) is having enduring descendants. "ذُرّیّة مَن... عبداً شكوراً"

Ayah

وَقَضَيْنَآ إِلَىٰ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ فِى ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لَتُفْسِدُنَّ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مَرَّتَيْنِ وَلَتَعْلُنَّ عُلُوًّا كَبِيرًا (٤)
We revealed to the Children of Israel in the Book: ‘Twice you will cause corruption on the earth, and you will perpetrate great tyranny.’ (4)

Key Points

The two instances of corruption by the Children of Israel might have been: one time due to the martyrdom of Isaiah (Sha'ya) and the opposition to Jeremiah (Irmiya), and another time due to the killing of Zechariah (Zakariyya) and John (Yahya). Of course, the possibility of other events has also been suggested, which will be referred to in the subsequent verses. Paradise is reserved for those who avoid all forms of seeking superiority and arrogance (Istikbar), not for those who consider themselves superior and greater than others. «تلك الدّار الآخرة نَجعلها للّذین لا یُریدون علوّاً فى الارض و لافساداً»(30)

30) Al-Qasas, 83.

Messages

1- Heavenly Books have also given news of the future. «قَضَینا»

2- The history of the Jewish people is also recorded in the Torah. «فى الكتاب»

3- Corruption (Fasad) includes all types of corruption: physical, cultural, economic, military, and political. «لتفسدنّ» is used absolutely (unrestricted).

4- Seeking superiority and corruption on earth are characteristics of those who seek the world (Dunya-talaban). «لتفسدنّ فى الارض و لتعلنّ»

5- Corruption is more dangerous when it has roots in arrogance (Istikbari) and is repeated. «لتفسدنّ... مَرّتین و لتعلنّ»

6- Sometimes the oppressed (Mustad'afin) also become arrogant (Mustakbir). «ولتَعلُنّ عُلوّا كبیرا»

Ayah

فَإِذَا جَآءَ وَعْدُ أُولَىٰهُمَا بَعَثْنَا عَلَيْكُمْ عِبَادًا لَّنَآ أُو۟لِى بَأْسٍ شَدِيدٍ فَجَاسُوا۟ خِلَـٰلَ ٱلدِّيَارِ ۚ وَكَانَ وَعْدًا مَّفْعُولًا (٥)
So when the first occasion of the two [prophecies] came, We aroused against you Our servants possessing great might, and they ransacked [your] habitations, and the promise was bound to be fulfilled. (5)

Key Points

The Quran has not specified an instance (or example) for the group that suppresses the corruptors of the Children of Israel. However, in the commentaries (Tafasir), it has been applied to Nebuchadnezzar, Talut, the King of Rome, Hitler, and others. But none of them is compatible with the sacred word «بَعثنا» (We sent forth), which is used for the Resurrection (Qiyamah) and the sending of the Prophets (Bi'that al-Anbiya), nor with «عباداً لنا» (servants of Ours), which is used for individuals like the Prophets. Therefore, perhaps the Ayah refers to a future that has not yet arrived.

Messages

1- The destruction of arrogant transgressors is among the Divine traditions (Sunan). «بَعثنا علیكم» (We sent forth against you).

2- The fulfillment of Divine promises does not mean that man is exempt from the duty of Jihad (struggle). «بعثنا علیكم عباداً» (We sent forth against you servants).

3- Jihad and the struggle against the arrogant are not exclusive to Islam. «بعثنا... اولى بأس شدید» (We sent forth... possessors of great might).

4- There is always a hand above every hand (a greater power). The Children of Israel transgressed, so Allah made those superior to them dominate them. «بعثنا... اولى بأس شدید» (We sent forth... possessors of great might).

5- Preparing power to suppress seditionists and stripping them of security, even through house-to-house searches, is a good deed. «فجاسوا خلال الدّیار» (So they searched through the homes).

6- Heavenly prophecies will certainly come to pass. «وعداً مفعولاً» (A promise fulfilled).

Ayah

ثُمَّ رَدَدْنَا لَكُمُ ٱلْكَرَّةَ عَلَيْهِمْ وَأَمْدَدْنَـٰكُم بِأَمْوَٰلٍ وَبَنِينَ وَجَعَلْنَـٰكُمْ أَكْثَرَ نَفِيرًا (٦)
Then We gave you back the turn [to prevail] over them, and We aided you with children and wealth, and made you greater in number, (6)

Key Points

The apparent meaning of the Ayah is that after the corruption and defeat of the Children of Israel, Allah would again restore them with wealth and children. However, it is possible that the addressee of "رَدَدنا لكم الكرّة" (We gave you back the return) is the same as "عباداً لنا اولى بأس" (servants of Ours possessing great might) from the previous Ayah. Meaning, We grant two favors to Our valiant servants: one is the crushing defeat of the aggressors among the Children of Israel, and the other is granting them power and governance.

Messages

  1. Granting respite and re-testing through wealth and children is among the Divine traditions. «رَددنا لكم»
  2. Wealth and human resources are means of power and governance. «اَمددناكم باموالٍ وبنین»
  3. Allah's favor encompasses both the sinful individual who repents and the sinful nation that has been chastised. «رَددنا لكم الكرّة»
  4. Population growth (or multitude of followers) is among the Divine blessings. «جعلناكم اكثر نفیراً»

Ayah

إِنْ أَحْسَنتُمْ أَحْسَنتُمْ لِأَنفُسِكُمْ ۖ وَإِنْ أَسَأْتُمْ فَلَهَا ۚ فَإِذَا جَآءَ وَعْدُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ لِيَسُـۥٓـُٔوا۟ وُجُوهَكُمْ وَلِيَدْخُلُوا۟ ٱلْمَسْجِدَ كَمَا دَخَلُوهُ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ وَلِيُتَبِّرُوا۟ مَا عَلَوْا۟ تَتْبِيرًا (۷)
[saying,] ‘If you do good, you will do good to your [own] souls, and if you do evil, it will be [evil] for them.’ So when the occasion for the other [prophecy] comes, they will make your faces wretched, and enter the Temple just as they entered it the first time, and destroy utterly whatever they come upon. (7)

Key Points

What is important in historical events and leads to constructive development are the lessons and warnings they contain, not the details of the incidents. The story of the destruction of the arrogant Jews at the hands of Allah's believing servants, which was mentioned in the last two verses, is meant to convey that arrogance and disbelief do not go unanswered. Since this general and educational goal was intended, the Ayah did not delve into the details of the conflict, the number of people, or the weapons, similar to the story of Prophet Yusuf عليه السلام, where, because the goal is educational and teaches chastity, many details of the story, even the name of Zulaikha, were not mentioned. Although some interpretations attribute the arrogance and corruption of the Jews and their subsequent eradication in two phases to individuals in the distant or near past, and Al-Aqsa Mosque has witnessed bitter events and memories many times over thousands of years, perhaps, considering the verses and narrations, we should await the future aggression and arrogance of Israel and their uprooting by Allah's righteous servants. As it appears from the surface meaning of Ayahs 5 and 6, such widespread corruption on earth by the Children of Israel and such a significant eradication by the men of God have not yet occurred. (31) Numerous narrations cited in the commentaries regarding this Ayah state that some believe the destruction of the aggressive Jews will occur before the uprising of Imam Mahdi عليه السلام, carried out by faithful revolutionaries who place their swords on their shoulders, advance to the point of martyrdom, and pave the way for the global government of Imam Mahdi عليه السلام. Others believe its occurrence will be during the advent of the Imam of the Age, when the aggressive Jews will be eradicated. (32)

31) Today, on the one hand, we witness the widespread aggression of Israel, their plan to occupy the land from the Nile to the Euphrates, the expulsion of Muslims from their homeland, the establishment of espionage networks globally, the control of propaganda outlets, the gathering of their forces from East and West, and various corrupting activities. On the other hand, we witness the Islamic Revolution and the emergence of Hezbollah youth in Palestine, attacking Israel and reclaiming Al-Aqsa Mosque, which perhaps the Ayah refers to. And Allah knows best. 32) Tafsirs Nur al-Thaqalayn, Furqan, Namuneh, etc.

Messages

1- Our good deeds or evil deeds do not benefit or harm Allah; rather, their result returns to ourselves. «اِن اَحسنتم اَحسنتم لانفسكم و اِن أسأتم فلها»

2- Good deeds should be mentioned first, then evil deeds. «اِن احسنتم... اِن اسأتم»

3- Man is free in his choice and the manner of his action. «اِن احسنتم... اِن اسأتم»

4- The ups and downs of history and the destruction of the Jews following their arrogance are based on divine law and tradition (Sunnah). «اِن احسنتم احسنتم...»

5- Mosques have always been the base of religious power and a symbol of strength, and freeing mosques from the domination of disbelievers is the duty of the believers. To suppress the aggressive Jews, the men of God enter Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem), just as Muslims entered Al-Masjid al-Haram upon the conquest of Mecca. «لیدخلوا المسجد»

6- To strengthen the truth, the eradication of the arrogant is necessary. «و لیتبّروا ما علوا تتبیرا»

Ayah

عَسَىٰ رَبُّكُمْ أَن يَرْحَمَكُمْ ۚ وَإِنْ عُدتُّمْ عُدْنَا ۘ وَجَعَلْنَا جَهَنَّمَ لِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ حَصِيرًا (۸)
Maybe your Lord will have mercy on you, but if you revert, We [too] will revert, and We have made hell a prison for the faithless. (8)

Key Points

The word haṣīr (حَصیر) derives from ḥaṣr (حَصر), meaning to be confined or besieged (put in a tight spot/under siege). A mat (ḥaṣīr) is also called haṣīr because it is woven tightly together.

Messages

  1. Despair and hopelessness regarding Divine Mercy are impermissible. Allah has left the door open for the return of even the corrupt. «عسى ربّكم أن یَرحمكم»
  2. The focus of the educator's work must be mercy and affection. «ربّكم, یَرحمكم»
  3. In the method of guidance, we should first speak of mercy and kindness, and then use warning and threat. «یَرحمكم... عُدنا»
  4. Divine Wrath depends on our own actions. «اِن عُدتم عدنا»
  5. Corruption that is not followed by repentance leads to disbelief (kufr). «و جعلنا جهنّم للكافرین»
  6. The inhabitants of Hell have no way of escape. «حَصیراً»

Ayahs

إِنَّ هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانَ يَهْدِى لِلَّتِى هِىَ أَقْوَمُ وَيُبَشِّرُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ٱلَّذِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ أَنَّ لَهُمْ أَجْرًا كَبِيرًا (۹)
Indeed this Quran guides to what is most upright, and gives the good news to the faithful who do righteous deeds that there is a great reward for them. (9)
وَأَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ أَعْتَدْنَا لَهُمْ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا (۱٠)
As for those who do not believe in the Hereafter, We have prepared a painful punishment for them. (10)

Key Points

The phrase «یَهدى للّتى هى اقوم» can be interpreted in two ways: A: The Quran guides to the most enduring method. B: The Quran guides the most steadfast nations and communities.

Messages

  1. Superstitions and illusions have no place in the steadfast logic of the Quran. «یَهدى للّتى هى اقوم»

  2. The Quran is the only book that possesses eternal and fixed laws. «یَهدى للّتى هى اقوم»

  3. Receiving reward requires faith and action, «المؤمنین الّذین یعملون الصالحات», but for deserving Hellfire, disbelief alone is sufficient. «الّذین لایؤمنون... لهم عذاباً»

  4. Divine punishment is prepared from now. «اَعتَدنا»

  5. Fear and hope must exist side by side. «أجراً كبیراً... عذاباً الیماً»

Ayah

وَيَدْعُ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ بِٱلشَّرِّ دُعَآءَهُۥ بِٱلْخَيْرِ ۖ وَكَانَ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ عَجُولًا (۱۱)
Man prays for ill as [avidly as] he prays for good, and man is overhasty. (11)

Key Points

In the Quran, the hastiness of man and his seeking of evil have been mentioned repeatedly: «و یَستعجلونك بالسیّئة قبل الحسنه»(33) meaning they demand immediate punishment from you. In another Ayah, He says, quoting the disbelievers: «أمطر علینا حجارةً من السماء»(34) They say: O God, if this religion is true, then rain stones upon us from the sky. The source of these hastinesses is ignorance, obstinacy, and fanaticism. The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: «انّما أهلك النّاس العجلة»(35) The greatest destruction and ruin of man comes from haste. Many of the curses that man invokes against himself or his children also stem from this haste.

33) Ra‘d, 6. 34) Anfal, 32. 35) Bihar, Vol. 17, p. 340.

Messages

1- Haste and impatience are among the afflictions of human thought and action. «و یَدع الانسان بالشرّ... عجولاً»

2- Man is inherently inclined toward good. «یَدعُ الانسان بالشرّ دعائه بالخیر» The comparison of man's seeking of evil to his seeking of good indicates that man is fundamentally inclined toward good.

3- Those who do not find the path to the firm guidance of the Quran «یَهدى للّتى هى اقوم» will hastily turn toward evil. «یَدع الانسان بالشرّ»

4- Haste exists in the nature of every human being, but we must recognize the proper place for its correct use. «كان الانسان عجولاً»

Ayah

وَجَعَلْنَا ٱلَّيْلَ وَٱلنَّهَارَ ءَايَتَيْنِ ۖ فَمَحَوْنَآ ءَايَةَ ٱلَّيْلِ وَجَعَلْنَآ ءَايَةَ ٱلنَّهَارِ مُبْصِرَةً لِّتَبْتَغُوا۟ فَضْلًا مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ وَلِتَعْلَمُوا۟ عَدَدَ ٱلسِّنِينَ وَٱلْحِسَابَ ۚ وَكُلَّ شَىْءٍ فَصَّلْنَـٰهُ تَفْصِيلًا (۱۲)
We made the night and the day two signs. Then We faded out the sign of the night, and made the sign of the day lightsome, so that you may seek from your Lord’s bounty and that you may know the number of years and calculation [of time], and We have elaborated everything in detail. (12)

Key Points

Perhaps the reason why the word 'night' usually precedes 'day' in the Quran is that the darkness of the earth originates from itself, while its illumination comes from the sun.

Messages

  1. The rotation of the earth around itself, the creation of day and night, and their changes, are according to the wise Divine Will, not by chance. «جعلنا... مَحَونا... لتبتغوا»

  2. Day is for work and night is for rest. «آیة النّهار مبصرةً لتبتغوا»

  3. Although grace and mercy are from Allah, we must also strive (make effort). «لِتَبتغوا فضلاً من ربّكم»

  4. Preparing the grounds for earning sustenance is among the functions of Divine Lordship. «لتبتَغوا فضلاً من ربّكم»

  5. Sustenance is from Allah; let us not be arrogant about our own cleverness. «فضلاً من ربّكم»

  6. The administration of the system of creation is not without purpose, and in this administration, the human being is the axis of the goals. «لتبتغوا ... لتعلموا»

  7. Night and day constitute a natural, permanent, universal, easy, and understandable calendar, and a means for order and planning. «لتعلموا عدد السّنین و الحساب»

  8. In the system of existence, everything is ordered, lawful, and distinctly separated so that friction, interference, or collapse does not occur. «وكلّ شى ء فصّلناه تفصیلاً»

Ayah

وَكُلَّ إِنسَـٰنٍ أَلْزَمْنَـٰهُ طَـٰٓئِرَهُۥ فِى عُنُقِهِۦ ۖ وَنُخْرِجُ لَهُۥ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ كِتَـٰبًا يَلْقَىٰهُ مَنشُورًا (۱۳)
We have attached every person’s omen to his neck, and We shall bring it out for him on the Day of Resurrection as a book that he will find wide open. (13)

Key Points

The word ṭā’ir (طائر) in the Ayah is a metaphor for human deeds, because human deeds fly like a bird until they reach their owner, and the record of everyone's deeds, good and evil, is hung around his neck. The issue of the record of deeds has been mentioned repeatedly in the Qur’an, and several points have been raised in various verses, including:

1- The record of deeds is for everyone. «كلّ انسان اَلزمناه طائره...» 2- Nothing has been omitted in that record. «لا یُغادر صغیرةً ولا كبیرة»(36) 3- The criminals fear it. «فتَرَى المجرمین مشفقین ممّافیه»(37) 4- Man himself, by reading his record of deeds, is the judge and arbiter. «اِقرَء كتابك كفى بنفسك الیوم علیك حسیباً»(38) 5- The successful ones will be given their book in their right hand, and the inhabitants of the Fire in their left hand. «اوتِىَ كتابه بیمینه... بشماله»(39)

36) Kahf, 49. 37) Kahf, 49. 38) Isra, 14. 39) Al-Haqqah, 19.

Messages

1- Accountability and reckoning are for everyone and admit no exception. «كلّ انسان»

2- All human beings will appear in the gathering place (of Resurrection) with the identification card of their deeds. «كلّ انسان اَلزمناه طائره فى عُنقه»

3- Man's deed is inseparable from him. «اَلزمناه... فى عُنُقِه»

4- Man's happiness and wretchedness depend on his own deeds, not on accidental factors like luck or fortune. «اَلزَمناه طائره...»

5- All deeds are clear to Allah; hanging the record of deeds around the neck is for man himself to understand. «نُخرج له»

6- All of man's deeds and movements are recorded. Although the recording of deeds is not perceptible to man in this world, on the Day of Resurrection, his record of deeds will be opened and revealed. «كتاباً یَلقاه منشورا»

Ayah

ٱقْرَأْ كِتَـٰبَكَ كَفَىٰ بِنَفْسِكَ ٱلْيَوْمَ عَلَيْكَ حَسِيبًا (۱٤)
‘Read your book! Today your soul suffices as your own reckoner.’ (14)

Key Points

Imam Sadiq علیه السلام says: Man sees his deeds in his record so clearly that it is as if He committed them that very hour. (40) Fakhr Razi says: The meaning of the Book (kitāb) is the page of the soul upon which man's deeds leave their mark, and the meaning of reading is the comprehension and understanding of it. (41) In Tafsir Al-Mizan, the Book is interpreted as the deeds themselves. In many narrations, it is recommended that man settle his accounts before the Day of Judgment. «حاسبوا انفسكم قبل أن تُحاسبوا» (42) And such self-accountability is the basis for man's awakening, while the absence of it is a sign of his heedlessness. It is stated in a hadith: Whoever holds himself accountable in this world, his reckoning in the Hereafter will be easy. Therefore, we must read the book of our life and the record of our deeds in this very world so that we may compensate for our weaknesses, repent for our misdeeds, and increase our good actions. (43)

40) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 41) Tafsir Kabir. 42) Bihar, Vol. 70, p. 73. 43) A father asked his son to write down all his actions for one day, and the son did so. The next day, the father repeated the same request. The son became distressed, saying that this task was difficult and asked to be excused. The father said: My dear son! All your deeds are recorded in this manner, and they will certainly be revealed on the Day of Judgment, and you will be ashamed of your actions. (Tafsir Kashf al-Asrar)

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, all people will be able to read their record of deeds. «اِقرَء»

2- On the Day of Judgment, consciences will awaken. «نفسك»

3- The Day of Judgment is the only court where the criminal testifies against himself. «كفى بنفسك الیوم علیك حسیبا»

Ayah

مَّنِ ٱهْتَدَىٰ فَإِنَّمَا يَهْتَدِى لِنَفْسِهِۦ ۖ وَمَن ضَلَّ فَإِنَّمَا يَضِلُّ عَلَيْهَا ۚ وَلَا تَزِرُ وَازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرَىٰ ۗ وَمَا كُنَّا مُعَذِّبِينَ حَتَّىٰ نَبْعَثَ رَسُولًا (۱٥)
Whoever is guided is guided only for [the good of] his own soul, and whoever goes astray, goes astray only to its detriment. No bearer shall bear another’s burden. We do not punish [any community] until We have sent [it] an apostle. (15)

Key Points

Considering Ayahs 25 of Al-An'am and 13 of Al-'Ankabut and the narrations, the leaders of disbelief and misguidance carry the burden of the sins of their followers in addition to the burden of their own sins, without anything being reduced from the sins of the followers. This is because they provided the means for their misguidance.

Messages

1- Man is free in choosing his path. «مَن اهتدى ... مَن ضَلّ»

2- Accepting guidance is for the benefit of man himself, and disbelief and turning away are to his own detriment; no benefit or harm reaches Allah. «یهتدى لنفسه... یضلّ علیها»

3- Everyone is accountable for his own deeds. «لاتزر وازرة وِزرَ اُخرى»

4- One must first speak of the positives, then the negatives. «مَن اِهتدى» comes before «مَن ضلّ».

5- It is the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah that He does not punish any person or nation without clarification and the completion of the argument (Itmam al-Hujjah). «ما كنّا معذّبین حتّى نَبعثَ رسولاً». And the prophets have been present for the guidance of humanity since the beginning of creation.

6- Reason (Aql) alone is not sufficient for human felicity; the guidance of the prophets is necessary. «حتّى نبعث رسولاً»

7- The prerequisite for the Hereafter is the coming of the prophets, because for man to be held accountable, he must first receive the description of his duties through the prophets. «وما كنّا معذّبین ...»

Ayah

وَإِذَآ أَرَدْنَآ أَن نُّهْلِكَ قَرْيَةً أَمَرْنَا مُتْرَفِيهَا فَفَسَقُوا۟ فِيهَا فَحَقَّ عَلَيْهَا ٱلْقَوْلُ فَدَمَّرْنَـٰهَا تَدْمِيرًا (۱٦)
And when We desire to destroy a town We command its affluent ones [to obey Allah]. But they commit transgression in it, and so the word becomes due against it, and We destroy it utterly. (16)

Key Points

The phrase «اَمَرنا مُترفیها» (We commanded its affluent ones) means that We command the leaders of the people to obey, because Allah does not command immorality (fisq) and sin, but rather commands justice ('adl) and benevolence (ihsan). Allah's method of destruction is such that He commands obedience, the heedless affluent ones disobey, and the common people follow them. Then, the Divine wrath becomes certain and realized for everyone, and the Divine punishment and anger arrive. The will of the Wise God for wrath is based on the grounds of our sins and negative characteristics; otherwise, the Merciful Allah does not arbitrarily will the destruction of a people. According to the statement of Hadrat Reza عليه السلام: The Divine will in this Ayah is conditional upon our immorality (fisq).(44)

44) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā'iq.

Messages

1- One of the methods of Divine wrath and punishment is excessive affluence and sinking into blessings. «اردنا أن نُهلك قریة...»

2- The presence of the affluent (mutrafīn) in society prepares the ground for Divine wrath. «ففسقوا فیها فحقّ ...»

3- The moral and social decline of a society is caused by its affluent leaders. «أمرنا مُترفیها ففسقوا»

4- The heedless affluent ones stand against the call of the prophets before others, because affluence is usually the source of corruption. «أمَرنا مترفیها ففسقوا»

5- Granting respite to the disobedient is a Divine tradition (Sunnah). «أمرنا، ففسقوا، فحقّ»

6- We must take Divine warnings seriously and reform ourselves, because the factor executing the Divine warnings is our own performance. «ففسقوا فیها فحقّ علیها القول»

7- Divine punishment is not sent down without the completion of the argument (proof). «أمَرنا، ففسقوا، فحقّ، فدمّرنا»

8- Divine wrath and punishment are severe. «فدمّرناها تدمیراً»

9- Everything and every action is accomplished by Divine power. «اَردنا، نُهلك، أمَرنا، دَمّرنا»

Ayah

وَكَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا مِنَ ٱلْقُرُونِ مِنۢ بَعْدِ نُوحٍ ۗ وَكَفَىٰ بِرَبِّكَ بِذُنُوبِ عِبَادِهِۦ خَبِيرًۢا بَصِيرًا (۱۷)
How many generations We have destroyed since Noah! Your Lord is sufficient as [a witness who is] well aware and percipient of His servants’ sins. (17)

Key Points

This Ayah, which recounts the extinction of one generation of humanity and the beginning of a new system in human life, shows that the time of Prophet Nuh عليه السلام was a turning point in human history. Life before Nuh was very simple, with a limited population, and did not have a history full of major events. The continuation of the human race occurred through the few believers who were with Nuh عليه السلام in the Ark. While the Ayah serves as a warning to the heedless and a lesson for the people, it is also a source of comfort and tranquility for the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, [indicating] that if the disbelievers do not cease their persecution, they will be afflicted by Divine wrath. After the people of Nuh, the people of 'Ad, Thamud, Lut, the dwellers of Madyan, Pharaoh, Haman, Qarun, and the People of the Elephant (Aṣḥāb al-Fīl) were also afflicted by Divine wrath. «من القرون من بعد نوح»

Messages

1- The nations throughout history that have been afflicted by Allah's wrath and anger due to disobedience are numerous. «كم اهلكنا»

2- Divine wrath is not exclusive to the Day of Judgment; He also destroys in this world. «كم اهلكنا»

3- History is a source of lessons and admonition. «من القرون»

4- Attention to Allah's knowledge and awareness of sins is sufficient for our moral development. «كفى بربّك»

5- For the punishment and retribution of criminals, Allah's awareness is sufficient, and He does not need witnesses or evidence. «كفى بربّك...»

6- The sinful human is a servant of Allah. «بذنوب عباده» (He did not say: «بذنوب الناس»)

7- Allah is Khabīr (All-Aware) of our inner state and Baṣīr (All-Seeing) of our outward state. «خبيراً بَصِيراً»

Ayah

مَّن كَانَ يُرِيدُ ٱلْعَاجِلَةَ عَجَّلْنَا لَهُۥ فِيهَا مَا نَشَآءُ لِمَن نُّرِيدُ ثُمَّ جَعَلْنَا لَهُۥ جَهَنَّمَ يَصْلَىٰهَا مَذْمُومًا مَّدْحُورًا (۱۸)
Whoever desires this transitory life, We expedite for him therein whatever We wish, for whomever We desire. Then We appoint hell for him, to enter it, blameful and spurned. (18)

Key Points

From the perspective of the Quran, worldliness (dunyā-talabī) and materialism (dunyā-garā'ī) are condemned if they cause neglect of the Hereafter and restrict desires solely to this world. This point has been raised in numerous verses, including: «یَعلمون ظاهِراً مِن الحَیوة الدّنیا و هم عَن الآخرة هم غافِلون»(45), «فأعرض عن مَن تَولّى عن ذكرِنا و لم یُرِد الاّ الحیوةَ الدّنیا»(46) and «أرَضیتُم بالحَیوة الدّنیا»(47). In the world of material things, there is much conflict and struggle, and not everyone reaches all their aspirations. Therefore, there must be a measure and a share determining who possesses how much: «عجّلنا له فیها ما نشاء لمن نرید». But in the world of spiritual matters, since there is no conflict or limitation, everyone is 100% successful, and the path is open for even more. «كان سعیهم مشكورا»(48)

45) Rūm, 7. 46) Najm, 29. 47) Tawbah, 38. 48) Isrā’, 19.

Messages

1- The world is fleeting. «العاجلة»

2- Man is created with will and is free in choice, but the Divine Will is the fundamental principle. «یرید... لمن نُرید»

3- What is ugly is the characteristic of worldliness, not the world itself. «كان یُرید العاجله»

4- Human desire is boundless, so it must be limited within the framework of Divine Will. «ما نشاء»

5- Not all who desire the world attain it. «لمن نُرید»

6- World-seekers are of two types: some lose both the world and the Hereafter, «خسر الدنیا و الآخرة»; others attain a portion of their worldly desires. «عجّلنا له فیها ما نشاء»

7- World-seekers and those with material goals may attain some fame and sustenance, but their Hereafter is ruined. «جعلنا له جهنّم»

8- The end of worldliness is regret and Hellfire. «جعلنا له جهنّم»

9- World-seekers will both burn in the Fire (physical torment) and be humiliated (spiritual torment). «یَصلیها مَذموماً مَدحوراً»

Ayah

وَمَنْ أَرَادَ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةَ وَسَعَىٰ لَهَا سَعْيَهَا وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ كَانَ سَعْيُهُم مَّشْكُورًا (۱۹)
Whoever desires the Hereafter and strives for it with an endeavour worthy of it, should he be faithful,—the endeavour of such will be well-appreciated. (19)

Messages

1- Man is free and possesses will. «مَن أراد»

2- The Hereafter is not attained without effort. «سَعى لها»

3- A specific effort is necessary for eternal happiness (in the Hereafter). «سَعى لها سَعیَها»

4- Faith is a condition for benefiting from worldly efforts. «و هو مؤمن»

5- The condition for eternal happiness is faith and effort. «كان سعیهم مشكوراً»

6- The effort of the believers is appreciated and thanked, whether it reaches its result or not. «كان سعیهم مشكورا»

7- The attainment of the world by the worldly-minded is probable, «عجّلنا... لِمَن نرید», but the attainment of reward by the Hereafter-oriented believers is certain. «كان سعیهم مشكورا»

8- Man's will and effort play a role in his happiness. «أراد... سعى... كان سعیهم مشكورا»

9- Thanking others for their efforts is a Divine act. «كان سعیهم مشكورا»

10- Allah's gratitude towards the believers is greater than the gardens of Paradise, because the thanks of every person is commensurate with the scope of his existence. «كان سعیهم مشكورا»

11- Not every effort and striving is appreciated and praised by Allah. «مَن اراد الآخرة و سَعى لها... كان سعیهم مشكورا»

Ayah

كُلًّا نُّمِدُّ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ وَهَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ مِنْ عَطَآءِ رَبِّكَ ۚ وَمَا كَانَ عَطَآءُ رَبِّكَ مَحْظُورًا (۲٠)
To these and to those—to all We extend the bounty of your Lord, and the bounty of your Lord is not confined. (20)

Key Points

Allah created humans free and tested them by placing resources at their disposal, so that their manner of action and selection (choice) becomes clear, and those who transact with God are separated from others. (This is) like the water and electricity organization which places these two blessings at the disposal of all houses, so that individuals, by their own choice, may make good or bad use of them.

Messages

1- It is Allah's tradition (Sunnah) to place His bounty and aid at the disposal of everyone, so that each person may manifest his good and bad qualities. «كلاً نُمِدّ»

2- Belief in Allah and seeking the Hereafter does not contradict benefiting from material resources. «كلاً نُمدّ»

3- Divine favors are among the affairs of His Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) and His grace (Tafaḍḍul); otherwise, we have no claim upon Him. «عطاء ربّك»

4- The gift of Allah is perpetual. «ما كان عطاء ربّك محظوراً»

Ayah

ٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ فَضَّلْنَا بَعْضَهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ ۚ وَلَلْـَٔاخِرَةُ أَكْبَرُ دَرَجَـٰتٍ وَأَكْبَرُ تَفْضِيلًا (۲۱)
Observe how We have given some of them an advantage over some others; yet the Hereafter is surely greater in respect of ranks and greater in respect of relative merit. (21)

Key Points

The superiority of individuals over one another is of two types: Sometimes, due to talent, inclination, intellect, family circumstances, geographical conditions, and the like, a person receives endowments. These are the grace of Allah and are accompanied by responsibility and trial, and this Ayah refers to these very advantages. And sometimes, advantages are based on oppression, exploitation, and manipulation, which have a separate reckoning. The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: The degrees of Paradise are commensurate with the intellect of its inhabitants.(49)

(49) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Paying attention to the wisdom behind the differences and variations in creation requires contemplation and precision. «اُنظر كیف فضّلنا»

2- The status of everyone in the Hereafter is not equal, and there are very great degrees of superiority. «اكبر درجات»

Ayah

لَّا تَجْعَلْ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَـٰهًا ءَاخَرَ فَتَقْعُدَ مَذْمُومًا مَّخْذُولًا (۲۲)
Do not set up another god besides Allah, or you will sit blameworthy, forsaken. (22)

Key Points

| Although the addressee of the Ayah is the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, the intent is all people. Examples of this type of address are abundant in the Holy Quran, including the following Ayah which states: «و اِمّا یبلغنّ عندك الكبر احدهما او كلاهما فلا تقل لهما اُفٍّ» (meaning: If one of them or both of them reach old age in your presence, do not say 'Uff' to them), even though the Prophet had lost his parents in childhood. In this Ayah (under discussion) as well, the intent of «لا تجعل» is the people, but due to the Prophet's leadership, he has been addressed.

Messages

1- Human flourishing and honor are achieved under the shadow of Monotheism (Tawhid), while polytheism (Shirk) and the worship of other than Allah make man humiliated and disgraced. «مَلوماً مَخذولا»

2- Following non-Divine schools of thought leads man to a dead end, stagnation, and a swamp. «فتَقعُد ملوماً مخذولا»

3- The polytheist is blamed by Allah, the angels, the believers, awakened consciences, and the wise people of the world. «مَلوماً مَخذولا» This is because the name of the condemner is not mentioned, making the condemnation absolute and comprehensive.

Ayah

۞ وَقَضَىٰ رَبُّكَ أَلَّا تَعْبُدُوٓا۟ إِلَّآ إِيَّاهُ وَبِٱلْوَٰلِدَيْنِ إِحْسَـٰنًا ۚ إِمَّا يَبْلُغَنَّ عِندَكَ ٱلْكِبَرَ أَحَدُهُمَآ أَوْ كِلَاهُمَا فَلَا تَقُل لَّهُمَآ أُفٍّ وَلَا تَنْهَرْهُمَا وَقُل لَّهُمَا قَوْلًا كَرِيمًا (۲۳)
Your Lord has decreed that you shall not worship anyone except Him, and [He has enjoined] kindness to parents. Should any of them or both reach old age at your side, do not say to them, ‘Fie!’ And do not chide them, but speak to them noble words. (23)

Key Points

In the narrations (Ahadith), great emphasis has been placed on kindness (Ihsan) to parents, and harming them has been strongly condemned. The reward for a merciful glance at one's parents is an accepted Hajj. Their satisfaction is Allah's satisfaction, and their anger is Allah's anger. Kindness to parents prolongs life and causes one's own children to be kind to him/her. It is stated in the narrations: Even if they strike you, do not say "Uff" (a word of annoyance), do not stare at them, do not raise your hand, do not walk ahead of them, do not call them by name, do not do anything that causes people to insult them, do not sit before they do, and help them before they ask you for anything.(50) A man was performing the Tawaf (circumambulation) while carrying his mother on his shoulders. He saw the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله in that state and asked: "Have I fulfilled the right of my mother?" He replied: "You have not even fulfilled the right of one of her groans during childbirth."(51) The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was asked: "Is there any kindness (Ihsan) remaining for parents after their death?" He replied: "Yes, through praying for them, seeking forgiveness for them, fulfilling their commitments, paying their debts, and respecting their friends."(52) A man complained about his father to the Prophet. The حضرت (His Holiness) summoned the father and questioned him. The elderly father said: "There was a day when I was strong and wealthy and I helped my son, but today he is wealthy and does not help me." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله wept and said: "There is no stone or pebble that hears this story and does not weep!" Then He said to the son: "اَنتَ و مالُك لاَبیك" (You and your wealth belong to your father).(53) It is stated in a narration: Even if the parents strike their child, the child should say: "May Allah forgive you," for this phrase is the very "قول كریم" (noble word).(54) Kindness to parents is among the characteristics of the Prophets. As it is mentioned regarding حضرت Isa (Jesus) عليه السلام, the description "بِرّاً بوالدتى" (dutiful to my mother)(55) and regarding حضرت Yahya (John) عليه السلام, "بِرّاً بوالدیه" (dutiful to his parents)(56) has been stated. Parents are not only the biological father and mother. In some narrations, the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله and Amir al-Mu'minin عليه السلام are considered the fathers of the Ummah (nation).(57) Just as حضرت Ibrahim (Abraham) is considered the father of the Arabs.(58) "ملّة ابیكم ابراهیم" (The religion of your father Abraham).(59) If parents realize that they are mentioned immediately after Tawhid (Monotheism), the motivation to invite their children to Tawhid will be revived in them. "قَضى ربّك ألاّ تعبدوا اِلاّ ایّاه و بالوالدین احساناً"

50) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 51) Tafsir Namuneh. 52) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 53) Tafsir Furqan. 54) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 157. 55) Maryam, 39. 56) Maryam, 14. 57) أنا و علىّ ابوا هذه الامّة». Bihar, Vol. 16, p. 95. 58) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 59) Hajj, 78.

Messages

1- Tawhid (Monotheism) is at the head of all Divine commandments. "قَضى ربّك ألاّ تعبدوا اِلاّ ایّاه"

2- Serving and being kind (Ihsan) to parents is among the characteristics of a true monotheist. "الاّتعبدوا الاّ ایّاه و بالوالدین احساناً"

3- The command to be kind to parents, like the command of Tawhid, is definite and unchangeable. "قَضى"

4- Kindness to parents is mentioned alongside Tawhid and obedience to Allah to show that this act is both a rational obligation and a human duty, as well as a religious obligation. "قَضى ربّك ألاّ تعبدوا اِلاّ ایّاه و بالوالدین احساناً"

5- The new generation must maintain a strong connection with the past generation under the shade of faith. "لاتعبدوا الاّ ایّاه و بالوالدین احساناً"

6- In being kind to parents, their being Muslim is not a condition. "بالوالدین احساناً"

7- In kindness to the father and mother, there is no difference between the two. "بالوالدین احساناً"

8- We should perform kindness to parents directly and by our own hands. "بالوالدین احساناً"

9- Ihsan (kindness) is higher than Infaq (spending/charity) and includes affection, respect, teaching, consultation, obedience, gratitude, care, and similar acts. "بالوالدین احساناً"

10- Kindness to parents has no limits or boundaries. "بالوالدین احساناً" (Unlike charity to the poor, which lasts until they are satisfied; or Jihad, which lasts until the removal of strife; or fasting, which lasts until Iftar.)

11- The Quran's command for kindness is directed toward the children, not the parents. "و بالوالدین احساناً" Because parents do not need a command and naturally show kindness to their children.

12- The greater the physical and spiritual needs of the parents, the more essential kindness to them becomes. "یبلغنّ عندك الكبر"

13- We should not take elderly parents to a nursing home, but rather keep them with us. "عندك"

14- When the Quran prohibits hurting a strange beggar, "امّا السائل فلا تَنهَر" (But as for the beggar, do not repel him), the duty toward parents is clear. "فلا تَنهَر هما"

15- Both kindness (Ihsan) and beautiful, good speech are necessary. "احساناً ... قل لهما قولاً كریماً" (After Ihsan, noble speech is the most important method of interacting with parents.)

16- In kindness to parents and noble speech, the condition of reciprocity is not mentioned. Meaning, even if they do not treat you nobly, you must speak nobly. "وقل لهما قولاً كریماً"

Ayah

وَٱخْفِضْ لَهُمَا جَنَاحَ ٱلذُّلِّ مِنَ ٱلرَّحْمَةِ وَقُل رَّبِّ ٱرْحَمْهُمَا كَمَا رَبَّيَانِى صَغِيرًا (۲٤)
Lower the wing of humility to them, mercifully, and say, ‘My Lord! Have mercy on them, just as they reared me when I was [a] small [child]!’ (24)

Messages

1- The child, regardless of his status, must be humble and should not show off his achievements to his parents. «واخفِض لهما جناح الذّل»

2- Humility before parents must stem from kindness and affection, not be superficial or artificial, nor for the purpose of acquiring their wealth. «واخفض لهما... من الرّحمة»

3- The child must be both humble toward his parents and ask Allah for mercy for them. «واخفض... و قل ربّ ارحمهما»

4- The supplication of the child for his parents is answered; otherwise, Allah would not have commanded the prayer. «وقل ربّ ارحمهما...»

5- Supplication for parents is a command from Allah and a sign of gratitude toward them. «قل ربّ ارحمهما...»

6- In supplication, let us not neglect the word «ربّ» (Rabb/Lord). «قل ربّ ارحمهما»

7- Divine Mercy compensates for the efforts of the parents in upbringing. «ربّ ارحمهما كما ربّیانى» It is as if Allah is telling the child: Do not withhold your own kindness, and also seek help from God, for fulfilling their right is beyond your capacity.

8- Do not forget your past, the bitterness and difficulties of childhood and infancy that were imposed upon the parents. «كما ربّیانى صغیراً»

9- Parents must raise their children based on affection. «ارحمهما كما ربّیانى»

10- Man must thank and appreciate his educators and mentors. «ارحمهما كما ربّیانى»

Ayah

رَّبُّكُمْ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا فِى نُفُوسِكُمْ ۚ إِن تَكُونُوا۟ صَـٰلِحِينَ فَإِنَّهُۥ كَانَ لِلْأَوَّٰبِينَ غَفُورًا (۲٥)
Your Lord knows best what is in your hearts. Should you be righteous, He is indeed most forgiving toward penitents. (25)

Messages

1- Knowledge is among the affairs of Allah's Lordship (Rububiyyah). «ربّكم اعلم»

2- Our motivation in every action and in serving parents is under His supervision. «ربّكم اعلم...»

3- Qualifications relate to the inner self and motivations. «فى نفوسكم ان تكونوا صالحین»

4- If the heart and inner self are pure and righteous, the door of repentance is open. «تكونوا صالحین... للاوّابین غفوراً»

5- We should perform our repentance with continuous turning (to Him). «اوّابین»

6- Allah is Forgiving, but the condition for us to benefit from Divine forgiveness is repentance. «ان تكونوا صالحین ... للاوّابین غفوراً»

7- If we have treated our parents inappropriately out of ignorance, we can attract Allah's mercy through repentance. «صالحین... غفورا» (Considering the previous Ayah)

8- Divine pardon and forgiveness are among the affairs of His Lordship. «ربّكم... غفوراً»

9- One of the signs of a person being righteous is repentance. «ان تكونوا صالحین... للاوّابین»

10- Sometimes even righteous individuals commit slips, which must, of course, be compensated for through repentance. «ان تكونوا صالحین... اوّابین»

Ayah

وَءَاتِ ذَا ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ حَقَّهُۥ وَٱلْمِسْكِينَ وَٱبْنَ ٱلسَّبِيلِ وَلَا تُبَذِّرْ تَبْذِيرًا (۲٦)
Give the relatives their [due] right, and the needy and the traveller [as well], but do not squander wastefully. (26)

Key Points

"Tabdhīr" (تَبذیر) is derived from the root "badhr" (بَذر), meaning extravagance or squandering. For example, preparing food for ten people when only two guests are present. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Anyone who spends something outside the path of obedience to Allah is a squanderer (mubadhdhir). (60) In response to the question of whether extravagance (isrāf) exists even in lawful expenditures, that Imam said: Yes, because a person who gives away his wealth in such a way that He leaves nothing for Himself has committed extravagance even in lawful spending. (61)

After the revelation of this Ayah, the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله sought to know Allah’s intention regarding "dhī al-qurbā" (the near relative). Allah revealed to him that He should give "Fadak" to Fatimah عليها السلام, and the Prophet granted Fadak (62) to Fatimah. However, after the Prophet’s passing, Fadak was unjustly taken from the Ahl al-Bayt, and the Ahl al-Bayt constantly cited this Ayah to reclaim their right.

Shi’a and Sunni commentators, such as Tabari, inspired by narrations, write that when Imam Sajjad عليه السلام arrived in Syria (Sham) with the caravan of captives during his captivity, in the course of arguing with those who called the Ahl al-Bayt captives irreligious and Kharijites, he cited this Ayah and said: The intent of "dhā al-qurbā" is us! (63)

Some narrations consider the Ayah related to Khums (the fifth), while others consider the subject of the Ayah to be higher than material matters, stating: The Ayah commands the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله to fulfill the right of Ali ibn Abi Talib عليهما السلام by appointing him as his successor (through a will). (64) And the intent of tabdhīr (squandering) forbidden in the Ayah is ghuluww (exaggeration/extremism). (65)

(60) Bihār, Vol. 75, p. 302. (61) Tafsīr Nūr al-Thaqalayn. (62) Fadak was a very large farm that came into the possession of the Prophet without war. Considering the popularity, infallibility, asceticism, trustworthiness, and precision of the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام, and for the purpose of providing economic support for the propagation of their path and the eradication of poverty from the Ummah, the Prophet placed it at the disposal of the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام. However, after the Prophet’s demise, Abu Bakr seized it. Then Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz returned it, but it was seized again. It was returned during the time of Ma’mun, and then seized yet again. There are many historical, legal, and political discussions regarding Fadak mentioned in the commentaries under this Ayah, all of which we shall disregard here. (63) Iḥtijāj Ṭabarsī, Vol. 2, p. 33; Tafsīr Nūr al-Thaqalayn. (64) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding this Ayah: The right of Ali ibn Abi Talib عليهما السلام is the succession of the Messenger of Allah and the possession of prophetic knowledge. Kāfī, Vol. 1, p. 294. (65) Tafsīr Furqān.

Messages

1- Religious commands are consistent with human nature (fitra). Man is naturally inclined first toward his parents and then toward his relatives; the command to fulfill rights in Islam is also based on this. (Considering the previous verses and the phrase) "آتِ ذا القربى حقه والمسكین و ابن السبیل" (Give the near relative his due, and the needy, and the traveler).

2- In spending (infāq), priorities must be considered. First parents, then relatives, then the poor and the traveler (ibn al-sabīl). "آتِ ذا القربى..."

3- Relatives have a right upon us, and we confer no favor upon them. "آت ذاالقربى حقه" (Give the near relative his due).

4- Kinship, poverty, and being stranded on a journey are causes for the establishment of specific rights. "آت ذاالقربى حقّه و المسكین و ابن السبیل"

5- Poverty is not a condition for paying the right of relatives. "آت ذا القربى حقّه والمسكین و ابن السبیل"

6- In spending, we must observe moderation and not exceed the limit. "آت... و لاتبذّر" (Give... and do not squander).

7- Squandering and unnecessary expenditure of wealth is forbidden (harām). "ولا تبذّر" (And do not squander).

8- Man is not free to spend his wealth and assets in any way He pleases. "و لاتبذّر تبذیرا" (And do not squander wastefully).

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلْمُبَذِّرِينَ كَانُوٓا۟ إِخْوَٰنَ ٱلشَّيَـٰطِينِ ۖ وَكَانَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ لِرَبِّهِۦ كَفُورًا (۲۷)
Indeed the wasteful are brothers of satans, and Satan is ungrateful to his Lord. (27)

Key Points

The word akh (أَخ) in Arabic means both "brother" and "companion" or "associate." For example, akhu al-safar (أَخو السفر) is used for someone who is constantly traveling, and akhu al-karam (أَخو الكَرَم) is said of a generous person. Brotherhood is sometimes based on lineage, and sometimes on ideology and creed; the extravagant are the ideological brothers of Satan. Ikhwan al-Shayatin (إِخوان الشیاطین) means those who are on the path of Satan and accompany him. In the Quran, there is constant mention of Satan's temptation (waswasah) or his authority (wilayah) over individuals, but the only instance where Ikhwan al-Shayatin (إِخوان الشیاطین) appears is in this verse. This means that the extravagant are Satan's collaborators, not merely under his command. Thus, the matter has moved beyond mere temptation and reached the stage of collaboration and assistance. Just as it is mentioned elsewhere: «نُقیّض له شیطاناً فهو له قرین»(66) Although tabdhir (extravagance/wastefulness) is mostly related to financial matters, it also applies to other blessings, such as wasting one's life and youth, using one's intellect, eyes, ears, and tongue in improper ways, entrusting responsibilities to unqualified individuals, accepting responsibilities without merit or beyond one's capacity and ability, teaching and learning unnecessary and useless subjects, and similar instances.(67)

66) Zukhruf, 36. 67) Tafsir Atayib al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Spending wealth and possessions inappropriately is a satanic act and a form of ingratitude. «المبذّرین... إِخوان الشیاطین»

2- The extravagant and wasteful person should be scorned, not appreciated and respected. «إِخوان الشیاطین»

3- The believer is a brother to the believer, «إنّما المؤمنون إِخوَة»(68) and the extravagant person is a brother to Satan. «إنّ المبذّرین... إِخوان الشیاطین»

4- Extravagance (tabdhir) is a sign of disbelief and ingratitude, not a sign of generosity and benevolence. «المبذّرین... لربّه كفوراً» 68) Hujurat, 10.

Ayah

وَإِمَّا تُعْرِضَنَّ عَنْهُمُ ٱبْتِغَآءَ رَحْمَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّكَ تَرْجُوهَا فَقُل لَّهُمْ قَوْلًا مَّيْسُورًا (۲۸)
And if you have to hold off from [assisting] them [for now], seeking your Lord’s mercy which you expect [in the future], speak to them gentle words. (28)

Key Points

This Ayah is about appropriate interaction with people. Thus, even when the Prophet was awaiting divine relief and mercy, if someone requested something from him that he did not possess, he would pray for them and say: May Allah provide sustenance for us and you from His bounty. (69) When Fatimah al-Zahra عليها السلام requested a maidservant from the Prophet to help with her household chores, the Prophet said: There are 400 people in the mosque who have neither clothes nor food. If it were not for that, I would have assigned a servant to you. He then taught her the supplications known as the "Tasbih of Hadrat Zahra." (70) The Quran provides instructions regarding how to speak to people, including that one should speak to them with words that are soft, gentle, easy, firm, appropriate (known), and eloquent. «قَولاً مَیسوراً», «قولاً لَیّناً» (71), «قولاً كریماً» (72), «قولاً سَدیداً» (73), «قولاً مَعروفاً» (74), «قولاً بَلیغاً» (75)

69) Tafsir Lahiji. 70) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 71) Taha, 44. 72) Isra, 23. 73) Nisa, 9. 74) Nisa, 8. 75) Nisa, 63.

Messages

1- Prophets, too, sometimes have nothing available to help others. «اِبتغاء رحمةٍ من ربّك ترجوها»

2- One must be hopeful of Divine mercy. «رحمةٍ من ربّك»

3- We are not creditors of Allah; whatever He gives is from His mercy. «رحمةٍ من ربّك»

4- Effort and hope must go hand-in-hand, and neither is sufficient on its own. «اِبتغاء... ترجوها»

5- The believer seeks resources in order to attend to the deprived. «اِبتغاءرحمةٍ»

6- We should consider material resources to be from Divine mercy. «اِبتغاء رحمة من ربّك»

7- Material resources are for the purpose of human training and perfection. «رحمةٍ من ربّك»

8- Lack of means is an acceptable excuse for refraining from spending (in charity) and not helping. «تعرضنّ عنهم ابتغاء...»

9- If we cannot meet someone's financial need, we should at least win their heart with appropriate speech. «قل لهم قولاً میسوراً»

10- Give the poor a promise that you can fulfill and which does not create problems. «قل لهم قولاً میسوراً»

Ayah

وَلَا تَجْعَلْ يَدَكَ مَغْلُولَةً إِلَىٰ عُنُقِكَ وَلَا تَبْسُطْهَا كُلَّ ٱلْبَسْطِ فَتَقْعُدَ مَلُومًا مَّحْسُورًا (۲۹)
Do not keep your hand chained to your neck, nor open it altogether, or you will sit blameworthy and regretful. (29)

Key Points

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The meaning of "مَحسور" (maḥsūr) is the regret (ḥasrat) of poverty and destitution. (76) Some have said: "Blame" (malāmat) relates to the beginning of the Ayah, which concerns stinginess, and "regret" (ḥasrat) relates to the end of the Ayah and extravagance. A woman sent her son to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and requested His shirt as a blessing (tabarruk). The Prophet gave His only shirt, and since He had no other clothing, He did not leave the house for prayer. This Ayah was revealed to convey the message that generosity does not mean giving away your only shirt. In another narration, it is stated: A quantity of gold was brought to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, and the Prophet immediately spent it all (infaqa). The next day, a poor person made a request to Him. He had nothing to give him. The poor man reproached Him. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله became saddened that He had nothing to help him with, upon which this Ayah was revealed. (77)

76) Kāfī, Vol. 2, p. 55. 77) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- A believer must avoid stinginess and help others. «لاتجعل یدك مغلولة»

2- Islam is the school of moderation and temperance, even in spending (infaq). «لاتجعل، لاتبسطها»

3- Excess (ifrāṭ) and negligence (tafrīṭ) are condemned, and moderation is the commendable method. «لاتجعل، لاتبسطها»

4- The result of extravagance and excess in spending is confinement (staying home), blame, and regret. «فتقعد ملوماً محسوراً»

5- Islam is never pleased with the humiliation and disgrace of Muslims and has forbidden any action that leads them to disgrace. «لا تجعل... لا تبسطها...فتقعد ملوماً محسوراً»

6- Attention to the dire consequence of an action causes one to avoid it. «لا تجعل... فتقعد...»

7- Foresight is one of the principles of management. «لا تجعل... فتقعد...»

Ayah

إِنَّ رَبَّكَ يَبْسُطُ ٱلرِّزْقَ لِمَن يَشَآءُ وَيَقْدِرُ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ بِعِبَادِهِۦ خَبِيرًۢا بَصِيرًا (۳٠)
Indeed your Lord expands the provision for whomever He wishes, and tightens it. Indeed, He is well aware of His servants and a keen observer. (30)

Key Points

The Qur'an discusses sustenance (rizq) extensively. Some of the points derived from those verses include:

A: The sustenance of every creature is incumbent upon Allah. «مَا مِنْ دَابَّةٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ إِلَّا عَلَى اللَّهِ رِزْقُهَا»(78) B: The source of sustenance is in the heaven. «وَفِي السَّمَاءِ رِزْقُكُمْ»(79) C: If all people were granted abundant sustenance, corruption would spread. «وَلَوْ بَسَطَ اللَّهُ الرِّزْقَ لِعِبَادِهِ لَبَغَوْا فِي الْأَرْضِ»(80) D: People must strive and seek sustenance from Allah. «فَابْتَغُوا عِنْدَ اللَّهِ الرِّزْقَ»(81) E: Piety (Taqwa) is among the factors that bring relief and cause expansion in sustenance. «وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجًا * وَيَرْزُقْهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَحْتَسِبُ»(82)

Imam Ali عليه السلام says: «قَدّر الأرزاق فكثّرها و قلّلها و قسّمها على الضیق و السّعة فعدل فیها لیبتلى مَن اراد بمیسورها و معسورها و لیختبر بذلك الشّكر و الصبر مِن غنیّها و فقیرها»(83) Allah decreed the sustenance of people by increasing and decreasing it, and distributed it between restriction and abundance, acting justly therein, so that He may test whomever He wills through ease and hardship, and examine and select the rich and the poor based on the gratitude and patience they demonstrate.

78) Hud, 6. 79) Adh-Dhariyat, 22. 80) Ash-Shura, 27. 81) Al-Ankabut, 17. 82) At-Talaq, 2. 83) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 91.

Messages

1- The increase and decrease of sustenance are in the hand of Allah. «يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ لِمَنْ يَشَاءُ وَيَقْدِرُ»

2- The expansion or restriction of sustenance is among the affairs of Allah's Lordship (Rububiyyah) and is intended for the growth and training of human beings. «إِنَّ رَبَّكَ يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ... وَيَقْدِرُ»

3- Allah's Will is based on His insight and awareness. «لِمَنْ يَشَاءُ ... خَبِيرًا بَصِيرًا»

4- Do not be stingy out of fear of your own poverty, nor give away all your wealth at once to eliminate the poverty of others, for sustenance is in the hand of Allah, and the poverty of some is based on Divine Wisdom. «لَا تَجْعَلْ يَدَكَ... إِنَّ رَبَّكَ يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ»

Ayah

وَلَا تَقْتُلُوٓا۟ أَوْلَـٰدَكُمْ خَشْيَةَ إِمْلَـٰقٍ ۖ نَّحْنُ نَرْزُقُهُمْ وَإِيَّاكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ قَتْلَهُمْ كَانَ خِطْـًٔا كَبِيرًا (۳۱)
Do not kill your children for the fear of penury: We will provide for them and for you. Killing them is indeed a great iniquity. (31)

Key Points

Imlāq (املاق) means poverty and destitution. Perhaps its root is malaq (مَلَق), because the poor person resorts to flattery (tamalluq). This Ayah illustrates the unstable economic situation during the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah). Even today, the world, due to concerns over population increase and economic shortages, permits abortion, and has thus fallen into a type of Jahiliyyah (Ignorance). In Ayah 151 of Surah Al-An'am, Allah attributes the killing of children to poverty, [stating]: «لا تقتلوا اولادكم من املاقٍ» (Do not kill your children because of poverty). But in this Ayah (Al-Isra 31), He counts the killing as being due to the fear of poverty, [stating]: «خشیة املاق» (fear of poverty). There (in Al-An'am), concerning poverty, He says: «نحن نرزقكم و ایّاهم» (We provide sustenance for you and for them). Here (in Al-Isra), concerning the fear of poverty, He says: «نحن نرزقهم و ایّاكم» (The provision for your children and yourselves is upon Us). Since the intensity of fear is greater, Allah first guarantees the children's provision, and then the person's own, so that the intensity [of the fear] may decrease. (84) Perhaps the secret behind this difference is that hunger is immediate, while the fear of poverty relates to the future. Therefore, in the first case (immediate poverty), He says: We provide sustenance for you and your children right now. But regarding the fear of the future, He says: Do not worry, We will provide sustenance for your children and yourselves.

84) Tafsirs Fi Zilal al-Qur'an and Furqan.

Messages

1- A child has the right to life, and parents must not take this right away from him. «لاتقتلوا اولادكم»

2- Poverty and destitution affect even the strongest human emotions. «لا تقتلوا اولادكم خشیة املاق»

3- The fear of harm, loss, or poverty is not a justification for sin or for disregarding the rights of others. «لا تقتلوا اولادكم خشیة املاق»

4- If there is no psychological security and faith, not only poverty but also the fear of poverty leads to murder. «لاتقتلوا... خشیة املاق»

5- Attention to the Divine guarantee prevents sin. «لا تقتلوا... نحن نرزقهم»

6- Do not forget faith and reliance (Tawakkul). «نحن نرزقهم»

7- Allah is kinder than parents; do not harbor ill thoughts about Him. «نحن نرزقهم»

8- Sustenance (Rizq) is in the hands of Allah, and the increase in numbers and population is ineffective [in causing poverty]; thus, a child is not a factor of poverty. «نحن نرزقهم و ایّاكم»

9- Sometimes our sustenance is provided in the shadow of the sustenance of our children. «نرزقهم و ایّاكم»

10- Infanticide and abortion are sins and crimes, whether the child is a boy or a girl. «لاتقتلوا اولادكم... خطاً كبیرا»

11- Sins are not equal; we have minor (Saghīrah) and major (Kabīrah) sins. «خطاً كبیراً»

Ayah

وَلَا تَقْرَبُوا۟ ٱلزِّنَىٰٓ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ فَـٰحِشَةً وَسَآءَ سَبِيلًا (۳۲)
Do not approach fornication. It is indeed an indecency and an evil way. (32)

Key Points

Illicit sexual relations (zina), entail numerous individual, social, and familial corruptions and harms. Therefore, it has been forbidden (haram) in Islam, and in the Holy Quran, it is mentioned alongside polytheism (shirk), murder (85), and theft (86). A few of the corruptions of zina are as follows:

1- It is the source of many conflicts, suicides, runaways, illegitimate children, sexually transmitted diseases, and anxiety for respectable families. 2- Since those who commit zina seek each other out merely for lustful gratification, there is no deep intimacy, affection, or peace between them. 3- The adulterer/fornicator does not view the other party as a life partner, but merely as a means of extinguishing lust. 4- Zina causes a reduction in the formation of families through marriage, prevents the establishment of familial connections, and severs the ties of kinship (silat al-rahm). This is because opening the door to zina leads to a lack of desire for marriage. 5- In zina, there is no sense of commitment or duty toward the continuation of the lineage and the upbringing of the child, and the feelings of fatherhood and motherhood disappear. 6- Zina disrupts the system of inheritance, and the true heir remains unknown. 7- It causes the destruction of human personality and identity and leads to premature deaths. «یذهب بالبهاء ویعجّل الفناء» (87) 8- Since committing zina is not without effort and expense, the adulterer/fornicator resorts to many sins to satisfy his desires. For example, sometimes to achieve gratification, he engages in slander, intimidation, and theft, and may even commit murder. 9- Illegitimate and identity-less children appear in society, which is the basis for many corruptions and crimes, and is a clear manifestation of immorality (fahisha).

Islam has provided programs for the prevention of zina, such as:

1- Prohibition of the mixing of men and women in certain places. 2- Prohibition of a man being alone with a non-mahram woman. 3- Prohibition of a woman displaying her adornments to non-mahram men. 4- Prohibition of looking at a non-mahram, shaking hands with a non-mahram, and avoiding thoughts of zina and viewing provocative images. 5- Severe punishment for the adulterer/fornicator. 6- Recommendation for marriage and keeping the dowry (mahriyyah) low. 7- Condemnation of late marriage. 8- Considering spousal care and effort for the family as acts of worship.

85) الذین لایدعون مع اللّه الهاً آخر و لایقتلون النفس التى حرّم اللّه الا بالحق و لایزنون» الفرقان، ۶۸. 86) اذ جاءك المؤمنات یبایعنك على ان لایشركن باللّه و لایسرقن و لایزنین...» الممتحنة، ۱۲. 87) بحار، ج ۷۷، ص ۵۸.

Messages

1- The attraction of some sins is so great that even approaching them is dangerous. «لاتقربوا» 2- One must avoid not only zina itself but also its preliminaries. (Sometimes a glance, a phone call, a letter, or contact becomes the basis for illicit relations.) «لاتقربوا» 3- Throughout history, zina has been an ugly and undesirable act, and it was forbidden in other religions as well. «كان فاحشةً» 4- In forbidding evil (nahy ‘anil munkar), we should explain the ugliness of the sin. «لاتقربوا... ساء سبیلا» 5- Zina is both a sin, a path to other sins, and a cause of a bad outcome. «ساء سبیلاً»

Ayah

وَلَا تَقْتُلُوا۟ ٱلنَّفْسَ ٱلَّتِى حَرَّمَ ٱللَّهُ إِلَّا بِٱلْحَقِّ ۗ وَمَن قُتِلَ مَظْلُومًا فَقَدْ جَعَلْنَا لِوَلِيِّهِۦ سُلْطَـٰنًا فَلَا يُسْرِف فِّى ٱلْقَتْلِ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ مَنصُورًا (۳۳)
Do not kill a soul [whose life] Allah has made inviolable, except with due cause, and whoever is killed wrongfully, We have certainly given his heir an authority. But let him not commit any excess in killing [the murderer], for he has been assisted [by law]. (33)

Key Points

The phrase «فلایسرف فى القتل» (So let him not commit excess in killing) means that in retaliation (Qisas), you should not kill more than the killer, nor should you mutilate him, as such a custom existed during the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah). Taking a life (Qatl al-Nafs) and murder are among the major sins (Kabā’ir). Whoever kills a person unjustly, not in retaliation (Qisas) or for spreading corruption (Fasād) on earth, it is as if he has killed all of mankind: «مَن قتل نفساً بغیر نفسٍ او فسادٍ فى الارض فكاّنما قتل النّاس جمیعاً»(88) And the punishment for such killing is eternal Hellfire. «مَن یقتل مؤمناً متعمّداً فجزائه جهّنم خالداً فیها»(89) This punishment is also established for anyone who draws a weapon, threatens murder, and is recognized as a corruptor (Mufsid) and a combatant (Muhārib).(90) In narrations, one of the manifestations (Masādiq) of the "guardian" (Walī) is considered to be Hadrat Mahdi عليه السلام, who will take revenge for his dear grandfather, Imam Husayn عليه السلام.(91) The phrase «فقد جعلنا لولیّه سلطاناً» (We have certainly given his guardian authority) signifies that Allah’s support for the oppressed and the granting of the right of retaliation (Qisas) to the victim's guardian (Walī al-Dam) is both a deterrent against murder and a deterrent against excess in retaliation. «جعلنا لولیّه سلطاناً»

88) Al-Ma'idah, 32. 89) An-Nisā', 93. 90) Al-Ma'idah, 33. 91) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Every human being has the right to life; even a non-Muslim who is not at war with Muslims has immunity regarding his life and property. «لا تقتلوا النفس ...»

2- Divine law is the axis of values and sanctities. «حرّم اللّه»

3- Suicide is forbidden (Harām). «لا تقتلوا النّفس الّتى حرّم اللّه»

4- From the perspective of Islam, killing individuals is permissible in cases of right (such as Qisas, self-defense, apostasy (Irtidād), sodomy (Liwāṭ), or adultery by a married person (Zinā al-Muḥṣanah)). «الاّ بالحقّ»

5- The oppressed, whoever he may be, must be supported. «من قتل مظلوماً... منصوراً»

6- Establishing the right of Qisas is for the protection of the oppressed. «جعلنا لولیّه سلطاناً»

7- The guardians of the victim possess the right of Qisas and the right to kill the murderer. «جعلنا لولیّه سلطاناً»

7- In Qisas, anger and rage must be controlled, and justice must be observed. «فلا یسرف فى القتل» Islam has stipulated justice even for those who transgress against human lives. The victim's guardians do not have the right to kill more than one person in retaliation (Qisas) or misuse the law of Qisas.

9- Do not mutilate the murderer, and do not act dishonorably in the type and manner of Qisas. «فلا یسرف فى القتل»

10- Support for the oppressed is among the Divine traditions. «انّه كان منصورا»

Ayah

وَلَا تَقْرَبُوا۟ مَالَ ٱلْيَتِيمِ إِلَّا بِٱلَّتِى هِىَ أَحْسَنُ حَتَّىٰ يَبْلُغَ أَشُدَّهُۥ ۚ وَأَوْفُوا۟ بِٱلْعَهْدِ ۖ إِنَّ ٱلْعَهْدَ كَانَ مَسْـُٔولًا (۳٤)
Do not approach the orphan’s property except in the best manner, until he comes of age. Fulfill the covenants; indeed all covenants are accountable. (34)

Key Points

The word ashudd (أَشُدّ), derived from shadd (شَدّ), refers to a firm knot. Here, it means the stage of the orphan's maturity and his physical and spiritual strength in preserving his wealth. The Quran has strongly emphasized preserving the rights of the orphan, caring for his condition, and attending to and taking responsibility for the affairs of orphans. However, because the potential for financial error and misuse of orphans' wealth is very high, greater caution is necessary, to the extent that approaching it is forbidden, and unjustly disposing of orphans' wealth is considered consuming fire. (92) When this verse was revealed, Muslims refrained from sitting at the tables of orphans and isolated them. Another verse was revealed stating that the intent of not approaching the wealth is misuse; otherwise, those who seek reform should not abandon orphans under the pretext of piety. «و ان تخالطوهم فاخوانكم واللّه یعلم المفسد من المصلح» (93) We read in a hadith: The covenant that will be questioned refers to the love, friendship, and obedience to Ali عليه السلام. (94)

92) Nisa, 10. 93) Baqarah, 220. 94) Bihar, Vol. 24, p. 187.

Messages

1- Islam supports the deprived, the weak, and the orphans. «لاتقربوا مال الیتیم»

2- Wherever there is a potential for misuse, greater emphasis must be placed. (95) «لا تقربوا مال الیتیم»

3- The attraction of wealth is so great that even approaching it sometimes leads to sin and prohibition. «لا تقربوا»

4- Children have the right of ownership. «مال الیتیم»

5- Inheritance is one of the means of ownership. «مال الیتیم»

6- Disposing of the orphan's wealth is conditional upon observing the highest and most fruitful methods. (If the orphan's benefit lies in the circulation of his wealth, it must be put to use, not left idle.) «لاتقربوا... الاّ بالّتى هى احسن»

7- In financial decisions, the interests and welfare of the orphans must be observed. «الاّ بالّتى هى احسن»

8- Improper disposal and squandering of orphans' wealth are forbidden. «لاتقربوا... الاّ بالّتى هى احسن»

9- The time for handing over the orphan's wealth to him is when he reaches intellectual, economic, and physical maturity. «حتّى یبلغ اشدّه»

10- We must be faithful to covenants—whatever they may be and with whomever they are made. «اوفوا بالعهد»

11- Attention to responsibility prevents a person from sin. «اِنّ العهد كان مسئولا»

95) For this reason, Imam Ali عليه السلام, in Nahj al-Balaghah, placed greater emphasis on observing the rights of the hired worker, the farmer, the woman, and the orphan. For example: «اللّه اللّه فى الایتام» Nahj al-Balaghah, Letter 47.

Ayah

وَأَوْفُوا۟ ٱلْكَيْلَ إِذَا كِلْتُمْ وَزِنُوا۟ بِٱلْقِسْطَاسِ ٱلْمُسْتَقِيمِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ خَيْرٌ وَأَحْسَنُ تَأْوِيلًا (۳٥)
When you measure, observe fully the measure, [and] weigh with an even balance. That is better and more favourable with respect to the outcome [in the Hereafter]. (35)

Key Points

"Qisṭās" (قِسطاس) is a combination of two words: "qisṭ" (قِسط), meaning justice, and "ṭās" (طاس), meaning the pan of a scale. (96) It is mentioned in a hadith that the infallible Imam (امام معصوم) is the model and embodiment of the "Qisṭās al-Mustaqīm" (the Straight Scale) in the Islamic society. (97) The issues of trade, exchange, and observing people's rights in transactions are so important that the Quran has emphasized them repeatedly. The longest verse in the Quran (98) relates to this topic, and a Surah is named "Al-Muṭaffifīn" (The Defrauders/Short-Changers). The primary call of some prophets, such as Prophet Shu'ayb, was also to abandon short-changing. (99)

96) Tafsir al-Mizan. 97) Bihar, vol. 24, p. 187. 98) Baqarah, 282. 99) Shu'ara, 181 - 183.

Messages

1- The Muslim marketplace must be free from fraud and short-changing, and the seller must be trustworthy, precise, honest, and meticulous in accounting. «اوفوا الكیل»

2- Giving full and correct measure is an example of fulfilling covenants, which was mentioned in the previous verse, because a transaction is a type of commitment. «اوفوا بالعهد... اوفوا الكیل»

3- Scales, measuring instruments, and commercial calculations must be sound and accurate. «وزنوا بالقسطاس المستقیم»

4- Precision in scales, weight, and calculations is a source of goodness (khayr) and blessing (barakah), while short-changing removes goodness and blessing. «ذلك خیرٌ»

5- The philosophy behind divine commands is the welfare of humans themselves. «اوفوا، زنوا... ذلك خیر»

6- Economic honesty is superior to wealth gained through short-changing. The result of honest trade and observing people's rights returns to the individual, as it builds trust.
