However, short-changing and inaccurate measures and accounts lead society toward financial corruption and the loss of trust. Thus, a good outcome and consequence depend on honesty. «ذلك خیر»

7- Righteous earning leads to a good ultimate outcome. «احسن تأویلاً»

Although the short-changer achieves temporary profit, the loss of worldly credibility and the preparation for eternal punishment make his endeavor ill-fated.

Ayah

وَلَا تَقْفُ مَا لَيْسَ لَكَ بِهِۦ عِلْمٌ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلسَّمْعَ وَٱلْبَصَرَ وَٱلْفُؤَادَ كُلُّ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ كَانَ عَنْهُ مَسْـُٔولًا (۳٦)
Do not pursue that of which you have no knowledge. Indeed hearing, eyesight, and the heart—all of these are accountable. (36)

Key Points

Mentioning the eye, ear, and heart (or mind) in the Ayah is perhaps by way of example, because on the Day of Judgment, in addition to the person himself, the hands, feet, and other limbs will also be questioned (100), and they will speak and confess (101). It is also possible that this is because the motivation to follow others comes through seeing, hearing, or thinking and understanding. Throughout history, many conflicts and seditions have arisen from hasty judgments, unsubstantiated claims, and theories lacking research and knowledge. Acting upon this Ayah safeguards the individual and society against many deceptions and errors. This is because undue optimism, gullibility, and acceptance of rumors expose society to humiliation and surrender to the enemy. Blind imitation, following habits and fantasies, adhering to ancestral traditions, believing prophecies, dreams, guesses, and conjectures, judging without knowledge, testifying without knowledge, taking a stance, praising or criticizing without knowledge, interpreting, analyzing, writing, and issuing fatwas without knowledge, relaying baseless hearsay and rumors, attributing something to Allah and religion without knowledge, making decisions in emotional and critical conditions without proof or evidence, relying on the false oaths and tears of others—all of these are instances of «لاتَقف ما لیس لك به علم» (Do not follow that of which you have no knowledge) and are forbidden. According to reliable and successive narrations (mutawatir), following definitive religious principles (such as the fatwa of a Mujtahid, or knowledge that leads to certainty-like assurance) is considered following knowledge. Just as people gain knowledge from the prescription of a compassionate and specialized doctor, they also gain knowledge of Allah’s ruling from the fatwa of a qualified and pious Mujtahid. Therefore, this level of knowledge is sufficient, although knowledge has degrees, and its deeper stages include: ‘Ilm al-Yaqin (Knowledge of Certainty), Haqq al-Yaqin (Truth of Certainty), and ‘Ayn al-Yaqin (Eye of Certainty). The infallible Imams علیهم السلام prevented their companions from hearing and saying every word, advising them to be the gatekeepers of their hearts and ears. In this matter, they cited the Ayah «لاتقف ما لیس لك به علم». For instance, Imam Sadiq علیه السلام, in order to caution a person who prolonged his stay in the restroom to listen to the sound of his neighbor’s music and singing, recited this Ayah and said: The ear will be questioned about what it heard, the eye about what it saw, and the heart about its thoughts. Imam Sajjad علیه السلام also said: A person does not have the right to say whatever he wishes (102).

100) Noor, 24; Yasin, 65; Fussilat, 20. 101) Perhaps the phrase «كل اولئك» indicates that these three organs are mentioned by way of example, otherwise the meaning of the Ayah would be complete even without «كلّ اولئك». 102) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Life must be based on knowledge, correct information, logic, and insight. «لاتقف ما لیس لك به علم»

2- We should not fuel the rumor mill or destroy the reputation and rights of individuals by relaying baseless hearsay. «لا تقف ...»

3- The Quran dismantles the foundation of sorcerers, soothsayers, and fortune-tellers who gather gullible people around them. «لاتقف ما لیس لك به علم»

4- The path to recognition is not only through the senses; the heart (mind) is also one of the paths to recognition. «السمع و البصر و الفؤاد»

5- On the Day of Judgment, the inner self and intentions will also be questioned. «الفؤاد»

6- Belief in the Day of Judgment and Allah’s accountability is the factor that generates piety (Taqwa). «كلّ اولئك كان مسئولا»

7- Failure to utilize the body and resources correctly will lead to accountability. «كلّ اولئك كان مسئولا»

Ayahs

وَلَا تَمْشِ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مَرَحًا ۖ إِنَّكَ لَن تَخْرِقَ ٱلْأَرْضَ وَلَن تَبْلُغَ ٱلْجِبَالَ طُولًا (۳۷)
Do not walk exultantly on the earth. Indeed, you will neither pierce the earth, nor reach the mountains in height. (37)
كُلُّ ذَٰلِكَ كَانَ سَيِّئُهُۥ عِندَ رَبِّكَ مَكْرُوهًا (۳۸)
The evil of all these is detestable to your Lord. (38)

Key Points

A sign of the comprehensiveness of Islam is that, in addition to matters of belief, politics, military affairs, and economics, it also provides instructions for minor actions, such as walking outside the home. Among these are: A: «واقصد فى مشیك»(103) Be moderate in your walking. B: «ولا تَمشِ فى الارض مرحاً» Do not walk upon the earth with arrogance and intoxication (of pride). C: «و عباد الرّحمن الّذین یمشون على الارض هوناً»(104) The servants of Allah are those who walk upon the earth with tranquility and without arrogance. D: It also criticizes the wealthy Qarun, who appeared with arrogance, splendor, and majesty when passing through the streets and markets.(105)

It is recorded in the biography (Sirah) of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله that even on the day of the Conquest of Mecca, he rode a donkey without a saddle. Imam Hassan al-Mujtaba عليه السلام, despite possessing many mounts, would walk to Mecca. These are signs of the humility of the guardians of the religion. Imam Ali عليه السلام also lists humble walking among the characteristics of the pious (Muttaqin). «و مَشیُهم التواضع»(106)

103) Luqman, 61. 104) Furqan, 63 105) Qasas, 79. 106) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 193.

Messages

1- Arrogance, even in the manner of walking, is condemned by the Quran. «ولا تمش فى الارض مَرَحاً»

2- The way to counter arrogance is by drawing attention to weaknesses and incapacities. «انّك لن تخرق الارض»

3- Inner characteristics influence human behavior. «ولا تمش فى الارض مرحاً»

4- The evil nature of vile deeds is a constant matter in all divine religions. «كان سیّئه...مكروها»

5- The commands and prohibitions of the Lord are aimed at the growth and refinement of humanity. «عند ربّك»

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ مِمَّآ أَوْحَىٰٓ إِلَيْكَ رَبُّكَ مِنَ ٱلْحِكْمَةِ ۗ وَلَا تَجْعَلْ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَـٰهًا ءَاخَرَ فَتُلْقَىٰ فِى جَهَنَّمَ مَلُومًا مَّدْحُورًا (۳۹)
These are among [precepts] that your Lord has revealed to you of wisdom. Do not set up another god besides Allah, or you will be cast into hell, being blameworthy and banished [from His mercy]. (39)

Key Points

Some commentators say: The meaning of "wisdom" (حكمت) refers to the fixed rulings mentioned in the previous verses, which were also present in other religions and are not subject to abrogation (naskh). In Ayah 22, the command prohibiting polytheism (shirk) was the first wisdom, and in this Ayah, the prohibition of polytheism is the conclusion of the wisdoms. Sometimes Allah, by addressing His Prophet sharply, is actually warning the disbelievers (kuffar) to abandon their hopes concerning the Prophet, as if He is saying: If you turn to other than Allah, you will be cast into Hell.

Messages

1- The commands and prohibitions of Allah are based on wisdom, knowledge of humanity's educational needs, and are in accordance with reason. «اَوحى الیك ربّك من الحكمة»

2- Reason ('aql) and innate nature (fitrah) do not render man independent of revelation (wahy); rather, man is in need of revelation. «ذلك ممّا اَوحى الیك»

3- Regarding the ugliness of polytheism (shirk), it is sufficient to know that even if the Prophet were to pursue it, he would be destroyed. «لاتجعل ... فتلقى فى جهّنم»

4- Apart from the school of revelation and the law of Allah, other paths and schools are deviations that lead to deprivation, blame, and Hell. «لاتجعل مع اللّه... فتلقى فى جهنّم»

5- The end of polytheism is Hell, and the polytheist deserves to be far from Allah's mercy. «فتلقى...»

6- In Hell, polytheists will suffer not only burning but also spiritual torment. «مَلوما مَدحورا»

Ayah

أَفَأَصْفَىٰكُمْ رَبُّكُم بِٱلْبَنِينَ وَٱتَّخَذَ مِنَ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ إِنَـٰثًا ۚ إِنَّكُمْ لَتَقُولُونَ قَوْلًا عَظِيمًا (٤٠)
Did your Lord prefer you for sons, and [Himself] adopt females from among the angels? Indeed, you say a monstrous word! (40)

Key Points

Among the deviant beliefs of the polytheists was that they considered the angels to be the daughters of Allah. The Quran has repeatedly addressed this issue and deemed it a great lie and slander, including: «أم له البنات و لكم البنون»(۱۰۷), «ألكم الذَّكَر و له الاُنثى»(۱۰۸). Of course, the belief that Allah has children has also existed among the Jews and Christians, but the belief in having daughters is specific to the idolaters.

107) Tur, 39. 108) Najm, 21.

Messages

1- The superiority of the son over the daughter is a baseless assumption. «أفاصفاكم... بالبنین»

2- The Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) of Allah encompasses even the polytheists. «ربّكم»

3- The belief that Allah has children is baseless and condemned. «لتقولون قولاعظیماً»

4- The assumption that angels are female (daughters) is wrong. «الملائكة اناثا... قولا عظيما»

5- Man should not approve for others what he dislikes for himself, let alone attributing it to Allah! «لتقولون قولا عظيماً»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ صَرَّفْنَا فِى هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانِ لِيَذَّكَّرُوا۟ وَمَا يَزِيدُهُمْ إِلَّا نُفُورًا (٤۱)
Certainly We have variously paraphrased [the principles of guidance] in this Quran so that they may take admonition, but it increases them only in aversion. (41)

Key Points

The term "صَرّفنا" (Sarrifnā) refers to the varied, diverse, and repeated presentation of discourse aimed at clarifying the subject matter. Man is inherently a seeker of variety, whether in nature or in the Divine Book, and this is the secret behind some of the repetitions and variations in expression found in the Quran. Just as the stench of decay rises from a putrid carcass when pure rain falls upon it, the entry of Divine verses into hearts accustomed to arrogance and obstinacy only increases the foulness of aversion. «ما یزیدهم الاّ نفورا»

Messages

1- The repetition of a subject must be varied and attractive, "صرّفنا," because monotony is often tiresome.

2- Man is constantly in need of reminder, and perhaps with every repetition, a group will be attracted. "صرّفنا"

3- Repetition in the verses of the Quran is for the purpose of reminding and taking heed. «صرّفنا، لیذكّروا»

4- Repetition is captivating for friends, but repulsive for the obstinate. «ما یزیدهم الاّ نفورا»

Ayahs

قُل لَّوْ كَانَ مَعَهُۥٓ ءَالِهَةٌ كَمَا يَقُولُونَ إِذًا لَّٱبْتَغَوْا۟ إِلَىٰ ذِى ٱلْعَرْشِ سَبِيلًا (٤۲)
Say, ‘Were there [other] gods besides Him, as they say, they would surely have encroached on the Lord of the Throne. (42)
سُبْحَـٰنَهُۥ وَتَعَـٰلَىٰ عَمَّا يَقُولُونَ عُلُوًّا كَبِيرًا (٤۳)
Immaculate is He, and greatly exalted above what they say!’ (43)

Key Points

The polytheists accepted Allah and knew "Allah" to be the Creator of existence, but they considered the idols to be their intercessors or partners of God. This verse negates the existence of such a relationship between God and the idols, because the idols can neither take power away from the Great God nor do they have any means of strengthening themselves.

Messages

1- The order of the universe is the best proof of God's Oneness. (If there were other gods, there would be competition, and if there were competition, the order would collapse.) «اذاً لابتغوا الى ذى العرش سبیلاً»

2- Whenever an undue attribution is made, acquittal and purification (of Allah) are necessary. «سبحانه»

3- Allah is pure from every kind of partner, and there is a great distance and superiority between His sacred Essence and the superstitions they utter. «سبحانه و تعالى عمّا یقولون»

4- No matter who praises God and to what extent, Allah is still superior to those sayings and descriptions. «تعالى عمّا یقولون علوّاً كبیراً»

Ayah

تُسَبِّحُ لَهُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتُ ٱلسَّبْعُ وَٱلْأَرْضُ وَمَن فِيهِنَّ ۚ وَإِن مِّن شَىْءٍ إِلَّا يُسَبِّحُ بِحَمْدِهِۦ وَلَـٰكِن لَّا تَفْقَهُونَ تَسْبِيحَهُمْ ۗ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ حَلِيمًا غَفُورًا (٤٤)
The seven heavens glorify Him, and the earth [too], and whoever is in them. There is not a thing but celebrates His praise, but you do not understand their glorification. Indeed, He is all-forbearing, all-forgiving. (44)

Key Points

This verse states: all existence performs tasbih (glorification), sujud (prostration), and qunut (devotion/prayer) for Allah. Some commentators consider this tasbih to be existential (takwini), meaning the structural existence of every particle in the universe indicates Allah's Will, Wisdom, Knowledge, and Justice. Others believe that existence possesses consciousness and knowledge, and all are engaged in tasbih, but our ears do not hear their sound. This view is more consistent with the apparent meaning of the verses. It is not impossible for things to possess speech, as this will occur on the Day of Judgment, «اَنطق كلّ شى»(109) Even stone possesses knowledge and awe, and falls from the mountain out of fear of Allah, «و اِنّ منها لما یَهبط من خشیة اللّه»(110) Prophet Sulayman (Solomon) understood the speech of the ant and knew the language of the birds (mantiq al-tayr). The hoopoe recognized the deviation of the people and came to Sulayman to report it. Allah addressed the mountains: «یا جبال اوّبى معه»(111) O mountains! Pray along with David. In the Quran, besides these instances, the tasbih of other creatures is also mentioned.

All particles of the universe secretly say to you, day and night: We are Hearing, Seeing, and Conscious; but to you, the strangers, we are silent.

The tasbih of animals and creatures is also mentioned in narrations, including: A: Do not slap the faces of animals, for they are glorifying Allah (112). B: Whenever a prey does not perform tasbih, it becomes the hunter's catch (113). C: No tree is cut down except because it abandoned its tasbih (114). D: Pebbles in the hand of the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله testified to His prophethood (115). E: The honeybee performs tasbih (116). F: The sound of sparrows is their tasbih (117).

All these narrations indicate actual tasbih, not merely the language of disposition (zaban-e hal).

Everyone praises Your attribute in a different tongue; the nightingale through singing ghazals, and the turtledove through song.

109) Fussilat, 21. 110) Al-Baqarah, 74. 111) Saba, 10. 112) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 113) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 114) Tafsir Ruh al-Jinan. 115) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 116) Bihar, Vol. 95, p. 372. 117) Bihar, Vol. 64, p. 27.

Messages

1- All existence glorifies Allah, so why should man lag behind this caravan? «تسبّح له السموات...»

2- There are also living and conscious beings in the heavens. «مَن فیهنّ»

3- The tasbih of existence is accompanied by praise and commendation. «یسبّح بحمده»

4- We may hear some sounds, but we do not understand that they are tasbih. «لاتفقهون تسبیحهم»

5- All existence possesses consciousness, but human knowledge of existence is incomplete. «اِن من شى ءٍ الاّ یسبّح... لا تفقهون»

6- Realities are hidden from those who are not worthy of them. «لا تفقهون تسبیحهم»

7- The tolerance of superstitious beliefs and improper speech stems from Allah's forbearance and patience. «عمّا یقولون... حلیماً غفوراً» If we return [to Him], He will surely forgive.

Ayah

وَإِذَا قَرَأْتَ ٱلْقُرْءَانَ جَعَلْنَا بَيْنَكَ وَبَيْنَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ حِجَابًا مَّسْتُورًا (٤٥)
When you recite the Quran, We draw a hidden curtain between you and those who do not believe in the Hereafter, (45)

Key Points

| The Quran is a means of guidance for the pious, «هُدىً للمتّقین» (118), and among the signs of the pious is certainty in the Hereafter, «و بالآخره هم یُوقنون» (119). Therefore, the Quran is not a guide for those who do not believe in the Day of Judgment. This is the hidden veil (hijab) that causes a person to be deprived of understanding revelation and the pleasure of grasping divine knowledge, even if he hears the revelation from the tongue of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله himself.

118) Al-Baqarah, 2. 119) Al-Baqarah, 4.

Messages

1- If a person lacks the capacity for guidance, the recitation of the Quran, even by the Messenger of Allah, will be ineffective. «اذا قَرأت... حجاباً مستورا»

2- Divine wrath follows human obstinacy and disbelief. «جعلنا... لایؤمنون بالاخرة... حجاباً»

3- The recitation of the Quran can secure a person from the evil of the disbelievers. «اذا قَرأت، جعلنا... حجاباً»

4- The recitation of the Quran strengthens the spirit of dissociation (tabarri) from the polytheists within a person. «اذا قَرأت جعلنا... حجاباً»

5- Deprivation from understanding revelation is a divine punishment and wrath. «جعلنا... حجاباً مستوراً» God's rod makes no sound.

6- Belief in the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is the factor for accepting the invitation of the prophets, and disbelief in the Resurrection is the cause for rejecting prophethood and divine knowledge. «لایؤمنون بالاخرة... حجاباً مستورا»

7- Allah has concealed the truths and knowledge from those who are not worthy of them. «حجاباً مستوراً»

Ayah

وَجَعَلْنَا عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ أَكِنَّةً أَن يَفْقَهُوهُ وَفِىٓ ءَاذَانِهِمْ وَقْرًا ۚ وَإِذَا ذَكَرْتَ رَبَّكَ فِى ٱلْقُرْءَانِ وَحْدَهُۥ وَلَّوْا۟ عَلَىٰٓ أَدْبَـٰرِهِمْ نُفُورًا (٤٦)
and We cast veils on their hearts, lest they should understand it, and a deafness into their ears. When you mention your Lord alone in the Quran, they turn their backs in aversion. (46)

Key Points

Akinnah (أَكِنَّة) is the plural of kinān (كِنَان) or kinn (كِنّ), meaning a means of covering. Waqr (وَقْر) means heaviness in the ear. A verse similar to this one has also appeared in other Surahs (120). Monotheism (Tawhid) has been mentioned more than a thousand times in the Quran (121), but unfortunately, the blind-hearted are deprived of and distressed by hearing the verses of Tawhid and benefiting from them, yet they are pleased by hearing words of polytheism (Shirk) and nonsense. «و اذا ذُكِر اللّه وحده اِشمأزّتْ قلوب الّذین لایؤمنون بالاخرة و اذا ذُكِر الّذین من دونه اذا هم یَستبشِرون» (122) Even today, whenever the discussion turns to Tawhid, some people are repulsed, but the words of the Easterners and Westerners hold attraction for them. The Quran has likened such groups who flee from the truth to donkeys that bolt from lions. «كانّهم حمرٌ مستنفرةٌ فَرّت من قَسورة» (123)

120) Fussilat, 44; An'am, 25; Kahf, 57. 121) Tafsir Atib al-Bayan. 122) Zumar, 45. 123) Muddaththir, 50.

Messages

1- A closed spirit and a dead heart do not accept the pure knowledge of the Quran. «اكنّةً ان یفقهوه»

2- Simple hearing and understanding is different from deep comprehension and deriving pleasure from it. «یفقهوه»

3- Being deprived of spiritual perception is a form of Divine wrath. «جعلنا على قلوبهم اكنّةً ان یفقهوه»

4- Remembering other than Allah is disbelief (Kufr); remembering Allah and other than Him is polytheism (Shirk); and remembering only the One God is Monotheism (Tawhid). «ذكرت... وحده»

5- Monotheism in Lordship (Tawhid al-Rububiyyah) is the hardest thing for the polytheists. «ذكرت ربّك فى القرآن وحده ولّوا...»

Ayah

نَّحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا يَسْتَمِعُونَ بِهِۦٓ إِذْ يَسْتَمِعُونَ إِلَيْكَ وَإِذْ هُمْ نَجْوَىٰٓ إِذْ يَقُولُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ إِن تَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا رَجُلًا مَّسْحُورًا (٤۷)
We know best what they listen for, when they listen to you, and when they hold their secret talks, when the wrongdoers say, ‘[If you follow him] You will be following just a bewitched man.’ (47)

Key Points

It is mentioned in the commentaries (tafasir) that each of the leaders of the disbelievers (kuffar), secretly and without informing one another, would appear behind the Prophet's house at night to listen to the sound of the Quran in the darkness and then analyze it. Sometimes they would run into each other in the darkness, recognize one another, and reproach each other, saying, "We ourselves cannot tear our hearts away from the voice of Muhammad, so what should the people do!" This Ayah gives reassurance to the Prophet of God that he should not be affected by the flight and disrespect of the disbelievers, because everything is clear to Him (Allah).

Messages

1- Allah is also aware of the goals and intentions of the people. «نحن اعلم بما یستمعون به»

2- Not all listeners have good intentions. «نحن اعلم ...»

3- The preacher of truth should not retreat due to people's flight or accusations; rather, he must know that he is under the supervision of Allah and be reassured. «نحن اعلم ...»

4- When enemies see people paying attention to divine leaders, they strike them with accusations and lies. «رجلاً مسحوراً»

Ayah

ٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ ضَرَبُوا۟ لَكَ ٱلْأَمْثَالَ فَضَلُّوا۟ فَلَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ سَبِيلًا (٤۸)
Look, how they coin epithets for you; so they go astray, and cannot find a way. (48)

Messages

1- The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, religious leaders, and believers must be aware of the enemy's propaganda methods. «انظر كیف»

2- Examples and analogies can be used both to prove the truth and to obscure the facts. «كیف ضربوا لك الامثال»

3- Those who lack logic to reject the religion resort to using analogies to slander the Prophet and humiliate him. «ضربوا لك الامثال»

4- Insulting divine leaders is a cause of misguidance. «ضربوا لك الامثال فضلّوا»

5- Human misguidance occurs gradually: first, they become involved in insults and inappropriate analogies, then they deviate, and finally, they reach a dead end. «ضربوا لك الامثال، فضلّوا، فلا یستطیعون سبیلاً»

6- Disbelief has no ultimate success. Despite utilizing various slanders and analogies to strike at Islam, the disbelievers still failed. «ضربوا لك الامثال، فضلّوا، فلا یستطیعون سبیلاً»

Ayah

وَقَالُوٓا۟ أَءِذَا كُنَّا عِظَـٰمًا وَرُفَـٰتًا أَءِنَّا لَمَبْعُوثُونَ خَلْقًا جَدِيدًا (٤۹)
They say, ‘What, when we have become bones and dust, shall we really be raised in a new creation?’ (49)

Key Points

"رُفات" (rufāt) means crushed clay. (124)

In the verses of the Quran, no proof is ever presented by the deniers of the Resurrection. All that exists (from them) is astonishment, questioning, and deeming it improbable; they create doubt by raising questions. The Quran, in response to them, relies on the knowledge, power, and wisdom of Allah in creation, and mentions examples in nature, history, and man himself, who did not exist initially and then came into being. Therefore, Allah can revive creatures again after death. 124) Mufradāt al-Rāghib.

Ayahs

۞ قُلْ كُونُوا۟ حِجَارَةً أَوْ حَدِيدًا (٥٠)
Say, ‘[That is bound to happen] even if you should become stones, or iron, (50)
أَوْ خَلْقًا مِّمَّا يَكْبُرُ فِى صُدُورِكُمْ ۚ فَسَيَقُولُونَ مَن يُعِيدُنَا ۖ قُلِ ٱلَّذِى فَطَرَكُمْ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ ۚ فَسَيُنْغِضُونَ إِلَيْكَ رُءُوسَهُمْ وَيَقُولُونَ مَتَىٰ هُوَ ۖ قُلْ عَسَىٰٓ أَن يَكُونَ قَرِيبًا (٥۱)
or a creature more fantastic to your minds!’ They will say, ‘Who will bring us back?’ Say, ‘He who originated you the first time.’ They will nod their heads at you, and say, ‘When will that be?’ Say, ‘Maybe it is near! (51)

Key Points

Although man disintegrates and turns to dust after death, dust is the source and gateway of life. Plants grow from the soil, and living creatures are nurtured in the soil. Therefore, resurrecting the dead from the dust is neither important nor difficult for Allah the Almighty, even if you become stone, iron, or something harder than these, whose distance from life is greater, Allah will still resurrect you. Deniers have no proof for denying the Resurrection; they only have questions: Who, when, and how will He resurrect us? The Quran's answer is that the same God Who created you the first time has the power to recreate you, and its time is not far off; perhaps it is near! «مَن یُعیدنا؟ مَتى هو؟ عسى أن یكون قریبا»

Messages

1- The Prophet is commissioned to answer questions, doubts, and objections. «قل»

2- Resurrecting the dead, even if they have turned into stone or iron, is easy for Allah. «حجارةً او حدیداً او خلقاً ...»

3- The Resurrection is physical (bodily). «كونوا حجارة او حدیدا...»

4- Many things appear great only in our minds and hearts, but they are not actually great. «یكبر فى صدوركم»

5- The reason for denying the Resurrection is heedlessness regarding Divine power. «مَن یُعیدنا... الّذى فَطركم»

6- Stubborn individuals, even upon hearing the proof, shake their heads out of mockery, denial, and disbelief (at the possibility). «فسیُنغضون الیك رئوسهم»

7- The lifespan of this world is short and brief compared to the Hereafter, and everyone should consider death and the Day of Judgment to be near. «أن یكون قربیاً»

Ayah

يَوْمَ يَدْعُوكُمْ فَتَسْتَجِيبُونَ بِحَمْدِهِۦ وَتَظُنُّونَ إِن لَّبِثْتُمْ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا (٥۲)
The day He calls you, you will respond to Him, praising Him, and you will think you remained [in the world] only for a little while.’ (52)

Messages

1- Do not forget the Day of Resurrection. The word 'Udhkur (remember) is implied before the word Yawm (Day).

2- The Day of Resurrection is the day of the general summoning of people. «یدعوكم»

3- At the time of resurrection, all the dead praise Allah (but this is of no benefit to the disbelievers). «فتستجیبون بحمده»

4- The duration of this world and the intermediate realm (Barzakh), compared to the Day of Resurrection, is very short. «اِن لبثتم الاّ قلیلاً»

Ayah

وَقُل لِّعِبَادِى يَقُولُوا۟ ٱلَّتِى هِىَ أَحْسَنُ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنَ يَنزَغُ بَيْنَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنَ كَانَ لِلْإِنسَـٰنِ عَدُوًّا مُّبِينًا (٥۳)
Tell My servants to speak in a manner which is the best. Indeed Satan incites ill feeling between them, and Satan is indeed man’s manifest enemy. (53)

Key Points

| Allah is the Creator of the best things: «احسن كلّ شى ءٍ خلقه» (125), «فاحسن صوركم» (126), «احسن تقویم» (127). He also demands the best from us: the best deed, «لیبلوكم ایُّكم احسن عملاً» (128); the best speech, «یقولوا التّى هى احسن»; and following the best words, «فیتّبعون احسنه» (129). It seems that Muslims had questions regarding how to deal with the disbelievers and their mockery, and how to retaliate, which the Ayah answers with «قل» (Say). This is because sometimes speaking ill of the disbelievers' idols would embolden them to insult Allah in return. «كلام احسن» (the best speech) includes every type of appropriate speech, such as: greeting (salām), supplication (du'ā), guidance (irshād), expressing affection, enjoining good and forbidding evil (amr bi al-ma'rūf wa nahy 'an al-munkar), remembrance of Allah (dhikr), repentance (tawbah) and seeking forgiveness (istighfār), and the like.

125) Sajdah, 7. 126) Ghafir, 64. 127) Tin, 4. 128) Hud, 7. 129) Zumar, 18.

Messages

1- In inviting others to good speech, one must speak well oneself. «یقولوا الّتى هى احسن»

2- Good speech and discourse pave the way for affection, while inappropriate speech paves the way for various Satanic whispers. Indeed, good speech removes the ground for temptation. «یقولوا الّتى هى احسن انّ الشیطان ینزغ...»

3- A requirement of servitude ('ubudiyyah) is speaking kindly to others. «لعبادى، یقولوا الّتى هى احسن»

4- Instigating enmity among people is the work of Satan, and whoever does so is Satan's collaborator. «ینزغ بینهم»

5- Satan has always been the enemy of man and has not hesitated for a moment in his enmity. «اِنّ الشیطان كان للانسان عدوّا»

6- Satan is very serious in stirring up strife and enmity against man. «اِنّ...كان... عدوّاًمبینا»

Ayah

رَّبُّكُمْ أَعْلَمُ بِكُمْ ۖ إِن يَشَأْ يَرْحَمْكُمْ أَوْ إِن يَشَأْ يُعَذِّبْكُمْ ۚ وَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَـٰكَ عَلَيْهِمْ وَكِيلًا (٥٤)
Your Lord knows you best. He will have mercy on you if He wishes, or punish you, if He wishes, and We did not send you to watch over them. (54)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the emphasis was on speaking well (good speech); in this Ayah, examples of it are presented: A person should not consider himself superior to others, nor should he belittle them, and he should not even say to the disbelievers (kuffār): "You are the people of the Fire (Hell) and we are the people of Paradise," because such a method causes discord (fitnah). Furthermore, how do we know who will have a good end? Allah is more knowledgeable; if He wills, He forgives or He punishes.

Messages

1- We should not be arrogant about our faith. «ربّكم اعلم بكم»

2- Divine actions are based on His knowledge. «اعلم بكم، یرحمكم، یعذّبكم»

3- Allah's knowledge, His mercy, and His wrath are among the aspects of Allah's Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «ربّكم، یرحمكم، یعذّبكم»

4- Mention of mercy and compassion precedes wrath and punishment. «یرحمكم، یعذّبكم»

5- Man must be between fear (khawf) and hope (rajā'). «یرحمكم، یعذّبكم»

6- People are free in choosing their belief; even the Prophets are not commissioned to compel people to believe. «ما ارسلناك علیهم وكیلاً»

7- The preacher of religion should not consider himself a guardian (wakīl) or supervisor over the people. «وكیلاً»

Ayah

وَرَبُّكَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ وَلَقَدْ فَضَّلْنَا بَعْضَ ٱلنَّبِيِّـۧنَ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ ۖ وَءَاتَيْنَا دَاوُۥدَ زَبُورًا (٥٥)
Your Lord knows best whoever is in the heavens and the earth. Certainly, We gave some prophets an advantage over the others, and We gave David the Psalms. (55)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, Allah's knowledge of human beings was discussed; here, His knowledge of all celestial and terrestrial beings is stated. It is mentioned in the narrations (Ahadith) that the Prophets numbered one hundred and twenty-four thousand; some of them were sent to all people and possessed a heavenly book, while others were assigned to a specific region or tribe and carried out their duties under the command of a greater Prophet.

Messages

1- The Prophets are under the special attention of Allah. «ربّك»

2- The universe is in the presence of Allah, and He is aware of everything. «ربّك اعلم»

3- Conscious beings also exist in the heavens. «من فى السموات»

4- There is also a hierarchy and superiority among the Prophets. Even the best individuals and the pious (Muttaqin) are not equal. «فضّلنا»

5- Allah's differentiation and granting of superiority are based on His comprehensive knowledge of everything and everyone. «ربّك اعلم، فضّلنا»

6- A heavenly book is one of the signs of a Prophet's superiority. «فضّلنا، آتینا داود زبوراً»

7- Cultural superiority is the most fundamental superiority, not wealth, status, or lifespan. «فضّلنا، زبوراً»

Ayah

قُلِ ٱدْعُوا۟ ٱلَّذِينَ زَعَمْتُم مِّن دُونِهِۦ فَلَا يَمْلِكُونَ كَشْفَ ٱلضُّرِّ عَنكُمْ وَلَا تَحْوِيلًا (٥٦)
Say, ‘Invoke those whom you claim [to be gods] besides Him. They have no power to remove your distress, nor to bring about any change [in your state]. (56)

Messages

1- Seeking help from other than Allah and placing hope in them is nothing more than an illusion. «زعمتم»

2- In the method of propagation (Da'wah), while the truth must be stated, people must be left to their own choice so that they may voluntarily reject or accept it. «قل ادعوا الّذین زعمتم... لایملكون كشف الضّر»

3- The motivation for worship is seeking refuge in a power, and idols do not possess this ability. «فلا یملكون كشف الضّر»

4- Other than Allah can neither remove danger, nor transfer it to another, nor change it, nor alleviate it. «كشف الضّر، تحویلاً» (The belief in the intercession (Shafa'ah) of Allah's chosen servants for the removal of difficulties and punishment is by the permission of Allah and is a separate matter.)

Ayah

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ يَبْتَغُونَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِمُ ٱلْوَسِيلَةَ أَيُّهُمْ أَقْرَبُ وَيَرْجُونَ رَحْمَتَهُۥ وَيَخَافُونَ عَذَابَهُۥٓ ۚ إِنَّ عَذَابَ رَبِّكَ كَانَ مَحْذُورًا (٥۷)
They [themselves] are the ones who supplicate, seeking a recourse to their Lord, whoever is nearer [to Him], expecting His mercy and fearing His punishment.’ Indeed your Lord’s punishment is a thing to beware of. (57)

Key Points

The Ayah has also been translated thus: The prophets who invite people to the truth, they themselves also (in their spiritual journey) seek a means (of approach) toward their Lord—a means that brings them closer to Allah more and faster. Or, the closer a prophet is (to Allah), the more He seeks Allah. Many narrations under this Ayah state that the two scales of fear and hope in a person must be balanced, otherwise, he will either become despairing or arrogant.

Messages

1- Fear and hope are signs of dependence and weakness. Therefore, how can you make someone a means (of approach) when he himself seeks a means toward Him? «یبتغون الى ربّهم الوسیلة»

2- For proximity to Allah, there are paths and means (of approach). «یبتغون الى ربّهم الوسیلة»

3- Seeking a means (of approach) and requesting intercession should not make a person oblivious to the punishment. «یبتغون... یخافون عذابه»

4- In drawing near to Allah, precedence and competition are valuable. «أیّهم أقرب» (Whoever is closer to Allah, his effort for tawassul [seeking means] is greater.)

5- The best means is that which brings a person closer to Allah. «أیّهم أقرب»

6- Divine Mercy precedes His Wrath. (Hope for mercy is mentioned before fear of punishment.) «یرجون... یخافون»

7- Punishment is among the affairs of Allah's Lordship (Rububiyyah); let us not take it lightly. «عذاب ربّك... محذوراً...»

Ayah

وَإِن مِّن قَرْيَةٍ إِلَّا نَحْنُ مُهْلِكُوهَا قَبْلَ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ أَوْ مُعَذِّبُوهَا عَذَابًا شَدِيدًا ۚ كَانَ ذَٰلِكَ فِى ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ مَسْطُورًا (٥۸)
There is not a town but We will destroy it before the Day of Resurrection, or punish it with a severe punishment. That has been written in the Book. (58)

Key Points

The term «قَریة» (Qaryah) refers to a place where people gather and is inhabited, whether it is a city or a village. The meaning of «الكتاب» (Al-Kitab) is either the Preserved Tablet (Lawh Mahfuz) or the Qur'an, in which the causes for the downfall and destruction of nations are explained.

Messages

1- Life is not permanent or lasting for anyone. «و اِن من قریة...»

2- The cessation of life on earth is not an accident, but is based on a law that Allah has predetermined and recorded. «فى الكتاب مسطوراً»

Ayah

وَمَا مَنَعَنَآ أَن نُّرْسِلَ بِٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ إِلَّآ أَن كَذَّبَ بِهَا ٱلْأَوَّلُونَ ۚ وَءَاتَيْنَا ثَمُودَ ٱلنَّاقَةَ مُبْصِرَةً فَظَلَمُوا۟ بِهَا ۚ وَمَا نُرْسِلُ بِٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ إِلَّا تَخْوِيفًا (٥۹)
Nothing keeps Us from sending signs except that the former peoples denied them. We gave Thamud the she-camel as an eye-opener, but they wronged her. We do not send the signs except as warning. (59)

Key Points

The fault-finding disbelievers of Mecca requested multiple miracles from the Prophet, including asking him to turn some of the mountains of Mecca (Mount Safa) into gold, or to move the mountains so that land for agriculture could be created. Allah says: Based on historical experience, stubborn disbelievers will not believe even after seeing these signs, and the system of creation is not subject to the whims of stubborn individuals. And if a miracle is presented at the suggestion of the people and they disbelieve in it, their worldly punishment is certain. The camel is an animal, but Allah says concerning the camel of Salih: «ناقة اللّه» (the She-Camel of Allah), because whatever is attributed to God possesses sanctity. Even the name of Abu Lahab, because it is in the Quran, cannot be touched without wudu (ablution).

Messages

1- Despite having various miracles, the Prophets were constantly afflicted by stubborn individuals. «كذّب بها الأوّلون»

2- Allah is capable of everything, but His action is based on wisdom, not on satisfying people's whims. «ما منعنا...»

3- Insulting sacred things and denying miracles leads to divine wrath and punishment. «آتینا... فظلموا بها»

4- Whatever is from Allah and has a divine color is separate from ordinary matters. «آتینا... فظلموا بها»

5- Miracles are a means of recognition, insight, and warning for people. «مبصرةً، تخویفاً»

Ayah

وَإِذْ قُلْنَا لَكَ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ أَحَاطَ بِٱلنَّاسِ ۚ وَمَا جَعَلْنَا ٱلرُّءْيَا ٱلَّتِىٓ أَرَيْنَـٰكَ إِلَّا فِتْنَةً لِّلنَّاسِ وَٱلشَّجَرَةَ ٱلْمَلْعُونَةَ فِى ٱلْقُرْءَانِ ۚ وَنُخَوِّفُهُمْ فَمَا يَزِيدُهُمْ إِلَّا طُغْيَـٰنًا كَبِيرًا (٦٠)
When We said to you, ‘Indeed your Lord encircles those people,’ We did not appoint the vision that We showed you except as a tribulation for the people and the tree cursed in the Quran. We warn them, but it only increases them in their outrageous rebellion. (60)

Key Points

The previous Ayah spoke of the killing of Salih's she-camel, and this Ayah speaks of the cursed tree (Shajarah al-Mal'unah), which were the killers of the Prophet's Household (Ahl al-Bayt) علیهم السلام. We read in narrations that the Ahl al-Bayt said: We are no less than Salih's she-camel; disrespect toward us leads to destruction. (130)

Several dreams of the Prophet of Islam have been mentioned in the Quran: One dream was on the eve of the Battle of Badr, where Allah showed the enemies as few in the Prophet's eyes, so that the Muslims would not lose heart. (131) Another was the dream of entering the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram) victoriously. (132) And one is the dream discussed in this Ayah. The previous two dreams occurred after the Hijra (migration) and in Medina, but this dream occurred in Mecca. Of course, some have related this vision (ru'ya) to the Mi'raj (Ascension), whereas the Mi'raj occurred while awake, and ru'ya primarily means seeing in a dream.

That dream and the cursed tree are one, because they have a single outcome, which is being a source of trial (fitnah) for the people. Allah showed this cursed tree and their actions to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله in a dream and informed Him that this would be the cause of trial for His nation, and He comforted the Prophet with the phrase, "إِنَّ رَبَّكَ أَحَاطَ بِالنَّاسِ" (Indeed, your Lord encompasses humanity). Shajarah (tree) means both a tree and any root that has branches and offshoots. Thus, a tribe is also called a shajarah. The Prophet also said: "Ali and I are from one tree" (أَنَا وَ عَلِيٌّ مِنْ شَجَرَةٍ وَاحِدَةٍ). (133) The lineage and ancestry are also called a shajarah-namah (genealogy/family tree). Therefore, the cursed tree refers to a deeply rooted people who are cursed.

At the end of this Ayah, it is stated that the divine warnings regarding the cursed tree result in nothing but the increase of their great transgression (tughyan kabir). And tughyan kabir (great transgression) is used only once, in this very Ayah. Thus, one must look in the Quran for a cursed tree, a people or tribe who are outwardly Muslim but inwardly possess hypocrisy and cause trials (fitnah) for the people.

Multiple entities are cursed in the Quran, such as: Iblis (Satan), the Jews, the hypocrites (munafiqun), the polytheists (mushrikun), scholars who concealed the truth, and those who harmed the Prophet. However, only the hypocrites are among the Muslims and cause trials for them, because Iblis, the People of the Book, and the polytheists have clear identities, but the hypocrites, who are outwardly Muslim, have constantly been agents of trial (fitnah). (134)

Some have assumed that the cursed tree is the "Tree of Zaqqum," which is a means of divine punishment. But other things have also been means of divine punishment, and this does not indicate that the means itself is cursed, such as the Nile River which drowned Pharaoh, or the angels who brought down punishment upon nations, or the believers who were the arm of Allah in punishing and destroying the disbelievers in battles. "قَاتِلُوهُمْ يُعَذِّبْهُمُ اللَّهُ بِأَيْدِيكُمْ" (Fight them; Allah will punish them by your hands). (135)

Allamah Tabataba'i قدس سره, in the narrative discussion of Surah Al-Qadr, quotes prominent figures of the Ahl al-Sunnah, such as Khatib al-Baghdadi, Tirmidhi, Ibn Jarir, Tabarani, Bayhaqi, and Ibn Mardawayh, and from Shi'a scholars, such as Kulayni, the author of Kitab al-Kafi, that the Noble Prophet saw monkeys climbing His pulpit in a dream and became very grieved. Gabriel descended. The Prophet related His dream to him. Gabriel ascended to the heaven, and when he returned, he brought these verses: "أَفَرَأَيْتَ إِنْ مَتَّعْنَاهُمْ سِنِينَ . ثُمَّ جَاءَهُمْ مَا كَانُوا يُوعَدُونَ . مَا أَغْنَى عَنْهُمْ مَا كَانُوا يُمَتَّعُونَ" (Have you considered if We should let them enjoy themselves for years? Then there comes to them that which they were promised. What they were enjoying will not avail them). (136) (Do you not see that) if We allow them to enjoy themselves for years, then the punishment that they were promised comes to them, their worldly enjoyments will not benefit them. This vision also caused the revelation of Surah Al-Qadr, to console the Prophet that even if the Bani Umayyah rule for a thousand months, in return, We have given you the Night of Decree (Laylat al-Qadr), which is better than a thousand months. The issue of seeing monkeys who seized power and climbed the Prophet's pulpit has also been narrated from Imam Baqir and Imam Sadiq علیهما السلام. (137) Both Shi'a and Sunni commentators consider the meaning of the cursed tree to be the "Bani Umayyah."

Imam Sajjad علیه السلام said: Gabriel applied the monkeys to the Bani Umayyah. The Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله asked: Will this event occur during my time? He replied: No, it will occur about forty years after your migration (Hijra). (138) Among the Bani Umayyah, the one who transgressed the most and caused the tragedy of Karbala was "Yazid," and this catastrophe was the greatest transgression in history.

130) Bihar, Vol. 28, p. 206 and Vol. 50, p. 192. 131) Al-Anfal, 43. 132) Al-Fath, 27. 133) Bihar, Vol. 38, p. 309. 134) The deepest view regarding this Ayah, accompanied by narrations, is found in Tafsir Al-Mizan. 135) At-Tawbah, 14. 136) Ash-Shu'ara, 205 - 207. 137) Tafsir Tibyan. 138) Tafsir Lahiji.

Messages

1- Allah sometimes inspires certain truths to the Prophet and others through dreams. "أَرَيْنَاكَ" (We showed you).

2- Every event, even the interpretation of a dream, can become a means of testing people. "الرُّؤْيَا... فِتْنَةً" (The vision... a trial).

3- Tribes and groups that become agents of deviation are the "cursed tree" (Shajarah al-Mal'unah). "فِتْنَةً لِلنَّاسِ وَ الشَّجَرَةَ الْمَلْعُونَةَ" (A trial for the people and the cursed tree).

4- Warning people, even cursed tribes and dynasties, is among the divine traditions. "نُخَوِّفُهُمْ" (We warn them).

5- Warnings have no effect on the hearts of stubborn bigots. (The iron nail does not penetrate the stone.) "فَمَا يَزِيدُهُمْ إِلَّا طُغْيَانًا كَبِيرًا" (But it increases them only in great transgression).

Ayah

وَإِذْ قُلْنَا لِلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ ٱسْجُدُوا۟ لِـَٔادَمَ فَسَجَدُوٓا۟ إِلَّآ إِبْلِيسَ قَالَ ءَأَسْجُدُ لِمَنْ خَلَقْتَ طِينًا (٦۱)
When We said to the angels, ‘Prostrate before Adam,’ they [all] prostrated, but not Iblis: he said, ‘Shall I prostrate before someone whom You have created from clay?’ (61)

Key Points

The issue of the prostration (sujud) of the angels and the disobedience of Iblis has been mentioned repeatedly in the Quran. Iblis is from the Jinn, «كان من الجنّ»(139) and he has armies, «وجنود ابلیس اجمعون»(140) and his army consists of foot soldiers and cavalry, «و اَجلِب علیهم بخَیلك و رَجِلِك»(141) and the cause of his deviation and refusal to prostrate was the comparison (qiyas) between earth and fire. «خلقتنى من نارٍ و خلقته من طین»(142)

139) كهف، 50. 140) شعراء، 95. 141) اسراء، 64. 142) اعراف، 12.

Messages

1- Prostration (sujud) for other than Allah, but by the command of Allah, is permissible. «قلنا...اُسجدوا لآدم»

2- Man is the choicest of creation, because the infallible angels prostrated before him. «اُسجدوا لآدم فسجدوا»

3- The angels are submissive to the command of Allah. «اُسجدوا... فسجدوا»

4- Belief in imaginary superiorities is satanic, because Satan believed in the superiority of his own kind. «ءاسجد لمن خلقت طیناً»

5- Objecting to the wisdom, justice, and command of Allah is worse than refusing to prostrate. «ءاسجد...»

6- Iblis is a creature capable of evaluation and possessing free will. «الاّ ابلیس ءاسجد...»

7- Ijtihad (independent reasoning) in opposition to a clear text (nass) is forbidden. (Iblis performed ijtihad against the clear text of Allah.) «قلنا... اُسجدوا... ءاسجد لمن خلقت طیناً»

8- The root of disobedience is sometimes self-superiority. «ءاسجد لمن خلقت طیناً»

9- The envy of Iblis blinded his truth-seeing eye; he saw Adam's composition of earth and clay, but he did not see the Divine Spirit «نفخت فیه من روحى»(143) within him. «ءاسجد... طیناً»

10- Iblis considers material elements to be the criterion of value. «طیناً»143) حجر، 29.

Ayah

قَالَ أَرَءَيْتَكَ هَـٰذَا ٱلَّذِى كَرَّمْتَ عَلَىَّ لَئِنْ أَخَّرْتَنِ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ لَأَحْتَنِكَنَّ ذُرِّيَّتَهُۥٓ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا (٦۲)
Said he, ‘Do You see this one whom You have honoured above me? If You respite me until the Day of Resurrection, I will surely lay my yoke on his progeny, [all] except a few.’ (62)

Key Points

| «أرأیت» means «اخْبرنى» (inform me), meaning "tell me." «اَحتنكنّ» (I shall surely bridle) derives from «حَنك» (hanak), which is a rope placed around an animal's neck to lead it. «اِحتناك» also means to uproot and to gain complete dominance. (144)

144) Mufradāt al-Rāghib.

Messages

1- Self-worship leads man to rebellion against Allah. «هذا الّذى كرّمت علىّ»

2- Detaching oneself from status and position is one of the most difficult tests. «كرّمتَ علىّ» (145)

5- Iblīs (Satan) was both aware of the Day of Resurrection and believed in it. «اخّرتن الى یوم القیامة»

3- Envy gradually turns into malice and action against others. «لئن اخّرتن... لاحتنكنّ ذرّیته»

4- Some envies and malice encompass future generations as well. «ذرّیته»

6- Man is free and has the right to choose in the face of Satan's temptations. «الاّ قلیلاً»

7- Some people are safe from the danger of Iblīs's dominance. «الاّ قلیلاً»

145) It is stated in the words of the great ones: «آخر ما یخرج من رؤوس الصدّیقین حُبّ الرئاسة» (The last thing that leaves the heads of the truthful ones is the love of leadership/status). The last thing a person detaches himself from in self-purification is status and position.

Ayah

قَالَ ٱذْهَبْ فَمَن تَبِعَكَ مِنْهُمْ فَإِنَّ جَهَنَّمَ جَزَآؤُكُمْ جَزَآءً مَّوْفُورًا (٦۳)
Said He, ‘Begone! Whoever of them follows you, indeed the hell shall be your requital, an ample reward. (63)

Messages

1- Allah accepted Iblis's request for respite. «اذهب»

2- Man is free to choose the path of God or follow Satan. «فمن تبعك»

3- The abode of Iblis and his followers is Hell. «جزائكم جهنّم»

4- Divine punishment is complete; sinners taste the punishment for their own sins and also receive the full punishment for misleading others. «موفورا»

Ayah

وَٱسْتَفْزِزْ مَنِ ٱسْتَطَعْتَ مِنْهُم بِصَوْتِكَ وَأَجْلِبْ عَلَيْهِم بِخَيْلِكَ وَرَجِلِكَ وَشَارِكْهُمْ فِى ٱلْأَمْوَٰلِ وَٱلْأَوْلَـٰدِ وَعِدْهُمْ ۚ وَمَا يَعِدُهُمُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ إِلَّا غُرُورًا (٦٤)
Instigate whomever of them you can with your voice; and rally against them your cavalry and your infantry, and share with them in wealth and children, and make promises to them!’ But Satan promises them nothing but delusion. (64)

Key Points

Istifzāz (اِستفزاز) means to cause slipping (or displacement) with speed and dexterity, and to push. Iblis (Satan) does not approach man through a single path to deceive him; rather, he besieges man with propaganda, promises, desires, whispers (temptations), companionship and partnership, on foot and mounted, and among these, a number of people join him. We read in narrations: Whoever does not care what he says or what is said about him, and engages in any action, and openly commits sin or backbiting, is a partner of Satan. (146) Likewise, unlawful sustenance (luqmah-ye haram), consuming the unlawful (haram-khwari), adultery (zina), and producing a corrupt and deviant generation (147) are instances of Satan's partnership. And whoever holds enmity toward the Ahl al-Bayt (the Household) of the Prophet علیهم السلام, Satan has become a partner in his offspring. (148) Unhealthy production and consumption, hoarding wealth (kanz-anduzi), investment by foreign companies and factories, and the establishment of colonial scientific, cultural, artistic centers, and international competitions are a type of partnership with Satan.

146) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 147) Bihar, Vol. 101, p. 136. 148) Kafi, Vol. 5, p. 502.

Messages

1- The power of Iblis is limited. «مَن استطعت»

2- Corrupt propaganda plays a role in the deviation of people. «بصوتك»

3- The enemy's propaganda and cultural invasion precedes the military invasion. Sawt (voice) comes before Khayl (cavalry). «بصوتك، اجلب علیهم بخیلك»

Satan first removes man's monotheistic motivation through propaganda, and then attacks him with his weapons and armies.

4- Iblis himself is a source of misguidance, «استفزز», and so are his forces. «خیلك»

5- Satan's method is to lie in wait, attract, and seize. «اجلب»

6- Iblis's partnership in man's wealth and children begins from its inception and continues through unlawful and doubtful sustenance (luqmah-ye haram wa mushtabah) and adultery (zina). «شاركهم»

7- Not every freedom and choice is a sign of Divine grace; sometimes, the power to maneuver is a type of Divine wrath. «استفزز، اجلب، شاركهم، و عِدهم»

8- The ground for Satan's infiltration is human desires, which are strengthened by Satan's illusory promises. «عِدهم و مایعدهم الشیطان الاّ غرورا»

9- In addition to deception, Satan justifies sin, gives false promises of intercession (shafa'ah), and delays repentance. «و ما یعدهم الشیطان الاّ غرورا»

Ayah

إِنَّ عِبَادِى لَيْسَ لَكَ عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطَـٰنٌ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِرَبِّكَ وَكِيلًا (٦٥)
‘As for My servants, you shall have no authority over them.’ And your Lord suffices as trustee. (65)

Key Points

Although all people are servants of Allah, Allah attributes some to Himself out of respect and honor. ("عبادى" - My servants), just as such an attribution is made regarding objects and inanimate things. ("بیتى" - My House). The servants of Allah are introduced in another Ayah with the characteristics of faith (Iman) and reliance (Tawakkul), over whom Satan has no authority. "اِنّه لیس له سلطانٌ على الّذین آمنوا و على ربّهم یتوكّلون" (149) Of course, Satan whispers (tempts) and makes contact, but they become mindful and resist. "انّ الّذین اتّقوا اذا مسّهم طائف من الشیطان تذكّروا" (150) It is stated in a Hadith: The Adhan (call to prayer) and the Salat (prayer) are factors that repel Satan. (151)

149) An-Nahl, 99. 150) Al-A'raf, 201. 151) Bihar, Vol. 63, p. 268 and Vol. 53, p. 182.

Messages

1- The true servants of Allah are few. "اِنّ عبادى لیس لك علیهم سلطان" We read in Ayah 62 that Iblis said: "لاحتنكنّ ذریّته الاّ قلیلا" (I will surely lead his descendants astray, except for a few). I will mislead all people except a small number.

2- Servitude to Allah insures a person against the propaganda and campaigns of Iblis. "اِنّ عبادى لیس لك علیهم سلطان" Yes, whoever connects himself to the infinite Power becomes impenetrable.

3- Man is free and can resist the attack of Iblis by taking refuge in faith and servitude (to Allah). "عبادى لیس لك علیهم سلطان"

4- Whoever becomes a servant of Allah, Allah becomes his Trustee (Wakil), Guardian (Hafiz), and Guarantor. "عبادى... كفى بربّك وكیلاً"

5- The Prophet is under special Divine protection and is safe from the influence of Satan. "انّ عبادى... كفى بربّك وكیلاً"

Ayah

رَّبُّكُمُ ٱلَّذِى يُزْجِى لَكُمُ ٱلْفُلْكَ فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ لِتَبْتَغُوا۟ مِن فَضْلِهِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ بِكُمْ رَحِيمًا (٦٦)
Your Lord is He who drives for you the ships in the sea that you may seek of His bounty. Indeed, He is most merciful to you. (66)

Key Points

Maritime routes, due to their affordability, permanence, and universality, play a good role in the transportation of people and goods, and the blessings of shipping in transporting cargo and passengers, and in fishing, are abundant. The sea's contribution to oxygen production, the generation of vapor, clouds, and rain, the cultivation of fish and nutrition, and its mineral, plant, and animal wonders is immense. In the Du'a Jawshan Kabir, we read: «یا مَن فى البَحر عجائبه» (O He whose wonders are in the sea).

Messages

1- Providing for the material needs of humanity is a sign of Allah's power to suffice mankind. «كفى بربّك وكیلاً ربّكم الّذى...»

2- Resources and grace are from God, while work and effort are from us. «یُزجى، لتبتغوا من فضله»

3- Whatever we gain from the blessings of the sea is His grace. «من فضله»

4- The Lordship (Rububiyyah) of Allah is accompanied by His perpetual mercy. «ربّكم... كان بكم رحیماً»

Ayah

وَإِذَا مَسَّكُمُ ٱلضُّرُّ فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ ضَلَّ مَن تَدْعُونَ إِلَّآ إِيَّاهُ ۖ فَلَمَّا نَجَّىٰكُمْ إِلَى ٱلْبَرِّ أَعْرَضْتُمْ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ كَفُورًا (٦۷)
When distress befalls you at sea, those whom you invoke besides Him are forsaken. But when He delivers you to land, you are disregardful [of Him]. Man is very ungrateful. (67)

Key Points

One of the proofs for the innate nature (Fitrah) of Monotheism (Tawhid) is that when a person is in a state of helplessness and despair regarding all material means, he turns his attention toward a single unseen source that can save him. An atheist (a person who denies God) asked Imam Sadiq عليه السلام for proof of God's existence. The Imam asked: "Have you ever been aboard a ship that met with disaster?" He replied: "Yes, once during a sea voyage, our ship broke apart, and I clung to a plank of wood." The Imam asked: "At that moment, was your heart turned toward a power that could save you?" He replied: "Yes." He said: "That unseen power is Allah."

Messages

  1. Seasonal or situational faith and repentance hold no value. «مسّكم الضرّ...»

  2. In the whirlpool of danger, man becomes a monotheist, «الاّ ایاه» (except Him), and the inadequacy of all apparent and imagined means becomes clear. «ضلّ مَن تدعون»

  3. Distancing oneself from material means is a factor in better understanding the truths. «مسّكم الضرّ فى البحر ضلّ مَن تدعون الاّ ایّاه»

  4. Except for Allah, every object of worship vanishes and is destroyed. «ضَلّ مَن تدعون الاّ ایّاه»

  5. Sincere supplication is answered. «الاّ ایّاه فلمّا نجّاكم»

  6. Comfort is a cause of heedlessness. «نجّاكم الى البَرّ اعرضتم»

  7. The attractions of the world are so captivating that man forgets everything just hours after being saved from destruction. «فلمّا نجّاكم... اعرضتم»

  8. Forgetting Allah after being saved is a clear manifestation of ingratitude (kufrān). «و كان الانسان كفوراً»

Ayah

أَفَأَمِنتُمْ أَن يَخْسِفَ بِكُمْ جَانِبَ ٱلْبَرِّ أَوْ يُرْسِلَ عَلَيْكُمْ حَاصِبًا ثُمَّ لَا تَجِدُوا۟ لَكُمْ وَكِيلًا (٦۸)
Do you feel secure that He will not make the coastland swallow you, or He will not unleash upon you a rain of stones? Then you will not find any defender for yourselves. (68)

Key Points

The destruction of previous nations occurred in various ways; Allah caused some transgressors and enemies to be swallowed by the earth, He drowned some in the sea, and He destroyed some by stoning and with heavenly thunderbolts. Thus, the Hand of Allah is open for the destruction of the disbelievers. If today He delivers you safely from the sea to the shore, do not imagine that there is no way to punish you, or that you will not be afflicted by torment again!

Messages

1- We should not be arrogant regarding divine blessings. «نجّاكم... افأمنتم» Salvation from one incident is not a sign of eternal salvation; perhaps we will suffer calamity at another time or in another place.

2- Feeling secure from punishment is the groundwork for negligence and transgression. «أفامنتم»

3- Man, wherever and whenever he may be, is within the Hand of Allah's power, and the land and the sea make no difference to Allah's wrath. «یخسف بكم جانب البَرّ»

4- Against the wrath of Allah, there is no power or support. «ثمّ لاتجدوا لكم وكیلاً»

Ayah

أَمْ أَمِنتُمْ أَن يُعِيدَكُمْ فِيهِ تَارَةً أُخْرَىٰ فَيُرْسِلَ عَلَيْكُمْ قَاصِفًا مِّنَ ٱلرِّيحِ فَيُغْرِقَكُم بِمَا كَفَرْتُمْ ۙ ثُمَّ لَا تَجِدُوا۟ لَكُمْ عَلَيْنَا بِهِۦ تَبِيعًا (٦۹)
Do you feel secure that He will not send you back into it another time and unleash against you a shattering gale and drown you because of your unfaith? Then you will not find for yourselves any redresser against Us. (69)

Key Points

«حاصِب» (Hāsib) refers to a storm on land, and «قاصِف» (Qāsif) refers to a storm at sea. «تَبیع» (Tabī‘), derived from the word «تَبَع» (taba‘), refers to someone who pursues blood money, seeks revenge, and demands justice.

Messages

1- We should not be deceived by temporary periods of calm; danger is always lurking. «أمنتم أن یعیدكم فیه تارةًاخرى» The danger is not permanently removed just because we were saved from one peril.

2- The punishment for some ingratitude occurs in this world. «فیُغرقكم بما كفرتم»

3- Neglecting Allah after being saved is an example of disbelief and ingratitude. «بما كفرتم»

4- The cause of human destruction and misery is his inclination towards disbelief and his own actions. «فیُغرقكم بما كفرتم»

5- No power can stand against Allah, and Allah is not accountable to anyone. «لا تجدوا لكم علینا تبیعاً»

Ayah

۞ وَلَقَدْ كَرَّمْنَا بَنِىٓ ءَادَمَ وَحَمَلْنَـٰهُمْ فِى ٱلْبَرِّ وَٱلْبَحْرِ وَرَزَقْنَـٰهُم مِّنَ ٱلطَّيِّبَـٰتِ وَفَضَّلْنَـٰهُمْ عَلَىٰ كَثِيرٍ مِّمَّنْ خَلَقْنَا تَفْضِيلًا (۷٠)
Certainly We have honoured the Children of Adam, and carried them over land and sea, and provided them with all the good things, and preferred them with a complete preference over many of those We have created. (70)

Key Points

Dignity (Karāmah) is sometimes spiritual and acquired, and is with Allah, like the dignity specific to the people of piety. «إِنَّ أَكْرَمَكُمْ عِندَ اللَّهِ أَتْقَاكُمْ» (152) And sometimes dignity is inherent in creation, similar to «أَحْسَنِ تَقْوِيمٍ» (153) which is mentioned regarding the creation of man. The intent of «كَرَّمْنَا» in this Ayah is likely the second aspect. Human dignity lies both in creation, intelligence, intellect, and potential, and in possessing divine law, infallible leadership, and being the object of prostration for the angels.

Man is superior to the angel, because: A: Man is the object of prostration for the angels. B: The angel is pure intellect and lacks desire (lust), and therefore its perfection is not equal to the value of human perfection. Whoever makes his intellect overcome his desire is superior to the angel. C: On the night of the Ascension (Mi'raj), Gabriel said to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: Be the Imam so that I may follow you, because Allah has granted you superiority over us. (154)

Although Allah granted humanity superiority over all creatures, even the angels, «فَضَّلْنَاهُمْ عَلَى كَثِيرٍ مِّمَّنْ خَلَقْنَا», yet man's poor choice and undesirable actions bring him down to the lowest degree, «أَسْفَلَ سَافِلِينَ» (155), and to the level of animals and lower than that. «كَمَثَلِ الْحِمَارِ» (156), «كَمَثَلِ الْكَلْبِ» (157), «كَالْأَنْعَامِ بَلْ هُمْ أَضَلُّ» (158), «كَالْحِجَارَةِ أَوْ أَشَدُّ...» (159)

In the Ayah, both dignity (karāmah) and virtue (faḍīlah) are mentioned for man. «كَرَّمْنَا», «فَضَّلْنَا». Perhaps the difference between the two consists of: A: Dignity is a distinction that others do not possess, but virtue is a distinction that others may also possess. B: Dignity refers to the God-given blessings inherent in man's existence, without effort. But virtue refers to the blessings attained through man's own effort combined with Divine success (Tawfīq). C: Dignity relates to material blessings, but virtue relates to spiritual blessings.

Travel is one of the necessities of human life, for fulfilling needs and gaining experience, the means of which Allah has placed at man's disposal on land and sea, and has counted it as one of His blessings. According to some commentators, "transportation on land and sea" refers to the subjugation of all terrestrial and marine forces by man, not just riding ships, donkeys, and the like!

Tayyibāt (good things) have categories: good life (Ḥayāt Ṭayyibah), good progeny (Dhurriyyah Ṭayyibah), and good sustenance (Rizq Ṭayyib). And Imam Baqir عليه السلام says: Good sustenance (Rizq Ṭayyib) is beneficial knowledge. (160)

152) Hujurat, 13. 153) Tin, 4. 154) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 155) Tin, 5. 156) Jumu'ah, 5. 157) A'raf, 176. 158) A'raf, 179. 159) Baqarah, 74. 160) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā'iq.

Messages

1- Man's attention to his own status and dignity is a cause for gratitude (Shukr) and distance from ingratitude (Kufrān). «بِمَا كَفَرْتُمْ... وَلَقَدْ كَرَّمْنَا»

2- Not only Prophet Adam, but the entire human race is the object of dignity, superiority, and honor. «كَرَّمْنَا بَنِي آدَمَ»

3- The difference between man and other living creatures, and his superiority, is very great. «لَقَدْ كَرَّمْنَا... فَضَّلْنَاهُمْ... تَفْضِيلًا»

4- The foundations for human growth and perfection come from Allah. «كَرَّمْنَا, حَمَلْنَا, رَزَقْنَا, فَضَّلْنَا, خَلَقْنَا»

Ayah

يَوْمَ نَدْعُوا۟ كُلَّ أُنَاسٍۭ بِإِمَـٰمِهِمْ ۖ فَمَنْ أُوتِىَ كِتَـٰبَهُۥ بِيَمِينِهِۦ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ يَقْرَءُونَ كِتَـٰبَهُمْ وَلَا يُظْلَمُونَ فَتِيلًا (۷۱)
The day We shall summon every group of people along with their imam, then whoever is given his book in his right hand—they will read it, and they will not be wronged so much as a single date-thread. (71)

Key Points

"Fatīl" (فَتیل) is the thin thread in the groove of a date stone, an allusion to something very small, minor, and insignificant. The Quran mentions two types of leaders (Imams): one is the Imam of light and guidance, "ائمّةً یهدون بأمرنا" (161), and the other is the Imam of fire and misguidance, "ائمّةً یدعون الى النّار" (162). The second group compels people to obey them through force, threat, temptation, and humiliation. Abu Basir said to Imam Sadiq عليه السلام: "أشهد أنّك اِمامى" (I bear witness that you are my Imam). The Imam said: On the Day of Resurrection, every group will be resurrected with their Imam; sun worshippers with the sun, and moon worshippers with the moon. (163)

161) Al-Anbiya, 73. 162) Al-Qasas, 41. 163) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Let us not forget the Day of Resurrection. "یوم ندعوا"

2- The categorization of people on the Day of Resurrection will be based on their leaders. "كلّ اُناس باِمامهم"

3- Man inevitably chooses a role model and a leader for himself. "كلّ اُناس بامامهم"

4- A Muslim should not be isolated or indifferent regarding issues of leadership. "ندعوا كلّ اُناسٍ بامامهم" because he will be resurrected alongside that leader on the Day of Resurrection.

5- The effects of choosing a leader and obeying him continue until the Day of Resurrection. "ندعوا كلّ اُناسٍ بامامهم"

6- The Day of Resurrection is not only a court for individuals but also the great tribunal for communities, nations, parties, schools of thought, and governments. "ندعوا كلّ اُناسٍ بامامهم"

7- Imamat (leadership) and Wilayah (guardianship) are central to life, not merely a theological topic on the periphery of life. The leader paves the way for the people's happiness or misery. "كلّ اُناس باِمامهم"

8- On the Day of Resurrection, the righteous will be joyful upon reading their record of deeds. "یقرئون كتابهم" and they will call out to one another, saying, "Come and read my record!" "هاؤم اقروا كتابیه" (164)

9- Man will receive his performance record in written form. "كتابه"

10- The trial of humans on the Day of Resurrection is based on written evidence and documents. "و لایُظلَمون فَتیلا"

11- Punishment and reward on the Day of Resurrection are 100% just. "ولا یُظلَمون فتیلاً" 164) Al-Haqqah, 19.

Ayah

وَمَن كَانَ فِى هَـٰذِهِۦٓ أَعْمَىٰ فَهُوَ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ أَعْمَىٰ وَأَضَلُّ سَبِيلًا (۷۲)
But whoever has been blind in this [world], will be blind in the Hereafter, and [even] more astray from the [right] way. (72)

Key Points

In the Hereafter (Qiyamah), besides the scenes where people are resurrected with sealed lips and mute, some will also be blind. That blindness (in the Hereafter) is rooted in the spiritual blindness (blindness of the heart) of this world. It is mentioned in narrations: Whoever is obligated to perform Hajj but does not go, or reads the Qur'an but does not act upon it, will be resurrected blind. (165)

Blindness of the heart is worse than blindness of the eye, just as Imam Ali عليه السلام says: «شرّ العمى عمى القلب» (The worst blindness is the blindness of the heart). And He also said: «أشدّ العمى مَن عمى عن فضلنا» (The worst and most severe blindness is the blindness of one who cannot see our virtues). (166)

Blindness in the Hereafter is also mentioned in other verses, including: «نحشرهم یوم القیامة على وجوههم عمیاً» (167). Likewise, Ayah 125 of Surah Taha attributes being resurrected blind in the Hereafter to spiritual blindness in this world, forgetting the divine signs (Ayat), and turning away from the truth (Haqq).

Question: In some verses, it is stated that sinners will be told: Read your book (record of deeds), «اقرء كتابك». How can these verses be reconciled with their blindness in the Hereafter?

Answer: Blindness occurs in one of the stations (Mawāqif) of the Hereafter; in another station, they will become sighted and will clearly see the realities they failed to recognize in this world. Imam Baqir عليه السلام says: Whoever does not gain spiritual benefit from observing creation is even more spiritually blind regarding the Hereafter, which he has not yet seen. (168)

165) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā'iq. 166) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā'iq. 167) Isra, 97. 168) Tawhid Saduq, p. 455.

Messages

1- The personality of a person in the Hereafter is a reflection of his personality in this world. «فى هذه اعمى فهو فى الآخرة اعمى » Insight here leads to insight there, and spiritual blindness here results in blindness there.

Ayah

وَإِن كَادُوا۟ لَيَفْتِنُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَيْكَ لِتَفْتَرِىَ عَلَيْنَا غَيْرَهُۥ ۖ وَإِذًا لَّٱتَّخَذُوكَ خَلِيلًا (۷۳)
They were about to beguile you from what Allah has revealed to you so that you may fabricate something other than that against Us, whereat they would have befriended you. (73)

Key Points

We read in narrations that the polytheists requested the Noble Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) to show respect to their idols or grant them a one-year respite to continue idol worship, and the Prophet was close to accepting, but Allah protected Him!! However, these narrations are rejected and are incompatible with the principle of the Prophet's infallibility ('isma) and decisiveness, which is mentioned in other verses of the Quran and constitutes the Prophet's conduct (sirah). (169)

The author of Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan says: The fact that the verse uses «عن الّذى اَوحینا» (concerning that which We revealed) and not «عمّا اَوحینا» (concerning what We revealed) indicates that the efforts of the disbelievers to divert the Prophet's attention and concern were directed away from a specific person, not from the revealed contents themselves. But who was that person concerning whom Allah revealed the revelation? A hadith is narrated from Imam Baqir and Imam Kadhim (علیهما السلام) concerning the Wilayah (guardianship) of Hadrat Ali (عليه السلام), regarding whom Allah gave specific instructions to the Prophet through revelation. Allah revealed this verse so that the Prophet would not pay attention to the envy of the people, nor accept or tolerate them, thus rendering the efforts of the disbelievers to deviate or change the Prophet's stance fruitless. Of course, this statement is acceptable, considering the word «الّذى» and the aforementioned hadith, provided that the intention was the introduction of Hadrat Ali (عليه السلام) in Mecca, since the Surah is Meccan.

169) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- The disbelievers also have plans and programs to attract the prophets and leaders. «كادوا لیفتنونك»

2- Religious leaders must be vigilant regarding the enemy's conspiracies aimed at creating weakness and change in doctrinal positions. «كادوا لیفتنونك»

3- If friendship and relations with individuals and countries come at the cost of overlooking the doctrine and sacred values, they are worthless. «اذاً لاتّخذوك خلیلاً»

4- Unless Muslims abandon their doctrine and religion, the disbelievers and enemies will not become their true friends. The enemy is not satisfied with little; he wants to separate you from the doctrine. «اذاً لاتّخدوك خلیلاً» (170)

170) Similar to the verse: «لن ترضى عنك الیهود و لاالنّصارى حتّى تتّبع ملّتهم» The Jews and the Christians will not be satisfied with you unless you follow their creed and religion. (Al-Baqarah, 120).

Ayah

وَلَوْلَآ أَن ثَبَّتْنَـٰكَ لَقَدْ كِدتَّ تَرْكَنُ إِلَيْهِمْ شَيْـًٔا قَلِيلًا (۷٤)
And had We not fortified you, certainly you might have inclined toward them a bit. (74)

Key Points

This Ayah has no conflict whatsoever with the infallibility (Ismah) of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. The phrase "you were near to sinning" does not mean "sinning." Furthermore, the phrase "If We had not strengthened you..." is conditional, meaning: if Our support had not been present, perhaps you would have inclined (towards them). But since that support was present, the inclination did not occur. In other instances, the condition (if) does not imply definite occurrence, such as the Ayah: «ولو تَقَوّل علینا بعض الاَقاویل... لقَطّعنا منه الوَتین»(171) (If he had fabricated some false statements against Us, We would certainly have cut off his aorta.) And the Ayah: «لئن اشركت لیحبطنّ عملك»(172) (If you associate partners [with Allah], your deeds will surely be rendered vain.) which does not mean that those actions were actually performed. As Imam Reza عليه السلام said: This Ayah falls under the category of "speaking to the door so the wall may hear." (173) In reality, the address of the Ayah is directed toward the Muslims, not the person of the Prophet.

171) Al-Haqqah, 44-46. 172) Az-Zumar, 65. 173) "Iyyaka a'ni wa isma'i ya jarah" (I mean you, but listen, O neighbor). Tafsir Nur ath-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- If Allah's favor and protection were not present, there would be no guarantee for the immunity and infallibility of the prophets. «لولا ان ثبّتناك»

2- Decisiveness and impenetrability against enemies and disbelievers are among the distinctions of the prophets. «ثبّتناك»

3- Allah keeps the prophets infallible. «ثبّتناك»

4- Retreating on matters of belief and the fundamentals of the school of thought constitutes inclination and tendency toward the oppressors, and leaders are exposed to such a danger. «لولا أن ثبّتناك لقدتركن الیهم»

5- Deviation, even if slight, is considered significant coming from someone like the Prophet. «شیئاً قلیلاً»

6- The slightest retreat from principles and values is forbidden, because it is counted as a success and victory for the enemies. «شیئاً قلیلاً»

Ayah

إِذًا لَّأَذَقْنَـٰكَ ضِعْفَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ وَضِعْفَ ٱلْمَمَاتِ ثُمَّ لَا تَجِدُ لَكَ عَلَيْنَا نَصِيرًا (۷٥)
Then We would have surely made you taste a double [punishment] in this life and a double [punishment] after death, and then you would have not found for yourself any helper against Us. (75)

Key Points

After the revelation of this Ayah, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله used to recite this supplication: «الّلهم لاتَكلنِى الى نفسى طَرفَة عین» (O Allah! Do not leave me to myself, even for the blink of an eye!) (174)

174) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān.

Messages

1- Allah possesses both grace and wrath. «ثبّتناك, اذقناك»

2- Rukūn (inclination and reliance) towards the disbelievers is among the major sins, because the promise of punishment has been given concerning it. «تَركنُ الیهم... اذقناك»

3- The penalty for the slightest slip of leaders is double that of others. The higher a person's scholarly status, social standing, and spiritual position, the greater the responsibility and the danger. «شیئاً قلیلاً... ضعف الحیاة»

4- If heavier penalties are established for officials in criminal laws, it is not contrary to justice. «اذقناك ضعف الحیاة»

5- Against the Divine wrath, even for the Prophet, no power or position can create a barrier. «لاتجد لك علینا نصیراً»

6- Inclination towards the disbelievers causes a person to be left alone and deprived of Allah's help. «تَركَنُ الیهم... لاتجد... نصیراً»

Ayah

وَإِن كَادُوا۟ لَيَسْتَفِزُّونَكَ مِنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ لِيُخْرِجُوكَ مِنْهَا ۖ وَإِذًا لَّا يَلْبَثُونَ خِلَـٰفَكَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا (۷٦)
They were about to hound you out of the land, to expel you from it, but then they would not have stayed after you but a little. (76)

Key Points

This Ayah refers to Ayah 30 of Surah Al-Anfal, where He says: «واذ یَمكر بِك الذّین كفروا لِیثبِتوك او یَقتلوك او یخرِجوك و یمكرون و یمكراللّه واللّه خیرالماكرین» (Recall the time when the disbelievers plotted against you to imprison you, or kill you, or expel you. They plot, and Allah plans, and Allah is the best of planners.) It recalls the time when the disbelievers were plotting to imprison you, or kill you, or expel you. They scheme, and Allah also plans, and Allah is the best of planners.

Messages

  1. The Prophet was exposed to every kind of danger, conspiracy, assassination, displacement, and exile. «كادوا لیستفزّونك ...»

  2. The enemies of the faith aim to uproot and destroy the foundation of religion, and in this path, they show mercy to no one, not even the Prophet. «لیستفزّونك»

  3. The plan of the disbelievers is, in the first stage, to create a breach in the thought and belief of the leader, «لیفتنونك عن الّذى...» and in the next stage, it is severe action, exile, and expulsion of him. «لیخرجوك...»

  4. The presence of the Prophet among the people prevents Divine wrath, and if they distance and exile him, they will be destroyed within a short time. «لایلبثون خلافك الاّ قلیلا»

  5. The Noble Quran both gives news of the unseen and offers solace to the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله by stating the failure of the enemies' conspiracies. «كادوا لیستفزّونك ... لایلبثون خلافك الاّ قلیلاً»

Ayah

سُنَّةَ مَن قَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا قَبْلَكَ مِن رُّسُلِنَا ۖ وَلَا تَجِدُ لِسُنَّتِنَا تَحْوِيلًا (۷۷)
A precedent concerning those We have sent before you from among Our apostles, and you will not find any change in Our precedent. (77)

Key Points

The confirmation of the prophets and the destruction of the disbelievers and the oppressors is a Divine tradition (Sunnah), which is also mentioned in Surah Ibrahim, Ayah 13: «قال الذّین كفروا لرسلهم لنخرجنّكم من ارضنا او لتعودنّ فى ملّتنا فاوحى الیهم ربّهم لنهلكنّ الظّالمین». The disbelievers said to their messengers: "We will surely expel you from our land, unless you return to our religion," but Allah revealed to them that We will surely destroy the oppressors.

Messages

1- Since the Divine traditions (Sunan) are based on Allah's wisdom, they are fixed and do not change throughout history. «سنّة مَن قد ارسلنا»

2- The conspiracies of the disbelievers and the unseen Divine aid granted to the prophets have historical roots. «سنّة مَن قد ارسلنا»

3- Historical transformations are based on fixed Divine traditions. «لاتجد لسنّتنا تحویلاً»

4- Allah is Just, and under similar conditions, His mercy and His wrath are also consistent. «لاتجد لسنّتنا تحویلاً»

Ayah

أَقِمِ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ لِدُلُوكِ ٱلشَّمْسِ إِلَىٰ غَسَقِ ٱلَّيْلِ وَقُرْءَانَ ٱلْفَجْرِ ۖ إِنَّ قُرْءَانَ ٱلْفَجْرِ كَانَ مَشْهُودًا (۷۸)
Maintain the prayer [during the period] from the sun’s decline till the darkness of the night, and [observe particularly] the dawn recital. Indeed the dawn recital is attended [by angels]. (78)

Key Points

"Dulūk" (دلوك) is when the sun inclines from the middle of the sky towards the west, which is called Zawāl (declination), and it is the time for the Dhuhr (noon) and 'Asr (afternoon) prayers. And "Ghasaq al-Layl" (غَسق اللیل) is the time for the Maghrib (sunset) and 'Ishā (night) prayers, when darkness becomes widespread, and "Fajr" (فجر) is the time for the Fajr (dawn) prayer. (175) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام says: The first prayer that was made obligatory was the Dhuhr prayer, and then He recited this Ayah. (176) In another narration, it is stated: If the Fajr prayer is established at the beginning of Fajr, both the angels of the night and the day record it. (177) And the fact that the Fajr prayer is "Mashhūd" (مشهود - witnessed) may also be due to this meaning. (178) Meaning, both groups of angels observe it and bear witness to it.

175) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayān. 176) Kāfī, Vol. 3, p. 275. 177) Kāfī, Vol. 3, p. 275. 178) Tafsir al-Mīzān.

Messages

1- The criterion for recognizing prayer times in Islam is natural, such that it is understandable and perceptible to everyone, everywhere, and always. «لدُلوك الشمس، غَسَق اللیل، الفجر»

2- Religious programs are time-bound. «لدُلوك الشمس الى غَسَق اللیل»

3- Prayer (Salat) is the only act of worship described by the name "Qur'an." «قرآن الفجر»

4- The Qur'an and its recitation during prayer are the main focus. «قرآن الفجر» (179)

5- Among the prayers, the Fajr prayer holds a special status. «اِنّ قرآن الفجر كان مشهوداً»

179) The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Lā Salāta illā bi Qur'ān" (There is no prayer except with the Qur'an). Kanz al-'Ummāl, Hadith 19698.

Ayah

وَمِنَ ٱلَّيْلِ فَتَهَجَّدْ بِهِۦ نَافِلَةً لَّكَ عَسَىٰٓ أَن يَبْعَثَكَ رَبُّكَ مَقَامًا مَّحْمُودًا (۷۹)
And keep vigil for a part of the night, as a supererogatory [devotion] for you. It may be that your Lord will raise you to a praiseworthy station. (79)

Key Points

The term «هَجود» (Hajūd) means sleeping, while «تَهجّد» (Tahajjud) means removing sleep through worship. The Night Prayer (Salat al-Layl) is among the prayers possessing great merit, and it is mentioned in Surahs Al-Muzzammil and Al-Muddaththir: «قم اللّیل الاّ قلیلاً». More than 30 merits for the Night Prayer have been enumerated in narrations, some of which are referred to below:

  • All prophets performed the Night Prayer. The Night Prayer is the secret to bodily health and the illumination of the grave. The Night Prayer is effective in improving character, sustenance (rizq), removing sorrow, paying debts, and providing light to the eyes (joy/insight).(180)
  • The Night Prayer erases the sins of the day and is the light of the Day of Judgment.(181)
  • Imam Sadiq عليه السلام says: The reward for the Night Prayer is so immense that Allah says: «فلاتعلم نفس ما اُخفى لهم من قُرّة اَعین جزاء بما كانوا یعملون»(182) No soul knows what reward has been hidden for them.(183)
  • Imam Sadiq عليه السلام says: The honor (sharaf) of the believer is the Night Prayer, and his dignity ('izzah) is refraining from harming and annoying people.
  • Abu Dharr, while advising people near the Ka'bah, said: Perform two units (rak'ahs) of prayer in the heart of the night for the terror and loneliness of the grave.
  • Wretched is the one who is deprived of the Night Prayer.
  • The best among you is he who feeds others, gives salutations openly, and prays when people are asleep.
  • Three things were obligatory (wajib) upon the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله and recommended (mustahabb) for others: the Night Prayer, using the tooth-stick (miswak), and rising before dawn.(184)

Greatness is inherent in the word «مقاماً» (due to the tanwin), and narrations state that the «مقام محمود» (Praised Station) is intercession (shafa'ah).(185)
