Imam Ali عليه السلام says: A person's sins and bad deeds and behavior cause his deprivation from the Night Prayer.(186)

Allah has sworn by all segments of time. For example: «والفجر»(187), «والصبح»(188), «والنهار»(189), «والعصر». However, the pre-dawn (sahar) time has been sworn by three times: «واللیل اذا یسر»(190), «واللیل اذا عَسعَس»(191), «واللیل اذ اَدبر»(192)—meaning, swearing by the night when it departs. And regarding seeking forgiveness (istighfar) at dawn, two verses have been revealed: «وبالاسحار هم یستغفرون»(193), «المستغفرین بالاسحار»(194)

180) Safinat al-Bihar. 181) Bihar, Vol. 84, p. 140. 182) Surah As-Sajdah, 17. 183) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan. 184) Tafsir Furqan. 185) Tafsir Nur ath-Thaqalayn. 186) Bihar, Vol. 87, p. 145. 187) Surah Al-Fajr, 1. 188) Surah Al-Muddaththir, 34. 189) Surah Ash-Shams, 3. 190) Surah Al-Fajr, 4. 191) Surah At-Takwir, 17. 192) Surah Al-Muddaththir, 33. 193) Surah Adh-Dhariyat, 18. 194) Surah Aal-Imran, 17.

Messages

  1. The Night Prayer was obligatory upon the Prophet of Islam. «فتهّجد به نافلة لك» Yes, the position of leadership necessitates heavier responsibilities.
  2. The heart of the night is the best time for worship. «و من اللیل فتهجّد»
  3. Praised spiritual stations are attained in the shadow of worship and servitude. «فتهّجد، مقاماً محموداً»
  4. We do not have a right over Allah that we can demand through our acts of worship; everything is hope and His grace. «عسى»
  5. The Night Prayer alone is not sufficient; other perfections are also necessary. «عسى»
  6. Unless a person takes a step toward perfection himself, Allah will not raise him to a station. «فتهجّد... یبعثك ربّك»
  7. A station is valuable only if it is praised by Allah; otherwise, vain fame is neither valuable nor lasting. Indeed, smoke rises high into the air, but it is black-faced (disgraced). «یبعثك ربّك»
  8. Allah Himself has praised intercession (shafa'ah) for His chosen servants (awliya), not because the Divine Awliya are kinder to the servants than He is. «یبعثك ربّك مقاماً محموداً»

Ayah

وَقُل رَّبِّ أَدْخِلْنِى مُدْخَلَ صِدْقٍ وَأَخْرِجْنِى مُخْرَجَ صِدْقٍ وَٱجْعَل لِّى مِن لَّدُنكَ سُلْطَـٰنًا نَّصِيرًا (۸٠)
And say, ‘My Lord! ‘Admit me with a worthy entrance, and bring me out with a worthy departure, and render me a favourable authority from Yourself.’ (80)

Key Points

Sincerity (Sidq) is the best description and state for an action and a person, both at its beginning and its end. How often are actions that have an insincere beginning, starting with the intention of showing off (Riya), seeking reputation (Sum'ah), deception, seeking superiority, and the like; or they have a sincere beginning, but end up mixed with arrogance (Ghurur), self-admiration ('Ujb), obligation (Minnah), nullification (Habt), and a bad outcome (Su'u 'Aqibah), and their blessings are erased. The secret to the failure of many movements is the lack of sincerity within them and the cessation of Divine aid from them. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام says: Whenever you feel fear or dread about entering into an action, recite this Ayah and proceed. (195) A person reaches a point where he becomes one of the "Siddiqin" (the truthful ones); whatever he says, he acts upon, and whatever he acts upon, he says. (196) His heart, tongue, and action become one. Among the manifestations of "Sultan Nasir" (a helping authority), one can count having a worthy successor, a loyal helper, and a compassionate and competent minister.

195) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 196) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- We should articulate our requests before Allah. «وقل ربّ»

2- A supplication that accompanies prayer and follows it has a greater effect. «اقم الصلوة... فتهجّد... و قل ربّ...»

3- We must learn how to supplicate from God Himself—how to call upon Him and what to ask for. «و قل ربّ أدخلنى...»

4- One of the principles of management is foresight and long-term planning. «أخرجنى مُخرج صدق»

5- More important than a good beginning is a good end and outcome. One should not be content with a sweet beginning, but rather must seek refuge in God from the danger of a bad outcome. «أخرجنى مُخرج صدق»

6- Leading a global movement whose beginning and end are sincere and free from deviation is impossible without supplication, seeking aid from God, and receiving Divine assistance. «ربّ أخرجنى»

7- All affairs are in the hands of God, and aid must be sought from Him. «أدخلنى، أخرجنى، اِجعل»

8- Gaining power for sacred goals is valuable. «واجعل لى من لدنك سلطاناً»

9- The believer seeks victory and aid only from God. «من لدنك سلطاناً نصیرا»

Ayah

وَقُلْ جَآءَ ٱلْحَقُّ وَزَهَقَ ٱلْبَـٰطِلُ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلْبَـٰطِلَ كَانَ زَهُوقًا (۸۱)
And say, ‘The truth has come, and falsehood has vanished. Indeed falsehood is bound to vanish.’ (81)

Key Points

Al-Haqq (The Truth/Right) means fixed and enduring (Bāqī). Therefore, Allah and everything that comes from Him is Haqq. Al-Haqq is one of the Names of Allah. The word zahūq means departing or vanishing. Zahaqa nafsahu means the soul departed from his body. Instances cited for this Ayah include the advent of Islam, the entry into Medina, the Conquest of Mecca (Fath Makkah), and the breaking of the idols, in all of which falsehood (Bātil) was defeated. However, the Ayah has a broad concept and promises the annihilation of falsehood and the permanence of truth (Haqq). The permanence of truth and the destruction of falsehood is a divine tradition and law, not an illusion or a coincidence, even if the followers of truth are few and the supporters of falsehood are many. This is because truth (Haqq) is fixed and lasting, like water, while falsehood is unstable and perishable, like foam. "فامّا الزَبَد فیذهب جُفاءً و امّا ما ینفع النّاس فَیَمكث فى الارض" (197) The Quran states: "نَقذِف بالحقّ على الباطل فیَدمغه" (198). We hurl the truth against falsehood and destroy it. It is in this manner that falsehood is destined to depart. Therefore, truth must attack falsehood with power and crushing force.

197) Ra'd, 17. 198) Anbiya, 18.

Messages

1- The Prophet must decisively announce the ultimate victory of truth to the people. "قل جاء الحق"

2- Truth must be brought into the field so that falsehood may vanish. "جاء...زهق"

3- Ultimately, falsehood is destined to depart and be destroyed, while truth is permanent and enduring. "جاء...زهق" (The past tense verb indicates certainty).

4- One should not fear the displays and maneuvers of falsehood, as they have no permanence. "كان زهوقاً"

Ayah

وَنُنَزِّلُ مِنَ ٱلْقُرْءَانِ مَا هُوَ شِفَآءٌ وَرَحْمَةٌ لِّلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ۙ وَلَا يَزِيدُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ إِلَّا خَسَارًا (۸۲)
We send down in the Quran that which is a cure and mercy for the faithful; and it increases the wrongdoers only in loss. (82)

Key Points

The word "min" (مِن) in "min al-Qur'an" (من القرآن) does not mean "some" (بعض) implying that only certain parts of the Qur'an are a cure. Rather, it clarifies that everything revealed from the Noble Qur'an is healing. Although the Qur'an is guidance for everyone: «هُدىً للنّاس» (199), only those benefit from this light who open the window of their soul towards it, abandon stubbornness and enmity, and approach the Qur'an with a sound spirit: «هُدىً للمتّقین» (200). Therefore, it only increases the loss of the stubborn and sick-hearted. It is like rain, which, when it falls upon a carcass, causes a foul odor to rise, even though the rain itself is pure and clear.

The argumentation of the Qur'an cures intellectual stagnation; the admonition of the Qur'an treats hardness of heart; the history narrated in the Qur'an removes confusion; the beauty of its rhythm and eloquence attracts the fleeing soul; its laws and rulings eradicate superstitious habits; its recitation and contemplation heal the sickness of heedlessness; seeking blessing through it cures physical ailments, and its guidance illuminates the darknesses.

The cure provided by the Qur'an differs greatly from the cure provided by material medicines. The medicine of the Qur'an has no side effects, does not become obsolete, and has no expiration date. One who is cured by the Qur'an becomes a means of cure for others. There is no mistake in the prescription of the Qur'anic cure; it is always available to everyone. The Physician who provides this medicine knows us, loves us, and the result of His prescription is eternal. His prescription and medicine have no equal.

Imam Ali عليه السلام says: «فانّ القرآن شفاءٌ من اكبر داء و هوالكفر و النفاق و الغى و الضلال» (201), meaning the Qur'an is the cure for the greatest diseases, which are disbelief (kufr), hypocrisy (nifaq), error, and misguidance.

Allah has made mercy incumbent upon Himself: «كتب على نفسه الرحمة» (202), and He has made His Prophet a mercy for all the worlds: «رحمة للعالمین» (203). Furthermore, His community shows mercy to one another: «رحماء بینهم» (204), and His Book is also a mercy. "A cure and a mercy."

199) Baqarah, 185. 200) Baqarah, 2. 201) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 176. 202) An'am, 12. 203) Anbiya, 107.

Messages

1- Since the Qur'an is from Allah, Who is the Creator of humanity and its nature (fitrah), its laws are also consistent with that nature and are His means of salvation. "A cure" (شفاء).

2- Whatever comes from the All-Merciful and Compassionate God is a mercy. «رحمة للمؤمنین».

3- Since the disbelievers do not act upon the commands and prohibitions of the Qur'an, the revelation of every instruction increases their sin and augments their loss. «لایزیدالظالمین الا خساراً».

Ayah

وَإِذَآ أَنْعَمْنَا عَلَى ٱلْإِنسَـٰنِ أَعْرَضَ وَنَـَٔا بِجَانِبِهِۦ ۖ وَإِذَا مَسَّهُ ٱلشَّرُّ كَانَ يَـُٔوسًا (۸۳)
When We bless man, he is disregardful and turns aside; but when an ill befalls him, he is despondent. (83)

Key Points

The term «نابجانبه» means he turns toward himself, inclines toward himself, and turns his shoulder (or side).

Messages

1- Prosperity and comfort are the grounds for negligence (or heedlessness). «اذا اَنعَمنا، أعرض»

2- Instead of recognizing the blessing as coming from Allah and inclining toward Him, man considers it to be the result of his own thought, talent, genius, and work, and abandons Allah. «نأبجانبه»

3- Blessings are from Allah, but evil and harm result from our own actions. «اَنعمنا، مَسّه الشرّ»

4- Man is so weak that he becomes afflicted with negligence due to a blessing, and becomes despairing due to a small hardship or incident. «اذا اَنعَمنا...أعرض، اذا مَسّه الشرّ كان یؤساً»

Ayah

قُلْ كُلٌّ يَعْمَلُ عَلَىٰ شَاكِلَتِهِۦ فَرَبُّكُمْ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنْ هُوَ أَهْدَىٰ سَبِيلًا (۸٤)
Say, ‘Everyone acts according to his character. Your Lord knows best who is better guided with regard to the way.’ (84)

Key Points

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام recited this Ayah when discussing intention (niyyah) and the fact that intention is better than action. (205) Shākilah (شاكله) means the structure and spiritual fabric of a human being, which develops in a person as a result of heredity, upbringing, and social culture. Some have taken shākilah to mean fitrah (innate disposition), whereas fitrahs are uniform and constant. What differs among people are their motivations, temperaments, habits, and their intellectual and familial fabric, and the action of every person is dependent upon these. (206)

204) Al-Fath, 29. 205) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 206) Tafsir Furqan.

Messages

1- Human behavior stems from his intellectual, spiritual, and moral personality. «یعمل على شاكلته» (What is in the jar is what pours out of it.)

2- Since behaviors are rooted in states and habits, one must avoid improper motivations and habituation so that improper actions do not become ingrained traits (malakah). «كلّ یَعمل على شاكلته»

3- The paths of guidance also have degrees and hierarchies. «أهدى»

4- Whoever has a better intention is closer to guidance. «أهدى سبیلاً»

Ayah

وَيَسْـَٔلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلرُّوحِ ۖ قُلِ ٱلرُّوحُ مِنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّى وَمَآ أُوتِيتُم مِّنَ ٱلْعِلْمِ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا (۸٥)
They question you concerning the Spirit. Say, ‘The Spirit is of the command of my Lord, and you have not been given of the knowledge except a few [of you].’ (85)

Key Points

The word Rūḥ (Spirit) appears 21 times in the Quran and is used in the following contexts: A: The spirit breathed into the human body. «نُفِخ فیه من روحه»(207) B: Revelation (Wahy). «یُلقى الرّوح من أمره على مَن یشاء من عباده»(208) C: The Quran. «كذلك اَوحینا الیك روحاً من أمرنا»(209) D: The Holy Spirit (Rūḥ al-Qudus). «ایّدناه بروح القدس»(210) E: The greatest of the angels. «تنزّل الملائكة والروّح»(211) Nevertheless, the human spirit is a complex, unknown matter, a Divine breath (Nafkhah), possessing a celestial dimension, and only Allah knows it. Imam Baqir عليه السلام did not interpret the word qalīl (little/few) as referring to little knowledge, but rather interpreted it as meaning few people (212). That is, complete knowledge has been given only to a few people, and only they are aware of the Spirit.

207) Sajdah, 9. 208) Ghafir, 15. 209) Shura, 52. 210) Baqarah, 87. 211) Qadr, 4. 212) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The Prophets were the reference point for people's questions. «یسئلونك»

2- The reality of the Spirit is beyond human understanding and knowledge, and it is a secret among the Divine secrets. «من أمر ربّى»

3- All human knowledge is a Divine gift. «اوتیتم»

4- Human knowledge is limited and very little. «ما اوتیتم من العلم الاّ قلیلاً»

Ayahs

وَلَئِن شِئْنَا لَنَذْهَبَنَّ بِٱلَّذِىٓ أَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَيْكَ ثُمَّ لَا تَجِدُ لَكَ بِهِۦ عَلَيْنَا وَكِيلًا (۸٦)
If We wish, We would take away what We have revealed to you. Then you would not find for yourself any defender against Us, (86)
إِلَّا رَحْمَةً مِّن رَّبِّكَ ۚ إِنَّ فَضْلَهُۥ كَانَ عَلَيْكَ كَبِيرًا (۸۷)
except a mercy from your Lord. Indeed His grace has been great upon you. (87)

Key Points

Ayah 86 has a threatening tone towards the Prophet, indicating that Allah, just as He grants, can take back if He wills (213). Ayah 87 has an encouraging tone. The Quran, revelation (wahy), prophethood, finality (khatamiyyah), and intercession (shafa'ah) are all divine favors bestowed upon the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, originating from the station of Allah's Lordship (Rububiyyah). Allah does not take back His gifts from him, even though His hand is open, His power is firm, and He is able to take everything back.

213) The same applies to other matters, just as He says: «خلقكم» (He created you) and «یذهبكم» (He takes you away). Just as He says: «یعزّ» (He grants honor) and «یذلّ» (He humiliates). Just as He says: «یهدى» (He guides) and «یضلّ» (He leads astray). Just as He says: «احیاكم» (He gives you life) and «یمیتكم» (He causes you to die). Just as He says: «ینصركم» (He helps you) and «یخذلكم» (He forsakes you). Yes, creation and removal, honor and humiliation, guidance and misguidance, life and death, help and abandonment are solely in His hand.

Messages

1- We must recognize blessings as coming from Allah and not become arrogant, because the permanence and disappearance of blessings are by His will. «لنذهبنّ بالّذى اوحینا»

2- No one can play a role against the divine wrath. «ثمّ لاتجد»

3- The revelation of heavenly books and divine inspiration (wahy) are among the affairs of Allah's Lordship (Rububiyyah) and are aimed at the nurturing of humanity. «الّذى اوحینا... الاّ رحمة من ربّك»

4- The Quran is a great divine favor. «اوحینا... كان فضله علیك كبیراً» Since Allah is Great and Exalted, His kindness is also great, and His great favor is specific to the Prophet.

Ayah

قُل لَّئِنِ ٱجْتَمَعَتِ ٱلْإِنسُ وَٱلْجِنُّ عَلَىٰٓ أَن يَأْتُوا۟ بِمِثْلِ هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانِ لَا يَأْتُونَ بِمِثْلِهِۦ وَلَوْ كَانَ بَعْضُهُمْ لِبَعْضٍ ظَهِيرًا (۸۸)
Say, ‘Should all humans and jinn rally to bring the like of this Quran, they will not bring its like, even if they assisted one another.’ (88)

Key Points

Ẓahīr (ظهیر), derived from ẓahr (ظَهر), means support or backer. This Ayah is a response to the statement of the disbelievers who said: «لو نشاء لقلنا مثل هذا» (214) (If we wished, we could certainly say the like of this [Qur'an]). And this challenge (taḥaddī) by the Qur'an to produce a sample like it has remained unanswered for centuries. Even now, Arabic-speaking enemies from the People of the Book and atheistic schools of thought, despite all their enmity towards Islam and the support of various powers, have been unable to produce the like of the Qur'an. The unique characteristics of the Qur'an are that: it is a miracle, it is fluent and diverse, it gives news of the future, it contains the best stories, it employs the best method of invitation (to faith), and it is an explainer of all individual and social, worldly and otherworldly issues and needs, in all fields and throughout all times.

214) Al-Anfāl, 31.

Messages

1- The Jinn, like humans, are obligated (by divine law), and the Qur'an is also their Book. «اجتمعت الانس و الجنّ»

2- The inability to produce the like of the Qur'an is proof of this Book's miraculous nature (i'jāz). «لایأتون بمثله»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ صَرَّفْنَا لِلنَّاسِ فِى هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانِ مِن كُلِّ مَثَلٍ فَأَبَىٰٓ أَكْثَرُ ٱلنَّاسِ إِلَّا كُفُورًا (۸۹)
We have certainly interspersed this Quran with every [kind of] parable for the people, but most people are only intent on ingratitude. (89)

Messages

1- The diversity and rhetorical variations of the Qur'an are among the dimensions of its miraculousness (i'jāz). «صرّفنا»

2- Allah has completed the argument (Hujjah) upon the people through the verses of the Qur'an. «لقد صرّفنا ...»

3- Monotony is tiring; the message of invitation must be diverse. «صرّفنا»

4- The Book of Guidance must be accessible to everyone. «هذا القرآن»

5- One of the best methods of teaching and training is the use of proverbs and allegorical expression. «صرّفنا للنّاس... من كلّ مثل»

6- People are different, and diverse examples can be enlightening for various groups. «صرّفنا للنّاس... من كلّ مثل»

7- The reason for people's denial is their obstinacy, not that the argument (Hujjah) has failed to reach them. «فأبى اكثر الناس...»

8- The majority is not a sign of truth (Haqqāniyyah). «فأبى اكثر الناس»

Ayahs

وَقَالُوا۟ لَن نُّؤْمِنَ لَكَ حَتَّىٰ تَفْجُرَ لَنَا مِنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ يَنۢبُوعًا (۹٠)
They say, ‘We will not believe you until you make a spring gush forth for us from the ground. (90)
أَوْ تَكُونَ لَكَ جَنَّةٌ مِّن نَّخِيلٍ وَعِنَبٍ فَتُفَجِّرَ ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرَ خِلَـٰلَهَا تَفْجِيرًا (۹۱)
Or until you have a garden of date palms and vines and you make streams gush through it. (91)

Key Points

| "Yanbū‘" (یَنبوع) refers to a spring that never dries up and constantly gushes forth. The polytheists, who denied the miraculous nature of the Quran, conditioned their belief on achieving material gains and had unreasonable expectations of the Prophet. They were oblivious to the fact that the Prophet performs miracles to prove his prophethood, not to satisfy the whims of stubborn people, or for entertainment and amusement, or to escape work and effort. In reality, unreasonable expectation, hardness of heart, stubbornness, fault-finding, ignorance of the goal, and disrespect combined, leading them to make illogical demands of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, such as: a spring of water, a garden, a river, the sky falling, summoning God and the angels, a house of gold, flying to the sky, or a private letter from God.

Messages

1- Everyone requests according to the extent of their thought. The criterion for prophethood in the view of the polytheists was gardens, springs, and material things. «لن نؤمن لك حتّى تفجر...»

2- The goal of the prophets was to guide people toward Monotheism (Tawhid), but a group was preoccupied with gardens, gold, and silver. «لن نؤمن لك حتّى تفجر لنا...»

3- Not every request or expectation is logical. Unreasonable expectations are common from prophets and ideological preachers. «حتّى تفجر...»

Ayahs

أَوْ تُسْقِطَ ٱلسَّمَآءَ كَمَا زَعَمْتَ عَلَيْنَا كِسَفًا أَوْ تَأْتِىَ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ قَبِيلًا (۹۲)
Or until you cause the sky to fall in fragments upon us, just as you have averred. Or until you bring Allah and the angels [right] in front of us. (92)
أَوْ يَكُونَ لَكَ بَيْتٌ مِّن زُخْرُفٍ أَوْ تَرْقَىٰ فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَلَن نُّؤْمِنَ لِرُقِيِّكَ حَتَّىٰ تُنَزِّلَ عَلَيْنَا كِتَـٰبًا نَّقْرَؤُهُۥ ۗ قُلْ سُبْحَانَ رَبِّى هَلْ كُنتُ إِلَّا بَشَرًا رَّسُولًا (۹۳)
Or until you have a house of gold, or you ascend into the sky. And we will not believe your ascension until you bring down for us a book that we may read.’ Say, ‘Immaculate is my Lord! Am I anything but a human apostle?!’ (93)

Key Points

The phrase "كما زَعَمت" (as you claimed) refers to the threat and promise of punishment that the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله mentioned in Ayah 9 of Surah Saba'. Regarding "أو نُسقِط علیهم كِسَفاً من السماء" (or cause fragments of the sky to fall upon them), miracles are among the affairs of Allah's Lordship (Rububiyyah), and their source is His divine Will and Wisdom. A condition for a miracle is that it must not be aimless, futile, or impossible. The requests of the disbelievers from the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله in this Ayah are futile. Causing the sky to fall would lead to the destruction of everyone, and no one would remain to believe. Summoning Allah and the angels is also impossible, because they are not physical bodies (jism) to be summoned and observed by us.

Messages

1- The disbelievers are obstinate and impolite. There is a type of impoliteness in the expressions "لن نؤمن" (we will never believe) and "كما زَعمت" (as you claimed).

2- The disbelievers did not believe the threats of the prophets. "كما زَعمتَ" (as you claimed).

3- Impossible and futile requests are not accepted. "تأتى باللّه" (you bring Allah).

4- The sensory orientation (sensory focus) of the polytheists is the reason for their undue expectations. "تُسقِط... تأتى" (you cause to fall... you bring).

Ayah

وَمَا مَنَعَ ٱلنَّاسَ أَن يُؤْمِنُوٓا۟ إِذْ جَآءَهُمُ ٱلْهُدَىٰٓ إِلَّآ أَن قَالُوٓا۟ أَبَعَثَ ٱللَّهُ بَشَرًا رَّسُولًا (۹٤)
Nothing has kept these people from believing when guidance came to them, but their saying, ‘Has Allah sent a human as an apostle?!’ (94)

Key Points

Those who lack sound logic mistake a point of strength for a point of weakness. The humanity of the Prophet is a point of strength, not weakness. This is because, due to having instincts, desires, and difficulties, he understands the pain of human beings, can serve as a practical role model, and can offer a beneficial prescription.

According to the verses of the Quran, the excuses of the disbelievers for not believing are as follows:

A: Why are the prophets human beings just like us? Answer: The best type of propagation is that which is accompanied by a practical example and the presentation of a role model.

B: If a human is to be a prophet, why shouldn't it be so-and-so from such-and-such a family or city? Answer: The divisions among people are based on imagination and material standards, but the descent and reception of revelation require merit.

C: Why shouldn't we ourselves be prophets? Answer: Prophethood is a very exalted station, even superior to the station of the angels, and not every human being, regardless of their background, is worthy of prophethood.

D: Why do the angels not descend upon us ourselves? Answer: The descent of the angel is upon those who have no history of deviation or sin, otherwise, they would bring a lie and a deviation to the people every day in the name of revelation.

Messages

1- The disbelievers are fault-finders and illogical. The excuse of the disbelievers was why the Prophet was a human being just like them. «ما منع...»

2- The Prophets went to the people for their guidance and training. «جائهم الهُدى»

3- Because the personality and dignity of man were unknown to the ignorant person, he imagined that man was not worthy of prophethood. «بَشَراً رسولا»

Ayah

قُل لَّوْ كَانَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مَلَـٰٓئِكَةٌ يَمْشُونَ مُطْمَئِنِّينَ لَنَزَّلْنَا عَلَيْهِم مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَلَكًا رَّسُولًا (۹٥)
Say, ‘Had there been angels in the earth, walking around and residing [in it like humans do], We would have sent down to them an angel from the heaven as apostle.’ (95)

Key Points

The Ayah, in refuting the view of those who objected to the Prophet's humanity, states: Sending a Prophet is a Divine tradition and a religious necessity. Even if all the inhabitants of the earth were angels, and there was no conflict or dispute among them, and all were in peace and prosperity, still, a messenger of their own kind would come from Allah, because the duty of the prophets is spiritual perfection, growth, and providing an example, not merely resolving hostility and enmity among them.

Messages

1- The homogeneity of leaders with the people is essential for their training and ability to follow the example. Human for human, angel for angel. «ملائكة، ملكاً»

2- Messengership and prophethood are from Allah and are not elective. «لنزّلنا... رسولا»

Ayah

قُلْ كَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ شَهِيدًۢا بَيْنِى وَبَيْنَكُمْ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ بِعِبَادِهِۦ خَبِيرًۢا بَصِيرًا (۹٦)
Say, ‘Allah suffices as witness between me and you. Indeed He is well aware His servants and a keen observer.’ (96)

Messages

1- Allah consoles the Prophet by stating His witness and knowledge regarding the illogical behavior of the disbelievers. «قل كفى باللّه»

2- The reliance of the Prophets is upon Allah; therefore, they possess firmness and do not retreat. «كفى باللّه»

3- Allah has completed the argument (proof) against the polytheists; therefore, disputes must cease. «كفى باللّه شهیداً»

4- Belief in Allah's knowledge and awareness causes distance from deviation and excuse-making. «كفى باللّه شهیداً»

5- A witness must be knowledgeable, precise, and insightful. «شهیداً... خبیراً بصیراً»

6- Allah is both a witness that the Prophet has fulfilled his duty, and He is aware of the obstinacy and stubbornness or the faith and submission of His servants. «كفى باللّه شهیداً... انّه كان بعباده خبيراً بصيراً»

Ayah

وَمَن يَهْدِ ٱللَّهُ فَهُوَ ٱلْمُهْتَدِ ۖ وَمَن يُضْلِلْ فَلَن تَجِدَ لَهُمْ أَوْلِيَآءَ مِن دُونِهِۦ ۖ وَنَحْشُرُهُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ عَلَىٰ وُجُوهِهِمْ عُمْيًا وَبُكْمًا وَصُمًّا ۖ مَّأْوَىٰهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ ۖ كُلَّمَا خَبَتْ زِدْنَـٰهُمْ سَعِيرًا (۹۷)
Whomever Allah guides is rightly guided, and whomever He leads astray—you will never for find them any guardians besides Him. On the Day of Resurrection, We shall muster them [scrambling] on their faces, blind, dumb, and deaf. Their refuge shall be hell. Whenever it subsides, We shall intensify the blaze for them. (97)

Key Points

Khabať (خَبت), means the subsiding of the flame. And Sa'īr (سَعیر), means the blazing up of the fire and is one of the names of Hell (Jahannam). Question: If the misguided are deaf, blind, and mute on the Day of Judgment, then how is it that other verses speak of them seeing the Fire: «رأى المجرمون النّار»(215), or hearing the sound of Hell: «سَمعوا لها تغیّضاً و زفیراً»(216), or their cries and wailing: «دعوا هنالك ثبوراً»(217)? Answer: The stages and stations (mawāqif) of the Day of Judgment are different and numerous. In every station, there may be a condition that does not exist in the stage following it.(218)

215) Kahf, 53. 216) Furqan, 12. 217) Furqan, 13. 218) Tafsir Tibyan.

Messages

1- Guidance is not solely dependent on knowledge, intellect, and argumentation; it also requires Divine success (tawfīq). «مَن یَهدى اللّه»

2- There is no savior for the misguided except Allah. «مَن یُضلِل فلن تَجِد ...»

3- Whoever does not utilize his ears, eyes, and tongue in the path of finding the truth, speaking the truth, and seeing the truth, will also be resurrected on the Day of Judgment as blind, deaf, mute, and face-down. This is, in reality, a form of the embodiment of man's deeds (tajassum-e 'amal)! «نحشرهم...على وجوههم عمیاً و بكماً وصمّاً»

4- The Fire of Hell continuously blazes more fiercely for the criminals. «كلمّاخَبت زدناهم سعیراً»

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ جَزَآؤُهُم بِأَنَّهُمْ كَفَرُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا وَقَالُوٓا۟ أَءِذَا كُنَّا عِظَـٰمًا وَرُفَـٰتًا أَءِنَّا لَمَبْعُوثُونَ خَلْقًا جَدِيدًا (۹۸)
That is their requital because they defied Our signs and said, ‘What, when we have become bones and dust, shall we really be raised in a new creation?’ (98)

Key Points

| The word "رُفات" (rufāt) refers to particles of straw (or dust) that have become so fine that they are no longer breakable. (219)

219) Mufradāt al-Rāghib.

Messages

1- Allah's wrath upon the disbelievers is not without reason; rather, it is the result of their own actions. "ذلك باَنّهم"

2- The disbelievers have no proof for denying the Resurrection (Ma'ad); what they say is either out of mockery, astonishment, or deeming it improbable. "ءاِنّا"

3- The Resurrection (Ma'ad) is physical (corporeal). "عِظاماً و رُفاتاً، خَلقاً جدیداً"

Ayah

۞ أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ قَادِرٌ عَلَىٰٓ أَن يَخْلُقَ مِثْلَهُمْ وَجَعَلَ لَهُمْ أَجَلًا لَّا رَيْبَ فِيهِ فَأَبَى ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ إِلَّا كُفُورًا (۹۹)
Do they not see that Allah, who created the heavens and the earth, is able to create the like of them? He has appointed for them a term, in which there is no doubt; yet the wrongdoers are only intent on ingratitude. (99)

Key Points

The phrase "لاریب فیه" (there is no doubt in it) means there is no room for any doubt regarding the Qur'an, not that no one will ever doubt it. The Qur'an has repeatedly referred to the creation of the heavens and the earth, the magnificence of the creation system, and the Divine power over creation, in order to remove doubts about the Resurrection (Ma'ad). "ءانتم اَشدّ خَلقاً أم السماء بَناها" (220) Is your creation harder, or the heaven which He built? This means the best proof for the possibility of something existing is its actual occurrence and existence. On the Day of Resurrection, the decayed bones are the same worldly bones, and the soul attached to them is the same soul. However, the Qur'an says "مِثلَهم" (like them), similar to a brick that, if crushed and then rebuilt into a brick using the same clay, people say: This is like the first one, not the exact same one, even though its materials are the same materials. (221) "مِثلَهم"

220) An-Nazi'at, 27. 221) Tafsir Nur ath-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The best proof for the possibility of the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is attention to the Divine power in nature. "أوَلَم یَروا"

2- Worldly life is only a few days; the disbelievers should not show so much obstinacy, for either their death will arrive, or the Resurrection. "جَعل لهم اجلاً"

3- Disregard for the power of Allah and deeming the Resurrection improbable is an oppressive act, "فأبى الظالمون," and oppression is the ground for disbelief. "الاّ كُفورا"

Ayah

قُل لَّوْ أَنتُمْ تَمْلِكُونَ خَزَآئِنَ رَحْمَةِ رَبِّىٓ إِذًا لَّأَمْسَكْتُمْ خَشْيَةَ ٱلْإِنفَاقِ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ قَتُورًا (۱٠٠)
Say, ‘Even if you possessed the treasuries of my Lord’s mercy, you would withhold them for the fear of being spent, and man is very niggardly.’ (100)

Key Points

"Qatūr" (قَتور) derives from "Qitr" (قِتر), meaning stinginess and withholding from giving.

Messages

1- The abundance of wealth has no effect on the generosity of greedy and stingy individuals. Some people are insatiable, and no matter how much they accumulate, they are still worried about its decrease. «لو انتم تملكون خزائن... لأمسكتم...»

2- Fear of destitution is one of the roots of stinginess. «اَمسكتم خَشیة الانفاق»

3- The source of human behavior lies in his disposition and characteristics. «اَمسكتم...كان الانسان قَتورا»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا مُوسَىٰ تِسْعَ ءَايَـٰتٍۭ بَيِّنَـٰتٍ ۖ فَسْـَٔلْ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ إِذْ جَآءَهُمْ فَقَالَ لَهُۥ فِرْعَوْنُ إِنِّى لَأَظُنُّكَ يَـٰمُوسَىٰ مَسْحُورًا (۱٠۱)
Certainly We gave Moses nine manifest signs. So ask the Children of Israel. When he came to them, Pharaoh said to him, ‘O Moses, indeed I think you are bewitched.’ (101)

Key Points

The nine miracles of Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) consisted of: 1- The staff turning into a serpent. 2- The whiteness of the palm (the shining hand). 3- Splitting the sea with the staff. 4- The flood (storm). 5- The plague of locusts. 6- The plague of lice. 7- The plague of frogs. 8- The water turning into blood. 9- Twelve springs gushing forth from the rock with a single strike of the staff. Of course, it can be said that His miracles were eighteen, because the return of each of them to its original state is also another miracle.

Question: Besides these nine miracles, the Quran has mentioned other miracles for Prophet Moses, such as the mountain being uprooted and placed above the heads of the Jews, the descent of the food mann and salwa (manna and quails), and the slaughtered cow that brought the dead back to life. So why are only nine miracles mentioned?

Answer: The number 9 miracles were those related to Pharaoh, who still did not believe even after seeing them. The other miracles were not related to Pharaoh.(222)

222) Tafsir al-Mizan, Furqan.

Messages

1- Allah supports His prophets. «لقد آتینا»

2- By granting miracles to the prophets, Allah completes the argument (establishes proof) against the people. «لقدآتینا»

3- Those who are more obstinate require more argumentation and miracles. «تِسعَ آیاتٍ»

4- A miracle must be clear and undeniable. «بیّنات»

5- Prophets are physicians who go out to the people.(223) «جاءهم»

6- The arrogant resort to belittlement and insult in the face of miracles and proofs. «لأظنّك یا موسى مَسحوراً»

223) Imam Ali (عليه السلام), in introducing the Prophet, said: «طبیبٌ دوّارٌ بطبّه» (A physician who travels with his medicine). Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 108.

Ayah

قَالَ لَقَدْ عَلِمْتَ مَآ أَنزَلَ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ إِلَّا رَبُّ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ بَصَآئِرَ وَإِنِّى لَأَظُنُّكَ يَـٰفِرْعَوْنُ مَثْبُورًا (۱٠۲)
He said, ‘You certainly know that no one has sent these [signs] except the Lord of the heavens and the earth, as eye-openers, and I, O Pharaoh, indeed think you are doomed.’ (102)

Key Points

| Question: Although Moses (عليه السلام) was certain of Pharaoh's destruction, why did He say, "لأظنّك" (I think/I suppose)? Answer: Firstly: Ẓann (ظنّ) does not only mean supposition; it is also used to mean belief and certainty (yaqīn). Secondly: Perhaps He still held out hope for his guidance. Thirdly: When the enemy does not violate boundaries (in speech), the etiquette of debate and discussion requires us to maintain respect as well. Pharaoh said, "لاظنّك يا موسى مسحوراً" (Indeed, I think you, O Moses, are bewitched). Moses, in response, said to him, "لاظنّك يا فرعون مثبوراً" (Indeed, I think you, O Pharaoh, are doomed/destroyed).

Messages

1- Sometimes a person pretends to be ignorant, even though he knows the truth. "لقد عَلِمتَ..."

2- Knowledge alone is not sufficient for human guidance and finding the way; rather, faith and submission are necessary, otherwise Pharaoh also possessed knowledge. "عَلِمتَ"

3- The miracles of Moses were so clear that even Pharaoh knew about them. "عَلِمتَ"

4- Miracles are for the insight (baṣīrah) of the people, but there is no insight in sorcery and magic. "بصائر"

5- In corrupt governmental systems, to guide the people, one must approach the head of the government. "يا فرعون"

6- The Divine Prophets were certain of their path; therefore, they explicitly warned the tyrants. "لأظنّك يا فرعون مثبوراً"

7- Whoever understands the truth but insists on obstinacy and arrogance will be destroyed. "عَلِمتَ...مثبوراً"

Ayahs

فَأَرَادَ أَن يَسْتَفِزَّهُم مِّنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ فَأَغْرَقْنَـٰهُ وَمَن مَّعَهُۥ جَمِيعًا (۱٠۳)
He desired to exterminate them from the land, so We drowned him and all those who were with him. (103)
وَقُلْنَا مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ لِبَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ ٱسْكُنُوا۟ ٱلْأَرْضَ فَإِذَا جَآءَ وَعْدُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ جِئْنَا بِكُمْ لَفِيفًا (۱٠٤)
After him We said to the Children of Israel, ‘Take up residence in the land, and when the occasion of the other [promise] comes, We shall gather you in mixed company.’ (104)

Key Points

Istifzāz (اِستفزاز) means expulsion by force. The Quran repeatedly refers to this method used by the oppressors against the friends of Allah (Awliyā' Allāh) and how their efforts failed. They threw Abraham (عليه السلام) into the fire, but God made the fire cool. They threw Joseph (عليه السلام) into the well, but He made him the ruler (Azīz) of Egypt. They expelled the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) from Mecca, but Allah granted him the governance of the Islamic world. This is the Divine tradition and law: that in the face of the deceit of the people of falsehood, He has an appropriate plan. Lafīf (لَفیف) means interconnected and massive groups.

Messages

1- The logic of the tyrant (Tāghūt) is violence and exile, and they do not tolerate the men of truth. (فأراد)

2- Against the will of the tyrant, there is the Will and Wrath of Allah. (فأراد... فاَغرقناه)

3- Although the thought of sin is not a sin, a dangerous intention, such as the intention to exile Prophets, brings about Divine wrath. (فأراد... فاَغرقناه)

4- All the power displays of the tyrants are limited and confined to the earth. (فى الارض)

5- Divine punishment follows the completion of the proof (Ithmām al-Ḥujjah). After the miracles arrive and the people fail to believe, destruction is certain. (اَغرقناه)

6- The end result for the tyrants and their collaborators is destruction and annihilation. (اَغرقناه و مَن معه جمعیاً)

7- Aiding the oppressors causes one to share in their destruction. (مَن معه)

8- Corrupt leaders play a role in the destruction of the people. (اَغرقناه و مَن معه)

9- Those who rule over a region for a few days must be accountable to Allah on the Day of Judgment. (اُسكنوا الارض... جِئنا بكم لَفیفاً)

Ayah

وَبِٱلْحَقِّ أَنزَلْنَـٰهُ وَبِٱلْحَقِّ نَزَلَ ۗ وَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَـٰكَ إِلَّا مُبَشِّرًا وَنَذِيرًا (۱٠٥)
With the truth did We send it down, and with the truth did it descend, and We did not send you except as a bearer of good news and as a warner. (105)

Messages

1- The integrity of the Quran is guaranteed. Allah sent down the Quran in truth, and the divine revelation reached its destination safely, and the devils could not harm it. «بالحق انزلناه و بالحق نزل»

2- The core mission of the prophets is giving good tidings and warning, and they do not have the right to subtract from or add to the revelation. «بشیراً و نذیراً»

3- The Prophet is not responsible for the people's acceptance or rejection, and no one has the right to impose belief upon others. «ما ارسلناك الاّ مبشّرا و نذیرا»

4- Giving good tidings and warning are effective when they are together. «مبشّراً و نذیراً»

Ayah

وَقُرْءَانًا فَرَقْنَـٰهُ لِتَقْرَأَهُۥ عَلَى ٱلنَّاسِ عَلَىٰ مُكْثٍ وَنَزَّلْنَـٰهُ تَنزِيلًا (۱٠٦)
We have sent the Quran in [discrete] parts so that you may recite it for the people a little at a time, and We have sent it down piecemeal. (106)

Key Points

One of the objections raised by the disbelievers was why the Quran was not revealed all at once: «لولا نُزّل القرآن جُملةً واحدة»(224). This Ayah is a response to their statement. Since the verses of the Quran relate to various events and are meant to address the needs of all segments of society throughout history, it was revealed in parts so that it could correspond to different occurrences and be more appealing to those awaiting it. For example, if the verses concerning Jihad had been revealed during a time of peace, they would not have been in their proper context. Gradualness is one of the Divine traditions, whether in creation—where He created the universe in six days—or in the revelation of the Quran, even though He could have revealed it or created the universe all at once. The gradual revelation of the Quranic verses was intended so that knowledge of it would be accompanied by action upon it. Furthermore, the gradual revelation of the Quran also implies the continuous connection of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله with the source of revelation.

224) Furqan, 32.

Messages

1- The division of the Quran into parts is by the will of Allah. «فَرَقناه»

2- Teaching, training, and guidance must be gradual. «فرقناه، على مُكثٍ»

3- The gradual recitation of the Quran makes the listener thirsty and expectant for the subsequent verses. «على مُكثٍ» Muth (مُكث) means tranquility accompanied by assurance.

4- An educator and reformer must have a long-term plan and must not be oblivious to the times and new needs. «على مُكثٍ»

5- Although the great soul of the Prophet had the capacity to receive all the revelation at once, people have limited capacity and must grasp it gradually. «لتقرأه على النّاس على مُكثٍ»

6- In addition to its educational aspect, the Quran also has an executive aspect that must be implemented gradually. Implementing hundreds of commands all at once in a society plagued by hundreds of corruptions is impossible. «نزّلناه تنزیلا»

Ayah

قُلْ ءَامِنُوا۟ بِهِۦٓ أَوْ لَا تُؤْمِنُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْعِلْمَ مِن قَبْلِهِۦٓ إِذَا يُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ يَخِرُّونَ لِلْأَذْقَانِ سُجَّدًا (۱٠۷)
Say, ‘Whether you believe in it, or do not believe in it, indeed when it is recited to those who were given knowledge before it, they fall down in prostration on their faces, (107)

Key Points

«یَخرّون» (Yakhirrūna), derived from kharīr, means the sound of water flowing down from a height. It is also used for those who involuntarily fall into prostration for Allah while their voices are raised in glorification (Tasbīh). "Falling upon the chin" is a metaphor for ultimate humility and submission, where, in addition to the forehead, the entire face and chin are placed upon the earth.

Messages

1- The faith or disbelief of people does not affect the truthfulness of the Qur'an. «آمنوا به او لا تؤمنوا»

2- Knowledge is a Divine gift. «اوتوا العلم»

3- Contrary to those who consider ignorance to be the source of faith in God and religion, the Qur'an considers knowledge and awareness to be the root of faith. «اوتوا العلم... یخرّون»

4- A group of scholars from the People of the Book believed in the Qur'an, and the Qur'an had a profound effect on their souls. «اوتوا العلم من قبله... یَخرّون»

5- True knowledge is that which compels a person to humility before the truth. «اوتوا العلم، یَخرّون للاذقان سجّداً»

6- When a group shows obstinacy against the truth, one must recall the group that was obedient, so that the stubbornness of the opponents does not negatively affect our morale. «من قبله، یَخرّون للاذقان سجّداً»

7- The Qur'an is so exalted and rich in content that its recitation leads to the humility of scholars. «اذا یُتلى... یخرّون للاذقان سجّداً»

Ayahs

وَيَقُولُونَ سُبْحَـٰنَ رَبِّنَآ إِن كَانَ وَعْدُ رَبِّنَا لَمَفْعُولًا (۱٠۸)
and say, ‘‘Immaculate is our Lord! Indeed Our Lord’s promise is bound to be fulfilled.’’ (108)
وَيَخِرُّونَ لِلْأَذْقَانِ يَبْكُونَ وَيَزِيدُهُمْ خُشُوعًا ۩ (۱٠۹)
Weeping, they fall down on their faces, and it increases them in humility.’ (109)

Key Points

The repetition of the phrase "yakhirrūna" (they fall down) is either due to the repetition of their prostration, or to honor that perfection (of worship), or because in the first stage, their prostration was due to the knowledge of the verses of the Qur'an and the fulfillment of the promise in the Torah and the Gospel, and in the second stage, it was due to tears and a spirit of humility (khushū'). The coming of the Seal of the Prophets had been promised many times in the Torah and the Gospel. This verse indicates that some People of the Book, upon seeing the realization of the Divine promise, engaged in the glorification of Allah and humble prostration.

Messages

  1. Recounting the good words and deeds of the righteous and the believers is effective in educating others. "wa yaqūlūna..."

  2. The knowledgeable declare Allah free from every flaw. "ūtū al-'ilma... yaqūlūna subḥāna Rabbinā" (The belief in a deficiency in God arises from human ignorance.)

  3. The recitation of the Qur'an affects some people in several ways: action, speech, and heart. "sujjadan, yaqūlūna, yabkūna wa yazīduhum khushū'an"

  4. The recitation of the Qur'an constantly elevates man. "idhā yutlā, yazīduhum khushū'an"

  5. Knowledge (ma'rifah) and humility (khushū') have no limit. The more knowledge increases, the greater the humility. "yazīduhum khushū'an"

  6. Weeping during worship and prostration is valuable. "yakhirrūna lil-adhqāni yabkūna"

  7. The remembrance of Allah during prostration indicates the value of prostration. "yakhirrūna lil-adhqāni sujjadan, yakhirrūna lil-adhqāni yabkūna"

  8. Weeping is among the blessings and effects of knowledge. "ūtū al-'ilma, yabkūna"

  9. A tear is valuable when it is continuous, not momentary. "yabkūna"

Ayah

قُلِ ٱدْعُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ أَوِ ٱدْعُوا۟ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنَ ۖ أَيًّا مَّا تَدْعُوا۟ فَلَهُ ٱلْأَسْمَآءُ ٱلْحُسْنَىٰ ۚ وَلَا تَجْهَرْ بِصَلَاتِكَ وَلَا تُخَافِتْ بِهَا وَٱبْتَغِ بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ سَبِيلًا (۱۱٠)
Say, ‘Invoke ‘‘Allah’’ or invoke ‘‘the All-beneficent.’’ Whichever [of His Names] you may invoke, to Him belong the Best Names.’ Be neither loud in your prayer, nor murmur it, but follow a middle course between these, (110)

Key Points

It is mentioned in some exegeses (Tafasir) that the Prophet was supplicating in the Masjid al-Haram, saying: "Ya Allah, Ya Rahman." Some people imagined that He was calling upon two gods, one being "Allah" and the other "Rahman." The Ayah was revealed and removed this doubt. (225)

Some names are merely signs or indicators and have nothing to do with the correspondence of the meaning to the named entity, such as identifying individuals through voice, clothing, or car, or identifying a country or institution through a logo or emblem. However, sometimes names are not just emblems; rather, they carry content and a message, such as the blessed Names of Allah, each of which expresses a perfection of His Essence.

Whenever the Prophet prayed loudly, the disbelievers would disrupt Him by reciting poetry loudly, and whenever He prayed softly, the companions could not hear. The revelation of this Ayah commanded a recitation that was between loud and soft. (226)

It is mentioned in Shi'a and Sunni narrations that Allah has 99 Names, and whoever calls upon Allah using them in supplication will be successful and victorious. (227)

225) Tafsir al-Tibyan and Furqan. 226) Tafsir Abu al-Futuh Razi. 227) Tafsir al-Tabari, Al-Mizan, and Majma' al-Bayan.

Messages

1- The Prophet must remove doubts. «قل» (Say)

2- All the Names of Allah return to a single reality and are identical to His Essence. «ایّاًما تدعوا» (By whatever Name you call upon Him)

3- The Most Beautiful Names (al-Asma al-Husna) and the highest attributes belong exclusively to Allah. «فله الاسماء الحسنى» (To Him belong the Most Beautiful Names)

4- Since Allah and His perfections are infinite on the one hand, and human intellect and words are limited on the other, the best way is to call upon Allah by the Names derived from Him and the Quran. Otherwise, man might fall into deviation and invent inappropriate names for Allah. «الاسماء الحسنى» (The Most Beautiful Names)

5- In supplication, man is free to call upon Allah by whichever of His Names he wishes. «ایّاًما تدعوا...»

6- In all the Names of Allah, the best meanings and the most sacred concepts are employed. «فله الاسماء الحسنى»

7- Islam is a moderate and balanced religion; even the recitation of its prayer (Salat) must be at a moderate volume. «وابتغ بین ذلك سبیلاً» (And seek a way between that)

8- Islam pays attention not only to intention and action but also to the method and quality of the action. Prayer must be performed according to specific manners. «لاتجهر، لاتخافت، اِبتَغ بین ذلك» (Do not be loud, nor be silent, seek a way between that)

Ayah

وَقُلِ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِى لَمْ يَتَّخِذْ وَلَدًا وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُۥ شَرِيكٌ فِى ٱلْمُلْكِ وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُۥ وَلِىٌّ مِّنَ ٱلذُّلِّ ۖ وَكَبِّرْهُ تَكْبِيرًۢا (۱۱۱)
and say, ‘All praise belongs to Allah, who has neither taken any son, nor has He any partner in sovereignty, nor has He any wali out of weakness,’ and magnify Him with a magnification [worthy of Him]. (111)

Key Points

This Surah began with the glorification (Tasbih) of Allah and ended with His praise (Hamd) and magnification (Takbir), just as Surah Al-Mu'minun also began with the success of the believers, «قد افلح المؤمنون» (Successful indeed are the believers), and ended with the failure of the disbelievers. «لا یفلح الكافرون» (The disbelievers will not succeed). In narrations (Riwāyāt), the recitation of this Ayah is considered effective for paying off debt, removing temptation (waswasah), and alleviating poverty and illness. The negation of a son for Allah is in opposition to Judaism and Christianity, the negation of a partner is in opposition to the Quraysh, and the negation of a protector (walī) is in opposition to some Zoroastrians (Majūs) and star-worshippers. (228)

228) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān and Namūnah.

Messages

1- Let us learn the praise of Allah from Allah Himself. «قل الحمدللّه...»

2- The reason for dedicating all praises to Allah is His self-sufficiency. «الحمدللّه الّذى لم یتّخد ولدا»

3- Acknowledge the grandeur and greatness of Allah as unique, and know Him to be absolutely self-sufficient. «كبّره تكبیراً»
